llm_reviewer_comment_short: 画像中の異常検出・分割とは近いが、医療・健康用途が主題で製造欠陥検出とは対象がずれる。
llm_reviewer_comment_detail: 課題文脈は欠陥・異常検出の目的を比較的よく表している。解決手段文脈は処理手順やモデル構成を読み取れる。技術手段は具体的なモデル・アルゴリズム名を含む。関連要素はあるものの、製造欠陥検出の代表例としては弱い。
human_reviewer_comment: 空間文脈変分オートエンコーダによる画像異常検出だが、疾病画像を対象としており、製造欠陥検出への参考性は限定的である。

title: ABNORMALITY DETECTION METHOD, DEVICE, EQUIPMENT AND STORAGE MEDIUM FOR NEW ENERGY VEHICLE PCB BOARD; 新能源汽车PCB板的异常检测方法、装置、设备及存储介质
abstract:
本申请提供了一种新能源汽车PCB板的异常检测方法、装置、设备及存储介质，其方法包括：采集新能源汽车PCB板的PCB图像及至少一种补充图像；采用多模态融合网络对PCB图像和补充图像进行特征提取；对多模态图像特征进行特征重构处理，以得到重构后多模态图像特征；对重构后多模态图像特征进行注意力特征提取，得到新能源汽车PCB板的布线密度和组件集成度的PCB板图像特征；对PCB板图像特征与PCB板样例图像特征进行特征距离计算处理，得到新能源汽车PCB板特征距离图像；基于新能源汽车PCB板特征距离图像检测新能源汽车PCB板的异常区域并输出异常区域的位置信息；该方案可以整合新能源汽车PCB板的多模态图像数据，提高检测的准确性和效率。

The present application provides a method, device, equipment and storage medium for detecting abnormalities of a PCB board of a new energy vehicle. The method comprises: collecting a PCB image and at least one supplementary image of a PCB board of a new energy vehicle; extracting features of the PCB image and the supplementary image using a multimodal fusion network; performing feature reconstruction processing on multimodal image features to obtain reconstructed multimodal image features; performing attention feature extraction on the reconstructed multimodal image features to obtain PCB board image features of wiring density and component integration of the PCB board of the new energy vehicle; performing feature distance calculation processing on PCB board image features and PCB board sample image features to obtain a feature distance image of the PCB board of the new energy vehicle; detecting abnormal areas of the PCB board of the new energy vehicle based on the feature distance image of the PCB board of the new energy vehicle and outputting location information of the abnormal areas; the scheme can integrate multimodal image data of the PCB board of the new energy vehicle to improve the accuracy and efficiency of detection.
claims:
1 . 一种新能源汽车PCB板的异常检测方法，其特征在于，包括： 采集新能源汽车PCB板的PCB图像及至少一种补充图像，其中，所述PCB图像为可见光图像，所述补充图像为与所述PCB图像不同模态的图像，至少包括红外图像和/或X射线图像； 采用多模态融合网络对所述PCB图像和所述补充图像进行特征提取，得到所述新能源汽车PCB板的多模态图像特征； 对所述多模态图像特征进行特征重构处理，以得到重构后多模态图像特征； 对所述重构后多模态图像特征进行注意力特征提取，得到所述新能源汽车PCB板的布线密度和组件集成度的PCB板图像特征； 对所述PCB板图像特征与PCB板样例图像特征进行特征距离计算处理，得到新能源汽车 PCB板特征距离图像，所述PCB板样例图像为基于所述新能源汽车PCB板的设计参数的样本 PCB板的图像特征，所述设计参数为关注所述新能源汽车PCB板的标准布线密度和组件集成度的参数； 基于所述新能源汽车PCB板特征距离图像检测所述新能源汽车PCB板的异常区域并输出异常区域的位置信息。 2 .如权利要求1所述的异常检测方法，其特征在于，所述多模态融合网络包括:多个输入分支网络、特征融合层以及深度学习核心层，其中，每个所述输入分支对应一种模态的图像特征提取； 采用多模态融合网络对所述PCB图像和所述补充图像进行特征提取，得到所述新能源汽车PCB板的多模态图像特征，包括： 将每个种模态对应的图像输入分支网络进行特征提取，得到不同模态的图像特征； 采用特征融合层将不同模态对应的图像特征映射到一个共同的特征空间，并在共同的特征空间中将映射后的特征进行融合，得到融合后特征； 采用深度学习核心层对所述融合后的特征进行深层次特征提取，所述新能源汽车PCB 板的多模态图像特征。 3 .如权利要求1所述的异常检测方法，其特征在于，所述对所述多模态图像特征进行特征重构处理，以得到重构后多模态图像特征，包括： 对所述多模态图像特征进行降维处理； 根据所述新能源汽车PCB板的布线密度和组件集成度，使用深度卷积自编码器网络对降维后的多模态图像特征进行去除冗余信息并保留关键特征； 将压缩后的特征通过深度卷积自编码器网络的解码器部分进行上采样和重构,得到重构后多模态图像特征。 4 .如权利要求3所述的异常检测方法，其特征在于，将压缩后的特征通过深度卷积自编码器网络的解码器部分进行上采样和重构,得到重构后多模态图像特征，包括： 将压缩后的特征通过深度卷积自编码器网络的解码器部分进行上采样和重构，其中解码器部分包括多个解码层，每个解码层通过反卷积操作或转置卷积操作逐步恢复特征的空间分辨率，以增强新能源汽车PCB板的布线密度和组件集成度特征的表示，得到重构后多模态图像特征。 5 .如权利要求1-4任一项所述的异常检测方法，其特征在于，所述新能源汽车PCB板特征距离图像包括像素特征;所述基于所述新能源汽车PCB板特征距离图像检测所述新能源 汽车PCB板的异常区域并输出异常区域的位置信息，包括： 提取所述PCB板图像特征中的每个像素单元对应的像素特征及所述新能源汽车PCB板特征距离图像中每个像素单元对应的像素特征； 对所述PCB板图像特征中的每个像素单元对应的像素特征及所述新能源汽车PCB板特征距离图像中每个像素单元对应的像素特征进行处理，得到所述PCB图像中每个像素单元的异常概率信息； 基于所述PCB图像中每个像素单元的异常概率信息检测所述新能源汽车PCB板的异常区域并输出异常区域的位置信息。 6 .如权利要求5所述的异常检测方法，其特征在于，对所述PCB板图像特征中的每个像素单元对应的像素特征及所述新能源汽车PCB板特征距离图像中每个像素单元对应的像素特征进行处理，得到所述PCB图像中每个像素单元的异常概率信息，包括： 设置所述PCB板图像特征中的每个像素单元对应的像素特征为键，设置所述新能源汽车PCB板特征距离图像中每个像素单元对应的像素特征为值，对所述新能源汽车PCB板特征距离图像中像素单元对应的像素进行互注意力机制的特征处理，得到最终像素特征； 根据所述最终像素特征计算所述PCB板图像中每个像素单元的异常概率信息。 7 .如权利要求5所述的异常检测方法，其特征在于，所述新能源汽车PCB板特征距离图像包括所述PCB板图像中每个像素单元与PCB板样例图像中对应像素单元的特征距离；基于所述PCB图像中每个像素单元的异常概率信息检测所述新能源汽车PCB板的异常区域并输出异常区域的位置信息，包括： 基于所述PCB图像中每个像素单元的异常概率信息确定所述PCB图像中的候选异常像素单元； 获取表征像素单元异常的距离阈值； 将所述PCB板图像中每个候选异常像素单元与PCB板样例图像中对应像素单元的特征距离，与所述距离阈值进行比较，根据比较结果确定所述PCB板图像中每个候选异常像素单元的异常结果； 根据每个候选异常像素单元的异常结果在所述PCB板图像中确定异常区域，并输出异常区域的位置信息。 8 . 一种新能源汽车PCB板的异常检测装置，其特征在于，包括： 的设计参数的样本PCB板的图像特征，所述设计参数为关注所述新能源汽车PCB板的标准布线密度和组件集成度的参数； 检测模块，用于基于所述新能源汽车PCB板特征距离图像检测所述新能源汽车PCB板的异常区域并输出异常区域的位置信息。 9 .一种计算机设备，其特征在于，包括:存储器和处理器；其中，所述存储器用于存储程序； llm_reviewer_comment_detail: 課題文脈は欠陥・異常検出の目的を比較的よく表している。解決手段文脈は処理手順やモデル構成を読み取れる。技術手段は具体的なモデル・アルゴリズム名を含む。製造欠陥検出・画像検査への対応が明確で、代表特許候補として説明しやすい。
human_reviewer_comment: 新能源汽车PCB板の異常検出を対象とし、多モーダル融合、特徴再構成、Attentionを用いているため、製造欠陥検出の代表例として強い。

title: VISUAL INSPECTION METHOD, DEVICE, SYSTEM AND STORAGE DEVICE; 视觉检测方法、设备、系统以及存储装置
abstract:
本申请公开了一种视觉检测方法、设备、系统以及存储装置，其中，视觉检测方法包括：获取目标对象在预设场所中的人体位姿信息，并获取目标对象的瞳孔偏移信息和面部俯仰信息；利用人体位姿信息、瞳孔偏移信息和面部俯仰信息，得到目标对象在预设场所中的视觉焦点；其中，预设场所安装有第一摄像头和第二摄像头，人体位姿信息是利用第一摄像头检测得到的，瞳孔偏移信息和面部俯仰信息是利用第二摄像头检测得到的。上述方案，能够提高视觉检测的准确性。

The present application discloses a visual detection method, device, system and storage device, wherein the visual detection method includes: obtaining the human body posture information of the target object in a preset place, and obtaining the pupil displacement information and facial pitch information of the target object; using the human body posture information, pupil displacement information and facial pitch information to obtain the visual focus of the target object in the preset place; wherein the preset place is installed with a first camera and a second camera, the human body posture information is obtained by detecting the first camera, and the pupil displacement information and facial pitch information are obtained by detecting the second camera. The above scheme can improve the accuracy of visual detection.
claims:
1 . 一种视觉检测方法，其特征在于，包括： 获取目标对象在预设场所中的人体位姿信息;其中，所述人体位姿信息至少包括人体位置信息、人体身高信息和人体面向信息，所述预设场所安装有第一摄像头和第二摄像头， 所述人体位姿信息利用所述第一摄像头检测得到； 利用所述人体位置信息，筛选在所述目标对象预设范围之内的第二摄像头，并发送所述目标对象的人体位姿信息至筛选到的所述第二摄像头，以根据所述人体身高信息调整垂直方向上的第一角度，以及根据所述人体面向信息调整水平方向上的第二角度； 利用所述人体面向信息，在筛选到的所述第二摄像头中，选择所述目标对象正面面向的第二摄像头，以及选择所述目标对象侧面面向的第二摄像头； 利用所述目标对象正面面向的第二摄像头，获取所述目标对象的瞳孔偏移信息，并利用所述目标对象侧面面向的第二摄像头，获取所述目标对象的面部俯仰信息； 利用所述人体位姿信息、所述瞳孔偏移信息和所述面部俯仰信息，得到所述目标对象在所述预设场所中的视觉焦点。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其特征在于，所述瞳孔偏移信息包括所述目标对象瞳孔的实际位置与参考位置之间的偏移距离，所述参考位置表示所述目标对象平视状态下的瞳孔位置;所述利用所述人体位姿信息、所述瞳孔偏移信息和所述面部俯仰信息，得到所述目标对象在所述预设场所中的视觉焦点，包括： 利用所述偏移距离，得到所述目标对象的视线偏转角度； 利用所述人体位姿信息、所述面部俯仰信息和所述视线偏转角度，得到所述视觉焦点。 3 .根据权利要求2所述的方法，其特征在于，所述视线偏转角度是利用预设映射关系对所述偏移距离进行转换得到的，所述预设映射关系的获取步骤包括： 获取若干组样本采集数据；其中，每组所述样本采集数据包括:样本对象平视状态下视线在预设参考面上的第一坐标，瞳孔偏移样本距离情况下所述样本对象的视线在所述预设参考面上的第二坐标，以及所述样本对象至所述预设参考面之间的第一距离值； 针对每组所述样本采集数据，利用所述第一坐标、所述第二坐标和所述第一距离值，得到瞳孔偏移所述样本距离情况下的样本偏转角度，并将所述样本距离和所述样本偏转角度，作为一组待处理数据； 将若干组所述待处理数据进行拟合，得到所述预设映射关系。 4 .根据权利要求2所述的方法，其特征在于，所述利用所述人体位姿信息、所述面部俯仰信息和所述视线偏转角度，得到所述视觉焦点，包括： 利用所述人体位姿信息和所述面部俯仰信息，得到所述目标对象在瞳孔无偏移情况下的理论视线； 按照所述视线偏转角度，调整所述理论视线，得到所述目标对象的实际视线； 将所述实际视线与所述预设场所的交点，作为所述目标对象的视觉焦点。 5 .根据权利要求2所述的方法，其特征在于，所述实际位置的获取步骤包括： 获取所述目标对象的眼部图像;其中，所述眼部图像是由所述目标对象正面面向的第二摄像头拍摄得到的； 对所述眼部图像进行边缘提取，得到虹膜的至少部分边缘； 利用所述至少部分边缘，确定瞳孔的实际位置。 6 .根据权利要求2所述的方法，其特征在于，所述参考位置的获取步骤包括以下任一者： 利用若干个样本对象在平视状态下的瞳孔位置，得到所述参考位置； 在所述目标对象进入所述预设场所之前，获取所述目标对象在平视状态下的瞳孔位置，作为所述参考位置。 7 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其特征在于，所述面部俯仰信息包括所述目标对象的面部俯仰角，所述获取所述目标对象的面部俯仰信息，包括： 获取所述目标对象的侧面图像；其中，所述侧面图像是由所述目标对象侧面面向的第二摄像头拍摄得到的； 对所述侧面图像进行边缘提取，得到所述目标对象的侧脸边缘； 利用所述侧脸边缘，确定所述目标对象的面部俯仰角。 8 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其特征在于，所述预设场所放置有若干物品，在所述利用所述人体位姿信息、所述瞳孔偏移信息和所述面部俯仰信息，得到所述目标对象在所述预设场所中的视觉焦点之后，所述方法还包括： 利用所述视觉焦点与各个所述物品之间的第二距离值，得到所述目标对象对所述物品的关注度;其中，所述第二距离值越大，所述关注度越低； 基于所述目标对象对所述物品的关注度，得到包含所述若干物品的热点图。 9 .一种视觉检测设备，其特征在于，包括相互耦接的存储器和处理器，所述存储器中存储有程序指令，所述处理器用于执行所述程序指令以实现权利要求1至8任一项所述的视觉检测方法。 10 .一种视觉检测系统，其特征在于，包括:如权利要求9所述的视觉检测设备，以及安装于预设场所的第一摄像头和第二摄像头，所述第一摄像头和所述第二摄像头与所述视觉检测设备通信连接。 11 .根据权利要求10所述的系统，其特征在于，所述第一摄像头安装于所述预设场所高于所述目标对象的位置处，且所述第一摄像头的视角范围覆盖所述预设场所； 和/或，所述第二摄像头的视角范围覆盖所述目标对象的面部； 和/或，所述第二摄像头有多个，且多个所述第二摄像头以预设间距环绕所述预设场所设置。 12 . 一种存储装置，其特征在于，存储有能够被处理器运行的程序指令，所述程序指令用于实现权利要求1至8任一项所述的视觉检测方法。

1 . A visual detection method, characterized in that it includes: obtaining human body posture information of a target object in a preset place; wherein the human body posture information at least includes human body position information, human body height information and human body orientation information, and the preset place is installed with a first camera and a second camera, and the human body posture information is detected by the first camera; using the human body position information, screening a second camera within a preset range of the target object, and sending the human body posture information of the target object to the screened second camera, so as to adjust a first angle in a vertical direction according to the human body height information, and adjust a second angle in a horizontal direction according to the human body orientation information; using the human body orientation information, selecting a second camera facing the front of the target object and selecting a second camera facing the side of the target object from the screened second cameras; using the second camera facing the front of the target object, obtaining pupil displacement information of the target object, and using the second camera facing the side of the target object, obtaining facial pitch information of the target object; using the human body posture information, the pupil displacement information and the facial pitch information, obtaining the visual focus of the target object in the preset place. 2 .The method according to claim 1 is characterized in that the pupil offset information includes an offset distance between an actual position of the pupil of the target object and a reference position, and the reference position represents the pupil position of the target object in a level state; the method of obtaining the visual focus of the target object in the preset place by using the human body posture information, the pupil offset information and the facial pitch information comprises: obtaining the sight line deflection angle of the target object by using the offset distance; obtaining the visual focus by using the human body posture information, the facial pitch information and the sight line deflection angle. 3 .The method according to claim 2 is characterized in that the line of sight deflection angle is obtained by converting the offset distance using a preset mapping relationship, and the step of obtaining the preset mapping relationship includes: obtaining several groups of sample acquisition data; wherein each group of the sample acquisition data includes: a first coordinate of the line of sight of the sample object on a preset reference plane in a level state, a second coordinate of the line of sight of the sample object on the preset reference plane when the pupil is offset by the sample distance, and a first distance value between the sample object and the preset reference plane; for each group of the sample acquisition data, using the first coordinate, the second coordinate and the first distance value, obtain the sample deflection angle when the pupil is offset by the sample distance, and use the sample distance and the sample deflection angle as a group of data to be processed; fitting several groups of the data to be processed to obtain the preset mapping relationship. 4 .The method according to claim 2 is characterized in that the use of the human body posture information, the facial pitch information and the line of sight deflection angle to obtain the visual focus includes: using the human body posture information and the facial pitch information to obtain the theoretical line of sight of the target object when there is no pupil displacement; adjusting the theoretical line of sight according to the line of sight deflection angle to obtain the actual line of sight of the target object; and using the intersection of the actual line of sight and the preset place as the visual focus of the target object. 5 .The method according to claim 2 is characterized in that the step of obtaining the actual position includes: obtaining an eye image of the target object; wherein the eye image is taken by a second camera with the target object facing the front; performing edge extraction on the eye image to obtain at least a partial edge of the iris; and determining the actual position of the pupil using the at least partial edge. 6 .The method according to claim 2 is characterized in that the step of obtaining the reference position includes any one of the following: obtaining the reference position by using the pupil positions of several sample objects in a level view state; obtaining the pupil position of the target object in a level view state before the target object enters the preset place as the reference position. 7 .The method according to claim 1 is characterized in that the facial pitch information includes the facial pitch angle of the target object, and obtaining the facial pitch information of the target object includes: obtaining a side image of the target object; wherein the side image is taken by a second camera with the target object facing sideways; performing edge extraction on the side image to obtain the side face edge of the target object; and determining the facial pitch angle of the target object using the side face edge. 8 .The method according to claim 1 is characterized in that a number of objects are placed in the preset place, and after obtaining the visual focus of the target object in the preset place by using the human body posture information, the pupil offset information and the facial pitch information, the method further includes: obtaining the target object's attention to the object by using a second distance value between the visual focus and each of the objects; wherein the larger the second distance value, the lower the attention; and obtaining a heat map containing the several objects based on the target object's attention to the objects. 9 .A visual inspection device, characterized in that it includes a memory and a processor coupled to each other, wherein the memory stores program instructions, and the processor is used to execute the program instructions to implement the visual inspection method described in any one of claims 1 to 8. 10 .A visual inspection system, characterized in that it comprises: a visual inspection device as described in claim 9, and a first camera and a second camera installed in a preset location, wherein the first camera and the second camera are communicatively connected to the visual inspection device. 11 .The system according to claim 10 is characterized in that the first camera is installed at a position higher than the target object in the preset place, and the viewing angle of the first camera covers the preset place; and/or the viewing angle of the second camera covers the face of the target object; and/or there are multiple second cameras, and the multiple second cameras are arranged around the preset place at a preset interval. 12 . A storage device, characterized in that it stores program instructions that can be executed by a processor, and the program instructions are used to implement the visual inspection method described in any one of claims 1 to 8.

title: NON-RIGID REGISTRATION METHOD, DEVICE AND ELECTRONIC EQUIPMENT; 非刚性配准方法、装置及电子设备
abstract:
本申请提供一种非刚性配准方法、装置及电子设备，该方法包括：依据非刚性待配准物体的场景数据以及模型数据，对所述待配准物体进行全局匹配，得到所述待配准物体的场景数据与所述模型数据之间的初始位姿变换关系；对于对所述待配准物体的场景数据进行有限元划分得到的模块中的任一有效模块，确定该有效模块对应的第一类型误差和第二类型误差；依据对所述待配准物体的场景数据进行有限元划分得到各有效模块的第一类型误差和第二类型误差，对各有效模块对应的位姿变换关系进行优化，确定所述待配准物体的配准结果。该方法可以提高非刚性物体匹配的准确性。

The present application provides a non-rigid registration method, apparatus, and electronic device. The method comprises: performing global matching on the non-rigid object to be registered based on scene data and model data of the object to be registered, obtaining an initial pose transformation relationship between the scene data and the model data of the object to be registered; determining a first type error and a second type error corresponding to any valid module among the modules obtained by performing finite element partitioning on the scene data of the object to be registered; optimizing the pose transformation relationship corresponding to each valid module based on the first type error and second type error obtained by performing finite element partitioning on the scene data of the object to be registered, and determining a registration result for the object to be registered. This method can improve the accuracy of non-rigid object matching.
claims:
1 . 一种非刚性配准方法，其特征在于，包括： 依据非刚性待配准物体的场景数据以及模型数据,对所述待配准物体进行全局匹配, 得到所述待配准物体的场景数据与所述模型数据之间的初始位姿变换关系； 对于对所述待配准物体的场景数据进行有限元划分得到的模块中的任一有效模块，确定该有效模块对应的第一类型误差和第二类型误差；其中，有效模块为包括待配准物体的点云的模块，第一类型损失用于表征该有效模块中的点与模型数据中对应的点之间的配准约束，有效模块中的点在模型数据中对应的点依据有效模块对应的位姿变换关系确定，有效模块对应的位姿变换关系的初始值为所述初始位姿变换关系，第二类型损失用于表征该有效模块与邻域有效模块之间的相对位姿约束； 依据对所述待配准物体的场景数据进行有限元划分得到各有效模块的第一类型误差和第二类型误差，对各有效模块对应的位姿变换关系进行优化，确定所述待配准物体的配准结果。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其特征在于，所述依据对所述待配准物体的场景数据进行有限元划分得到各有效模块的第一类型误差和第二类型误差，对各有效模块对应的位姿变换关系进行优化，包括： 以最小化各有效模块的第一类型误差和第二类型误差的融合值为优化目标，对各有效模块对应的位姿变换关系进行优化。 3 .根据权利要求2所述的方法，其特征在于，所述依据对所述待配准物体的场景数据进行有限元划分得到各有效模块的第一类型误差和第二类型误差，对各有效模块对应的位姿变换关系进行优化，确定所述待配准物体的配准结果，包括： title: DIABETIC FOOT MONITORING METHOD AND COMPUTER READABLE STORAGE MEDIUM; MÉTODO DE MONITORAMENTO DE PÉ DIABÉTICO E MEIO DE ARMAZENAMENTO LEGÍVEL POR COMPUTADOR
claims:
1/2 REIVINDICAÇÕES 1. Método de monitoramento de pé diabético CARACTERIZADO por compreender as etapas de: a) exibir na tela de um dispositivo móvel os campos para a identificação do usuário e histórico médico; b) capturar por meio de uma câmera do dispositivo móvel fotografias dos pés para inspeção visual; c) comparar as fotografias dos pés do usuário com fotos anteriores e/ou bancos de dados; (c.1) caso haja alguma alteração, um alerta é enviado ao usuário. d) ativar função de vibração do dispositivo móvel para teste de sensibilidade; e) receber resposta do usuário; (e.1) caso haja alteração em relação a testes anteriores, um alerta é enviado ao usuário; f) armazenar dados no perfil do usuário para acompanhamento médico. 2. Método, de acordo com a reivindicação 1, CARACTERIZADO pelo fato de que na etapa a), os dados inseridos pelo usuário são coletados e armazenados no banco de dados. 3. Método, de acordo com a reivindicação 1, CARACTERIZADO pelo fato de que na etapa b), são fornecidas instruções para fotografar seis regiões específicas do pé: Pé Esquerdo Vista Frontal; Pé Esquerdo — Vista Lateral; Pé Direito Vista Frontal; Pé Direito - Vista Lateral; Pés Juntos - Vista da Ponta dos Dedos; Pés Juntos - Vista Inferior. 4. Método, de acordo com a reivindicação 1 ou 3, CARACTERIZADO pelo fato de que as imagens capturadas na etapa b) são armazenadas no banco de dados na nuvem, associadas ao Petição 870240060140, de 17/07/2024, pág. 27/35 2/2 perfil do usuário e marcadas com data e hora. 5. Método, de acordo com a reivindicação 1, CARACTERIZADO pelo fato de que na etapa c) , a inteligência artificial, através de uma rede neural convolucional, analisa as fotografias, avaliando aspectos como textura da pele, coloração, presença de feridas ou anormalidades. 6. Método, de acordo com a reivindicação 1, CARACTERIZADO pelo fato de que na etapa d), o dispositivo móvel é configurado para emitir vibrações que replicam a funcionalidade de um diapasão médico de 128 Hz; em que o usuário posiciona o dispositivo móvel de modo que ele faça contato direto com a superfície do pé, especificamente sobre o lado dorsal da falange distal do hálux, ou sobre outro dedo caso o hálux não esteja disponível. 7. Método, de acordo com a reivindicação 1 ou 6, CARACTERI ZADO pelo fato de que a aplicação da vibração é feita perpendicularmente e com pressão constante. 8. Método, de acordo com qualquer uma das reivindicações 1, 6 ou 7, CARACTERIZADO pelo fato de que realizam-se pelo menos 3 testes vibratórios em cada pé; em que após pelo menos 3 segundos, surge uma pergunta na tela, questionando o que o usuário está sendo sentido. 9. Método, de acordo com a reivindicação 1, CARACTERIZADO pelo fato de que na etapa e), é exibido na tela o resultado do teste indicando se a sensibilidade vibratória está normal ou não, fornecendo orientações sobre a ação a ser tomada. 10. Meio de armazenamento legível por computador CARACTERIZADO por executar o método conforme definido em qualquer uma das reivindicações de 1 a 9. Petição 870240060140, de 17/07/2024, pág. 28/35

title: A VISUAL DETECTION METHOD FOR SILICONE TOY INJECTION MOLDING OVERFLOW; 一种硅胶玩具注塑溢胶视觉检测方法
abstract:
本发明涉及图像处理技术领域，具体涉及一种硅胶玩具注塑溢胶视觉检测方法。该方法包括：获取硅胶玩具的灰度图像中的闭合边缘线；基于闭合边缘线上各边缘像素点对应的特征线段上像素点的灰度差异得到突变程度；根据各边缘像素点的邻域内边缘像素点的位置信息，得到对应的方向指标和距离指标，结合突变程度获得各边缘像素点对应的目标矩阵，进而得到各边缘像素点的灰度优势；结合灰度图像中边缘像素点的灰度优势与标准图像中边缘像素点的灰度优势的相似度、灰度图像中闭合边缘线对应的边界特征向量与标准图像中闭合边缘线对应的边界特征向量的相似度，判断硅胶玩具是否存在溢胶现象。本发明提高了溢胶现象检测结果的准确度。

The invention relates to the technical field of image processing, and in particular to a visual detection method for silicone toy injection molding overflow. The method includes: obtaining the closed edge line in the grayscale image of the silicone toy; obtaining the mutation degree based on the grayscale difference of the pixel points on the characteristic line segment corresponding to each edge pixel point on the closed edge line; and obtaining the mutation degree based on the edge within the neighborhood of each edge pixel point. Based on the position information of the pixel, the corresponding direction index and distance index are obtained, and the target matrix corresponding to each edge pixel is obtained by combining the degree of mutation, and then the grayscale advantage of each edge pixel is obtained; combined with the grayscale of the edge pixels in the grayscale image The similarity between the advantage and the gray-scale advantage of the edge pixels in the standard image, the similarity between the boundary feature vector corresponding to the closed edge line in the gray-scale image and the boundary feature vector corresponding to the closed edge line in the standard image, determine whether there is overflow in the silicone toy glue phenomenon. The invention improves the accuracy of detection results of glue overflow phenomenon.

title: SUPER-RESOLUTION RECONSTRUCTION NETWORK MODEL TRAINING METHOD AND SCANNED IMAGE PROCESSING METHOD; 超分辨率重建网络模型的训练方法和扫描图像处理方法
abstract:
本发明公开了一种超分辨率重建网络模型的训练方法和扫描图像处理方法，训练方法包括：获取低分辨率训练图像和与对应的高分辨率训练图像；将低分辨率训练图像输入至待训练的超分辨率重建网络模型；通过超分辨率重建网络模型对低分辨率训练图像进行图像重建处理以获取与低分辨率训练图像对应的超分辨率训练图像；计算超分辨率及高分辨率训练图像之间的第一损失；从超分辨率训练图像中识别出注意力区域并从高分辨率训练图像中识别出同样的注意力区域，计算识别出注意力区域的超分辨率及高分辨率训练图像之间的第二损失；根据第一及第二损失训练超分辨率重建网络模型。本发明可以灵活选择图像细节恢复，明显提升了对图像关注区域的还原效果。

The present invention discloses a training method for a super-resolution reconstruction network model and a scanned image processing method. The training method includes: obtaining a low-resolution training image and a corresponding high-resolution training image; inputting the low-resolution training image into the super-resolution reconstruction network model to be trained; performing image reconstruction processing on the low-resolution training image using the super-resolution reconstruction network model to obtain a super-resolution training image corresponding to the low-resolution training image; calculating a first loss between the super-resolution and high-resolution training images; identifying a focus region from the super-resolution training image and the same focus region from the high-resolution training image, calculating a second loss between the super-resolution and high-resolution training images of the focus region; and training the super-resolution reconstruction network model based on the first and second losses. The present invention can flexibly select image detail restoration, significantly improving the restoration effect of the image focus region.

1 .A training method for a super-resolution reconstruction network model, characterized in that it includes: obtaining a low-resolution training image and a high-resolution training image corresponding to the low-resolution training image, wherein the pixel density of the high-resolution training image is higher than the pixel density of the corresponding low-resolution training image; wherein the image is a medical image; inputting the low-resolution training image into the super-resolution reconstruction network model to be trained; performing image reconstruction processing on the low-resolution training image through the super-resolution reconstruction network model to obtain a super-resolution training image corresponding to the low-resolution training image, wherein the pixel density of the super-resolution training image is higher than or equal to the pixel density of the corresponding high-resolution training image; calculating a first loss between the super-resolution training image and the high-resolution training image; performing tissue segmentation on the super-resolution training image and the high-resolution training image respectively using a tissue structure diagram to obtain a tissue segmentation map of the super-resolution training image and a tissue segmentation map of the high-resolution training image; An attention region is identified from the tissue segmentation map of the super-resolution training image and the same attention region is identified from the tissue segmentation map of the high-resolution training image. A corresponding loss calculation weight is assigned to each pixel of the training image according to the distance between the pixel of the training image and the identified attention region, wherein the distance between the pixel and the attention region and the loss calculation weight assigned to the pixel are in a relative relationship; a second loss between the super-resolution training image and the high-resolution training image is calculated using the loss calculation weight corresponding to each pixel; a third loss between the tissue segmentation map of the super-resolution training image and the tissue segmentation map of the high-resolution training image is calculated; and the super-resolution reconstruction network model is trained according to the first loss, the second loss and the third loss. 2 .The training method as described in claim 1 is characterized in that after the step of obtaining a low-resolution training image and a high-resolution training image corresponding to the low-resolution training image, the training method further includes: aligning the low-resolution training image and the high-resolution training image to maintain image consistency. 3 The training method according to any one of claims 1 to 2, wherein the super-resolution reconstruction network model includes a plurality of residual blocks; each residual block includes at least two first convolutional layers, an activation function, two second convolutional layers, and a constant scaling layer; wherein the first convolutional layer includes a convolutional layer of size 3 x 1 x 1, and the second convolutional layer includes a convolutional layer of size 1 x 3 x 3. 4 .The training method as described in claim 1 is characterized in that the super-resolution reconstruction network model includes any one or more of a dynamic neural network model, a convolutional neural network model, a fully convolutional network model, a deep neural network model, a generative adversarial network model, a recurrent neural network model, a deep residual network model and a long short-term memory network model. 5 .A scanned image processing method, characterized in that it includes: acquiring a scanned image; inputting the scanned image into a super-resolution reconstruction network model trained by the super-resolution reconstruction network model training method as described in any one of claims 1 to 4; performing image reconstruction processing on the scanned image through the super-resolution reconstruction network model; and outputting the super-resolution reconstructed image after the image reconstruction processing. 6 The scanning image processing method according to claim 5, characterized in that after the step of outputting the super-resolution reconstructed image, the scanning image processing method further comprises: reconstructing an image for the attention area from the super-resolution reconstructed image, Anomaly detection is performed on the image of the domain to screen out anomalies in the attention area. 7 .An electronic device comprising a memory, a processor, and a computer program stored in the memory and executable on the processor, wherein when the processor executes the computer program, the processor implements the training method for a super-resolution reconstruction network model as described in any one of claims 1 to 4 or the scanning image processing method as described in any one of claims 5 to 6. 8 .A computer-readable medium having computer instructions stored thereon, characterized in that when the computer instructions are executed by a processor, they implement the training method of the super-resolution reconstruction network model as described in any one of claims 1 to 4 or the scanning image processing method as described in any one of claims 5 to 6.

title: HIGHLY INTELLIGENT INSPECTION SYSTEM FOR SHIP WALL INSPECTION; 船舶の壁面検査用高度知能化検査システム
claims:
1. 船舶の壁面を撮像し、前記壁面の撮像画像データを利用して、前記壁面の状態を良否判定する船舶の壁面検査用高度知能化検査システムであって、撮像による壁面の撮像画像データから、画像処理により、時間帯、季節、天候などの変化に基づく光量変動に対して不変な情報を作成する前処理手段と、前記前処理手段に先立って、予め撮像して取得した複数の 撮像画像データ から、画像処理により、それぞれ時間帯、季節、天候などの変化に基づく光量変動に対して不変な複数の基準データを記憶させ学習させる学習手段と、前記前処理手段にて作成された前記不変な情報を、前記複数の基準データに基づいて、前記壁面の状態を良否判定する状態判定手段とを備え、前記前処理手段は、前記撮像による濃淡ムラに基づく光量変動に対して濃淡正規化による前処理を実施するものであり、前記不変な情報は、濃淡ムラに不変な情報であって、前記壁面である判定対象の学習時と判定時の色合いに依存しない白黒濃淡情報であることを特徴とする船舶の壁面検査用高度知能化検査システム。 2. 前記学習手段が、前記複数の基準データを、ニューラルネットワークを用いて学習させるものであり、前記状態判定手段が、前記不変な情報を前記複数の基準データに基づいて、ニューラルネットワークを用いて良否判定する手段である、請求項1に記載の船舶の壁面検査用高度知能化検査システム。 3. 前記壁面の状態について良否判定のために予め記憶されている基準データは、前記壁面の形状及び性状の違いに応じて異なる複数種類が作成されており、前記複数種類の基準データは、前記撮像する壁面に基づき、作業者の指示で使い分けられる、請求項1または2記載の船舶の壁面検査用高度知能化検査システム。 4. 前記船舶の壁面の撮像は、カメラを搭載したマルチコプターを送信機にて操縦して飛行させて行うものであり、前記前処理手段は、前記マルチコプターによる前記壁面の空撮時における上下左右などの傾き変動に対し、 撮像画像データ から前記傾き変動に不変な情報を作成するものである、請求項1乃至3のいずれか1項に記載の船舶の壁面検査用高度知能化検査システム。 5. 前記撮像画像データ を検査用パソコンに取り込み、良否判定するオフライン検査モードを有する、請求項4又は5記載の船舶の壁面検査用高度知能化検査システム。 7. 前記船舶の壁面の撮像は、カメラを用いて作業者が行い、 撮像画像データ はモバイルメモリに保存されるものであり、前記状態判定手段は、撮像完了後、前記モバイルメモリに保存した 前記撮像画像データ を検査用パソコンに取り込み、良否判定するオフライン検査モードを有する、請求項1乃至3のいずれか1項に記載の船舶の壁面検査用高度知能化検査システム。 8. 前記状態判定手段は、前記撮像画像データを上下左右に複数に分割し、この分割された個々の画像を判定の単位画像として良否判定を行うものである、 請 求項6又は7記載の船舶の壁面検査用高度知能化検査システム。 9. 前記前処理手段は、カラー画像を色相均等加算平均により白黒濃淡画像する手段と、フレーム画像内の画素内の画素内の最小値Pを探索し、全画素から最小値Pを差し引き、画像を前記最小値Pからの差分画像とするオフセットカット処理手段と、光量変動に対する濃淡正規化手段と、を有し、前記最小値Pは、濃度ヒストグラムにより最頻画像の濃度値つまり平均値を基準画像濃度とする、請求項1記載の船舶の壁面検査用高度知能化検査システム。 13. 前記濃淡正規化手段は、下記式1の濃淡ムラ正規化演算式により算出する手段を有する、請求項12記載の船舶の壁面検査用高度知能化検査システム。 (式1)

title: A METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR DETECTING ABNORMAL DATA IN REGIONAL HEALTH BIG DATA; 一种用于区域健康大数据的异常数据检测方法及系统
abstract:
本发明涉及异常数据检测技术领域，公开一种用于区域健康大数据的异常数据检测方法及系统，其中方法包括：基于数据集获取各个数据点的混合k近邻，并计算每个数据点的海拔；基于数据点及其k近邻海拔，利用绝对中位差获取绝对中位差变异性因子；基于数据点及其k近邻海拔，利用于k近邻的重叠高斯分布获取两阶段高斯变异性因子；基于混合k近邻和局部角度方差获取角度变异性因子；基于绝对中位差变异性因子和两阶段高斯变异性因子和角度变异性因子，获取检测异常数据的异常因子，根据异常因子和阈值获取异常数据点集。本发明克服了传统方法缺乏对多异常因素的考虑缺失的不足和局限性，显著提高了异常数据的检测方法的效率和质量。

The present invention relates to the field of abnormal data detection technology, and discloses an abnormal data detection method and system for regional health big data, wherein the method comprises: obtaining mixed k-nearest neighbors of each data point based on a data set, and calculating the altitude of each data point; obtaining an absolute median difference variability factor based on the data point and its k-nearest neighbor altitude using the absolute median difference; obtaining a two-stage Gaussian variability factor based on the data point and its k-nearest neighbor altitude using the overlapping Gaussian distribution of the k-nearest neighbor; obtaining an angle variability factor based on mixed k-nearest neighbors and local angle variance; obtaining an abnormal factor for detecting abnormal data based on the absolute median difference variability factor and the two-stage Gaussian variability factor and the angle variability factor, and obtaining an abnormal data point set according to the abnormal factor and a threshold. The present invention overcomes the shortcomings and limitations of traditional methods that lack consideration of multiple abnormal factors, and significantly improves the efficiency and quality of abnormal data detection methods.

title: FAST AND LOW MEMORY FOOTPRINT CONVOLUTIONAL NEURAL NETWORK FOR TOMOGRAPHIC FUSION DATA PROCESSING AND FEATURE DETECTION; 用于断层融合数据处理和特征检测的快速和低存储器占用卷积神经网络
abstract:
本发明提供了一种卷积神经网络(CNN)(202)，在用于分析由X射线成像系统(100)提供的图像(208)的自动特征和/或异常检测系统(204)中采用该卷积神经网络。自动检测系统(204)以减少所需的全分辨率CNN卷积层(212)的数量的方式操作，以便加快检测系统(204)的网络推断和学习过程。为此，检测系统(204)利用断层摄影数据(206)的更紧凑表示作为输入，以缓解现有技术X射线系统中的CNN存储器占用空间和计算时间问题。

The present invention provides a convolutional neural network (CNN) (202) for use in an automated feature and/or anomaly detection system (204) for analyzing images (208) provided by an x-ray imaging system (100) Convolutional neural network. The automated detection system (204) operates in a manner that reduces the number of required full resolution CNN convolutional layers (212) in order to speed up the detection system's (204) network inference and learning process. To this end, the detection system (204) utilizes a more compact representation of the tomographic data (206) as input to alleviate CNN memory footprint and computation time issues in prior art X-ray systems.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR GENERATING 3D INFORMATION FROM 2D IMAGE, AND METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR RECOGNIZING STRUCTURE DEFECT USING THE 3D SHAPE; 2차원 영상으로부터 3차원 정보를 생성하는 방법 및 그 장치와, 이를 이용한 구조물 결함 검출 방법 및 그 장치
abstract:
2차원 영상으로부터 3차원 정보를 생성하는 방법 및 그 장치와, 이를 이용한 구조물 결함 검출 방법 및 그 장치가 개시된다. 3차원정보생성장치는 영상에 대해 히스토그램 쉬프팅을 수행하여 경계 영역의 대비(contrast)를 향상시킨 화이트닝 이미지를 생성하고, 화이트닝 이미지에 경계 검출 알고리즘을 적용하여 영상에 존재하는 경계를 검출하여 경계선 이미지를 생성하고, 다양한 방향에서 촬영한 복수의 영상에 대하여 각각 생성한 복수의 경계선 이미지로부터 복수의 특징점을 추출하고, 서로 다른 경계선 이미지에서 각각 추출된 서로 다른 시점의 3개 이상의 특징점들을 매칭하여 영상에 포함된 객체에 대한 3차원 포인트 클라우드를 생성한다.

Disclosed are a method and apparatus for generating 3D information from a 2D image, and a structure defect detection method and apparatus using the same. The 3D information generator performs histogram shifting on the image to generate a whitening image with improved contrast in the border area, and detects the border existing in the image by applying a border detection algorithm to the whitening image to obtain a border image. and extracts a plurality of feature points from a plurality of boundary images respectively generated for a plurality of images taken in various directions, matches three or more feature points from different viewpoints extracted from different boundary images, and includes them in the image. Creates a 3D point cloud for a given object.

Generating a whitening image in which the contrast of a boundary is improved by performing histogram shifting on the image; generating a border image by detecting a border existing in the image by applying a border detection algorithm to the whitening image; extracting a plurality of feature points from a plurality of boundary line images of a plurality of images photographed in various directions; and generating a 3D point cloud for an object included in an image by matching three or more feature points of different viewpoints extracted from different borderline images. The method of claim 1, further comprising generating a mesh or solid 3D model of the object based on the 3D point cloud. The method of claim 1, wherein the generating of the whitening image comprises shifting the histogram of pixels in the left area to the left based on a predefined median brightness value in the histogram for brightness values of each pixel of the image, and shifting the histogram to the left for pixels in the right area. A method of generating 3D information comprising the steps of shifting the histogram to the right with respect to pixels. The method of claim 1, wherein the generating of the whitening image removes pixels having a brightness value below a predetermined minimum cutoff value or pixels having a brightness value above a predetermined maximum cutoff value from among pixel data constituting the image. 3-dimensional information generation method characterized in that it further comprises; reducing the data size of the image by doing. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the generating of the whitening image comprises uniformly distributing pixels having a brightness value below a predetermined threshold in an image histogram in an area exceeding the threshold and performing level shifting. Three-dimensional information generation method characterized in that. The method of claim 1, wherein the generating of the boundary image comprises: detecting a first temporary boundary in an outward direction of the object in the image by applying a first boundary detection algorithm; detecting a second temporary boundary in an inward direction of an object in the image by applying a second boundary detection algorithm; and determining a boundary between the first temporary boundary and the second temporary boundary as the boundary of the object in the image. The method of claim 1, wherein the extracting of the plurality of feature points comprises using a predefined portion of a central region of the boundary line image to extract feature points in order to eliminate distortion caused by lens aberration. Characterized by a method for generating 3D information. The method of claim 1, wherein the generating of the 3D point cloud comprises: generating a temporary point cloud based on three or more feature points of different viewpoints; and obtaining a 3D point cloud by using the three or more feature points of different viewpoints together with the temporary point cloud. The method of claim 1, wherein the image is an image of the surface of the structure, and the step of determining whether the object is located below the surface of the structure based on a 3D point cloud of the object; characterized in that it further comprises A method for generating 3D information. The method of claim 1, wherein the image is an image obtained by photographing a surface of a structure, and displaying a 3D model of the object generated based on a 3D point cloud of the object by superimposing it on the 3D model of the structure; Three-dimensional information generating method characterized in that it further comprises. a whitening unit that performs histogram shifting on an image to generate a whitening image with improved contrast in a border area; a boundary detection unit generating a boundary image by detecting a boundary of an object existing in the image by applying a boundary detection algorithm to the whitening image; a feature point extraction unit extracting a plurality of feature points from a plurality of boundary line images respectively generated for a plurality of images of the object photographed from various directions; and a point cloud generating unit generating a 3D point cloud for the object by matching three or more feature points of different viewpoints extracted from different boundary line images. A computer-readable recording medium recording a computer program for performing the method according to any one of claims 1 to 10.

title: ANONYMIZING PERSONALLY IDENTIFYING INFORMATION IN IMAGE DATA
abstract: Receiving image data of a location in the form of a video feed, pictures, and/or depth information, as examples, from a client device controlled by a user, and/or other data is described. The received data serves as an input to a model (e.g., an artificial intelligence (AI)-based model such as a machine learning model) configured to generate an electronic representation of the location enriched with spatially localized details about surfaces and contents of the location. The electronic representation can be used for various purposes. The present disclosure provides a systems, methods, and computer programs that resolve several impediments in existing three dimensional image data acquisition and/or visualization systems by anonymizing personally identifying information (PII) in the received image data.
claims: 1 . A non-transitory, machine readable medium storing instructions which, when executed by at least one programmable processor, cause the at least one programmable processor to perform operations comprising: receiving image data from an imaging device; determining that a suspect portion of the image data can or does contain personally identifying information (PII); generating anonymized image data by digitally altering the suspect portion of the image data to substantially remove the PII; and modifying the image data by replacing the suspect portion of the image data with the anonymized image data. 2 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein PII comprises information which is related to an identified or identifiable natural person. 3 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein a suspect portion of the image data that can contain PII comprises types of objects identified in the image data that are known to sometimes include PII, but not always. 4 . The medium of claim 3 , wherein the types of objects comprise mirrors, picture frames, photographs, television or computer monitors, windows, and/or text containing objects. 5 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein the PII comprises one or more of an image of a face, a person, an animal, a document listing a name, a document listing an address, an identification card, a calendar, a prescription bottle, a title of a book, a document that includes PII, an image of a landmark, or a license plate. 6 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein the imaging device comprises a depth sensor and/or a camera, and the image data comprises depth information from the depth sensor and/or pictures or video from the camera. 7 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein digitally altering the suspect portion of the image data comprises one or more of gaussian blurring, redacting or removing, or replacement of the suspect image data with a generic image from a library or a generic 3D model. 8 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein digitally altering the suspect portion of the image data comprises blurring the suspect image data. 9 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein a degree of digitally altering the suspect portion of the image data varies from a blurring or pixelation to a full redaction or removal of the suspect portion of the image data. 10 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein substantially removing the PII comprises applying one or more digital alterations to the image data that reduce or eliminate an ability of a person or computer software to obtain the PII from the anonymized image data. 11 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein digitally altering the suspect image data is configured to make it impossible for most humans to obtain the PII by visual inspection of images generated from the anonymized image data. 12 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein digitally altering the suspect image data is configured to make it impossible, or require a significant increase in processing power and/or time, for a computer to extract usable PII from the anonymized image data. 13 . The medium of claim 1 , the operations further comprising determining that the suspect portion of the image data does contain PII. 14 . The medium of claim 13 , the determining comprising performing facial recognition on the image data to detect a face, wherein the generating of anonymized image data digitally alters the face. 15 . The medium of claim 13 , the determining comprising performing optical character recognition on the image data to detect characters, wherein the generating of anonymized image data digitally alters the characters. 16 . The medium of claim 1 , the operations further comprising: identifying an object from the image data; determining that the object is a suspect object that can include the PII; and generating the anonymized image data for at least a portion of the suspect object. 17 . The medium of claim 16 , the operations further comprising: identifying a plurality of objects from the image data; and generating anonymized image data for only the suspect object and not generating anonymized image data for any of the plurality of objects not identified as suspect objects. 18 . The medium of claim 17 , wherein generating anonymized image data only for the suspect object comprises anonymizing the PII and potential incidental anonymization of nearby image data including pixels near a boundary of a photo, face, or document; and/or pixels in spaces between and inside text. 19 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein the anonymized image data comprises only the PII and does not include other portions of the image data that do not contain the PII. 20 . The medium of claim 1 , the operations further comprising: storing the suspect portion of the image data in temporary computer memory; performing the generation of the anonymized image data from the suspect portion without storing the suspect portion in permanent computer memory; and transferring the anonymized image data to permanent computer memory. 21 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein the generating of the anonymized image data is performed at the imaging device. 22 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein the determining is performed in a first part at the imaging device and in a second part at a server. 23 . The medium of claim 22 , wherein the first part includes performing facial recognition and the second part includes performing object identification. 24 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein the suspect portion is anonymized within a two dimensional (2D) bounding box, a three dimensional (3D) bounding box, a 2D polygon, a 3D polygon, or a semantic segmentation. 25 . The medium of claim 1 , wherein the image data is 3D. 26 . The medium of claim 25 , wherein determining that a suspect portion of the image data can or does contain personally identifying information (PII) comprises performing object detection and mask prediction on the image data using a neural network. 27 . The medium of claim 26 , wherein the neural network is a mask region based convolutional neural network (R-CNN). 28 . The medium of claim 26 , wherein generating anonymized image data by digitally altering the suspect portion of the image data to substantially remove the PII, and modifying the image data by replacing the suspect portion of the image data with the anonymized image data comprises: extracting binary masks from neural network outputs and saving the binary masks to a video file; saving a metadata file that stores indices of frames of the image data in which PII was detected; combining the binary masks with the image data based on the indices; and applying a blur to frames of the image data that contain PII based on the masks. 29 . The medium of claim 1 , the operations further comprising generating a 2D image or 3D model based at least on the anonymized image data. 30 . The medium of claim 1 , the operations further comprising generating, with a machine learning model, an inventory list based at least on the anonymized image data.

title: UNDERGROUND PIPELINE INTEGRITY EVALUATION SYSTEM AND METHOD; 一种地下管道完整性评价系统及方法
abstract:
本发明实施例公开了一种地下管道完整性评价系统及方法，所述系统包括：三维模型构建模块，用于根据针对炼厂设备、地上标志物以及与炼厂设备连接的管道实地勘测结果，在三维可视化平台中建立关于炼厂设备、地上标志物以及与炼厂设备连接的管道的三维模型；管线信息获取模块，用于根据建立的三维模型获取管线信息；管道完整性评价模块，用于基于与建立的三维模型的交互，对管道进行管道基础数据管理、检测数据管理、缺陷数据管理和缺陷评价及维修决策管理。本发明实施例通过与埋地管道三维可视化平台交互，实现埋地管道，特别是炼厂内地下管道管体缺陷的分析评价和维修决策。

The embodiment of the present invention discloses an underground pipeline integrity evaluation system and method, the system comprising: a three-dimensional model construction module, which is used to establish a three-dimensional model of refinery equipment, ground markers and pipelines connected to refinery equipment in a three-dimensional visualization platform based on field survey results of refinery equipment, ground markers and pipelines connected to refinery equipment; a pipeline information acquisition module, which is used to obtain pipeline information based on the established three-dimensional model; a pipeline integrity evaluation module, which is used to perform pipeline basic data management, detection data management, defect data management, defect evaluation and maintenance decision management on the pipeline based on interaction with the established three-dimensional model. The embodiment of the present invention realizes analysis and evaluation of defects in the buried pipeline, especially underground pipelines in refineries, and maintenance decisions by interacting with the buried pipeline three-dimensional visualization platform.
claims:
1 . 一种地下管道完整性评价系统，其特征在于，包括： 三维模型构建模块，用于根据针对炼厂设备、地上标志物以及与炼厂设备连接的管道实地勘测结果，在三维可视化平台中建立关于炼厂设备、地上标志物以及与炼厂设备连接的管道的三维模型； 管线信息获取模块，用于根据建立的三维模型获取管线信息； 管道完整性评价模块，用于基于与建立的三维模型的交互，对管道进行管道基础数据管理、检测数据管理、缺陷数据管理和缺陷评价及维修决策管理； 管道完整性评价模块在基于与建立的三维模型的交互，对管道进行缺陷数据管理时， 具体用于： 对检测管道所检出的缺陷，按照不同缺陷类型分类进行缺陷信息管理，与三维模型交互，实现检测管道缺陷信息的增、查、删、改； 通过缺陷点编号实现缺陷点在三维模型中的高亮定位，并通过下拉列表，选择年份、装置、管道编号、检测工程，查看该检测工程下所有的缺陷点及缺陷信息； 管道完整性评价模块在三维模型上新增缺陷时，具体采用如下两种方式实现： A、对于单个的缺陷，在已知缺陷定位的前提下，在管道的三维模型上通过在检测管道上的标志物进行标尺测量，直接添加缺陷点位置，之后录入缺陷数据； B、对于批量缺陷，通过下载缺陷信息模板并编辑缺陷信息实现批量缺陷数据的导入， 缺陷数据导入成功后，针对每一条缺陷数据人工录入缺陷定位，并将新增缺陷在三维模型中的关联展示；或在列表中选定要添加定位的缺陷，通过缺陷定位的添加按钮，在三维管道上添加缺陷，并将缺陷信息自动更新到三维缺陷点上； 管道完整性评价模块在三维模型上删除缺陷时，具体用于采用如下三种方式实现： A、删除缺陷点位置保留缺陷数据，再次添加相同缺陷编号的缺陷位置后，自动更新缺陷数据； B、删除缺陷数据而保留三维缺陷点位置，再次点击三维缺陷点新增信息后，自动关联到三维缺陷点上； C、将缺陷数据和三维模型管道上的缺陷位置同时删除。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的地下管道完整性评价系统，其特征在于，管道完整性评价模块在基于与建立的三维模型的交互，对管道进行检测数据管理时，具体用于： 在管线三维模型上，通过检测标志物，由人工定位在待检测管线上点选确定检测管道的检测范围； 根据检测管道所在的管线自动生成检测管道编号； 根据检测管道编号，实现与三维模型中检测管道的高亮定位； 对检测管道的信息进行维护和修改。 3 .根据权利要求1所述的地下管道完整性评价系统，其特征在于，管道完整性评价模块在基于与建立的三维模型的交互，对管道进行检测数据管理时，具体用于： 在三维模型新增的检测管道下新建检测工程，对检测工程及其相关信息进行维护管理； 通过检测管道编号，查看对应检测管道下的所有检测工程列表。 4 .根据权利要求1所述的地下管道完整性评价系统，其特征在于，管道完整性评价模块 在基于与建立的三维模型的交互，对管道进行缺陷评价及维修决策管理时，具体用于： 针对信息缺失或无法量化尺寸及类型的缺陷，通过调用信号图，通过专家经验对单一缺陷进行过人工评价并给出维修建议； 其中，单一缺陷人工评价是在人工评价功能开启后，选择要评价的缺陷，该缺陷所在管道对应的检测信号图自动与三维模型中管道的起终点对应，以直接定位该缺陷点的信号， 通过分析缺陷信号对缺陷直接给出严重程度评价及维修建议； 其中，批量人工评价是通过选择炼厂设备，管道编号，检测工程，缺陷类型后，对检测工程下的某一类缺陷点，直接进行批量评价和维修决策。 5 .根据权利要求1所述的地下管道完整性评价系统，其特征在于，管道完整性评价模块在基于与建立的三维模型的交互，对管道进行缺陷评价及维修决策管理时，具体用于： 针对定量的缺陷，通过检测获得缺陷尺寸和缺陷类型的批量缺陷，对于不同的缺陷类型采用的缺陷评价方式不同； 其中，单一缺陷自动评价是针对缺陷类型已知的缺陷点，选择需要评价的缺陷点，选择评价方法，进行缺陷点自动评价； 其中，批量缺陷自动评价是通过选择装置、管道编号、检测工程、缺陷类型后，对需要评价的缺陷点，选择评价方法、输入评价所需信息后通过系统内置评价算法，批量评价相应类型的缺陷。 6 .根据权利要求1所述的地下管道完整性评价系统，其特征在于，管道完整性评价模块在基于与建立的三维模型的交互，对管道进行管道基础数据管理时，具体用于： 基于所述三维模型以及所述管线信息，从三维可视化平台中获取管道所在管线的基本信息，包括管径、壁厚、管材、输送介质、连接设备信息的增、查、删、改。 7 . An underground pipeline integrity evaluation system, characterized in that it includes: a three-dimensional model construction module, which is used to establish a three-dimensional model of refinery equipment, ground markers and pipelines connected to refinery equipment in a three-dimensional visualization platform based on the field survey results of refinery equipment, ground markers and pipelines connected to refinery equipment; a pipeline information acquisition module, which is used to obtain pipeline information based on the established three-dimensional model; a pipeline integrity evaluation module, which is used to perform pipeline basic data management, detection data management, defect data management and defect evaluation and maintenance decision management for the pipeline based on the interaction with the established three-dimensional model; when the pipeline integrity evaluation module performs defect data management for the pipeline based on the interaction with the established three-dimensional model, it is specifically used to: manage defect information of defects detected by the detection pipeline according to different defect types, interact with the three-dimensional model, and realize the addition, query, deletion and modification of the detection pipeline defect information; highlight the defect point in the three-dimensional model by numbering the defect point, and select the year, device, pipeline number, and detection project through the drop-down list to view all defect points and defect information under the detection project; When the pipeline integrity assessment module adds defects to the 3D model, it is implemented in the following two ways: A. For a single defect, under the premise of knowing the defect location, the defect point location is directly added on the three-dimensional model of the pipeline by measuring the markers on the inspection pipeline, and then the defect data is entered; B. For batch defects, batch defect data can be imported by downloading defect information templates and editing defect information. After the defect data is successfully imported, the defect location is manually entered for each defect data, and the newly added defects are displayed in the 3D model; or the defects to be added are selected in the list, and the defect location is added to the 3D pipeline through the Add button of the defect location, and the defect information is automatically updated to the 3D defect point; When the pipeline integrity evaluation module deletes defects on the 3D model, it is specifically used in the following three ways: A. Delete the defect data and keep the 3D defect point position. Click the 3D defect point again to add new information, and it will be automatically associated with the 3D defect point. C. technical_means: 画像生成・再構成
llm_reviewer_comment_short: 製造物や設備の欠陥・異常検出を画像処理で扱っており、専門家確認候補として強い。
llm_reviewer_comment_detail: 課題文脈は欠陥・異常検出の目的を比較的よく表している。解決手段文脈は処理手順やモデル構成を読み取れる。技術手段は関連するが一般語・抽象語が中心である。製造欠陥検出・画像検査への対応が明確で、代表特許候補として説明しやすい。
human_reviewer_comment: 地下管道の完全性評価・欠陥管理であり、設備保全の欠陥評価としては関係するが、画像に基づく製造欠陥検出の候補としては弱い。

title: A DEFECT IDENTIFICATION METHOD FOR SEALING SURFACE ERROR EVALUATION BASED ON OPTICAL MEASUREMENT POINT CLOUD; 一种光学测量点云的密封面误差评估缺陷识别方法
abstract:
本发明涉及密封面测量缺陷识别技术领域，具体公开了一种光学测量点云的密封面误差评估缺陷识别方法。该方法包括：1、采集主泵法兰密封面点云数据；2、对点云数据进行预处理；5、根据主泵法兰密封面的点云数据，进行缺陷识别；5.1、建立误差色谱图；5.2、利用误差色谱的颜色变换梯度，获得梯度变换超过阈值的像素点所围城的封闭区域，实现对密封面缺陷的自动识别。该方法克服来现有测量方法无法实现原位、高精度、高效率自动化测量的缺陷；同时，基于测量原始点云数据自动评估零件2D尺寸误差、圆度/平面度误差，识别表面缺陷，简化了数据处理和误差评估的过程，提升了效率和精度，克服来传统方法获取深度信息困难的缺点。

The invention relates to the technical field of sealing surface measurement defect identification, and specifically discloses a sealing surface error evaluation defect identification method based on an optical measurement point cloud. The method includes: 1. Collecting point cloud data of the sealing surface of the flange of the main pump; 2. Preprocessing the point cloud data; 5. Identifying defects according to the point cloud data of the sealing surface of the flange of the main pump; 5.1. Establishing an error chromatogram Fig. 5.2. Use the color transformation gradient of the error spectrum to obtain the enclosed area surrounded by the pixels whose gradient transformation exceeds the threshold, and realize the automatic identification of sealing surface defects. This method overcomes the defect that the existing measurement methods cannot achieve in-situ, high-precision, and high-efficiency automatic measurement; at the same time, based on the measurement original point cloud data, the 2D size error, roundness/flatness error of the part are automatically evaluated, surface defects are identified, and the simplification The process of data processing and error evaluation is improved, the efficiency and accuracy are improved, and the shortcomings of traditional methods that are difficult to obtain depth information are overcome.

title: A PIPE NETWORK MANAGEMENT METHOD AND SYSTEM BASED ON BIG DATA ANALYSIS; 一种基于大数据分析的管网管理方法及系统
abstract:
本发明涉及一种基于大数据分析的管网管理方法及系统，属于互联网服务技术领域。其中，该方法包括：获取管网和管段的测绘数据、排查数据、检测数据和修复数据，基于测绘数据建立管网模型，将排查数据、检测数据和修复数据都挂接到管网模型上。对排查出现问题的管点和管段进行检测，对检测得到的视频结果进行自动判读和缺陷等级评定。实现了自动化的数据校验复核、智能化的数据挂接和图形化的结果展示，保障管网管理的时效性和规范性。

The invention relates to a pipe network management method and system based on big data analysis, and belongs to the technical field of Internet services. Among them, the method includes: obtaining surveying and mapping data, inspection data, detection data and repair data of the pipeline network and pipe sections, establishing a pipeline network model based on the surveying and mapping data, and linking the inspection data, inspection data and repair data to the pipeline network model. Detect pipe points and pipe sections where problems arise, and automatically interpret and evaluate defect levels on the video results obtained. It realizes automated data verification and review, intelligent data hooking and graphical result display, ensuring the timeliness and standardization of pipeline network management.

problem_context: 对排查出现问题的管点和管段进行检测，对检测得到的视频结果进行自动判读和缺陷等级评定。 Detect pipe points and pipe sections where problems arise, and automatically interpret and evaluate defect levels on the video results obtained.
solution_context: 本发明涉及一种基于大数据分析的管网管理方法及系统，属于互联网服务技术领域。 其中，该方法包括：获取管网和管段的测绘数据、排查数据、检测数据和修复数据，基于测绘数据建立管网模型，将排查数据、检测数据和修复数据都挂接到管网模型上。 一种基于大数据分析的管网管理方法，其特征在于，包括： S1 ：获取管点和管段的测绘数据,对所述测绘数据进行数据验证，生成管网管理地区的管网模型，所述测绘数据中包含所述管点和管段的基本属性，所述基本属性中包含管点坐标和管段坐标； S2 :获取对所述管点和管段排查得到的排查数据，所述排查数据与所述管网模型挂接； S3：获取所述管点和管段中经过排查出现问题的所述管点和管段的检测数据，所述检测数据与所述管网模型挂接，对所述检测数据进行数据验证，对视频检测结果进行自动判读和缺陷等级评定； S4 :获取所述有问题的管点和管段的修复数据，所述修复数据与所述检测数据挂接； 所述数据验证的方法为： 将所述测绘数据、检测数据和修复数据载入验证项集，所述验证项集包括:一般属性验证项、业务信息验证项、业务逻辑验证项、基础空间验证项、空间业务验证项； 核对所述验证项集中一般属性验证项的属性值是否包含在所述属性值的限定值域内； 将所述验证项集中的所述业务信息验证项和业务逻辑验证项输入业务函数模型，验证输出结果是否符合预设的业务规范； 核对所述验证项集中所述基础空间验证项的唯一性标识是否重复及是否缺失； 核对所述验证项集中所述空间验证项是否符合预设的字段规则； 所述挂接方法为： 所述测绘数据存储于管点表和管段表中，所述管点表的主键为管点编码，两个所述管点编码分别构成所述管段表中的起点编码和终点编码，得到一个管段； 所述排查数据通过所述起点编码和终点编码与所述管点表关联，所述排查数据挂接到所述起点编码和所述终点编码对应的所述管段上； 所述检测数据存储于检测数据表中，所述检测数据表的主键为工程编码，所述检测数据通过所述起点编码和终点编码与所述管点表关联，所述检测数据挂接到所述起点编码和所述终点编码对应的所述管段上； 所述修复数据通过所述起点编码和终点编码与所述管点表关联，同时通过所述工程编码与所述检测数据表关联，所述修复数据挂接到所述起点编码和所述终点编码对应的所述管段上，同时挂接到所述工程编码对应的所述检测数据上。

title: AUTOMATIC DETECTION METHOD OF CONCRETE STRUCTURE SURFACE DEFECTS BASED ON COMPUTER VISION; 基于计算机视觉的混凝土结构表面缺陷自动检测方法
abstract:
本发明属于计算机视觉技术领域，具体涉及一种基于计算机视觉的混凝土结构表面缺陷自动检测方法，包括对视频数据进行时间轴上采样获取图像，再将图像输入深度卷积神经网络模型获取缺陷的位置、缺陷的类别以及缺陷的分割效果。在实际的混凝土结构检测中，本发明的方法基于深度学习框架下的视频图像识别技术，从根本上解决了以往计算机视觉方法中存在的误检率高、通用性差等实际问题；从视频图像数据的处理和最终结果的输出，具有自动化程度高、实时性好、准确率高、通用性好、便于后期升级维护等优点。

The invention belongs to the technical field of computer vision, and specifically relates to a computer vision-based automatic detection method for surface defects of concrete structures, which includes sampling video data on a time axis to obtain images, and then inputting the images into a deep convolutional neural network model to obtain the position of the defect , Defect category and defect segmentation effect. In the actual concrete structure inspection, the method of the present invention is based on the video image recognition technology under the deep learning framework, and fundamentally solves the actual problems of high false detection rate and poor versatility in the previous computer vision methods; from the video image data The processing and the output of the final result have the advantages of high automation, good real-time performance, high accuracy, good versatility, and ease of later upgrade and maintenance.
claims:
1. 一种基于计算机视觉的混凝土结构表面缺陷自动检测方法,其特征在于:对视频数 据进行时间轴上采样获取图像，图像输入深度卷积神经网络模型获取缺陷的位置、缺陷的 类别以及缺陷的分割效果。 2. 根据权利要求1所述的基于计算机视觉的混凝土结构表面缺陷自动检测方法，其特 征在于:所述深度卷积神经网络模型包括： 特征提取模块，用于提取图像特征； 缺陷位置生成模块，用于根据图像特征提取出图像中的疑似缺陷目标区域； 缺陷区域位置检测模块，用于获取疑似缺陷目标区域中缺陷在图像中的精确坐标信 息； 缺陷区域分类模块，用于确定疑似缺陷目标区域中缺陷的类别； 缺陷区域分割模块，用于对疑似缺陷目标区域中的缺陷进行语义分割。 3. 根据权利要求2所述的基于计算机视觉的混凝土结构表面缺陷自动检测方法，其特 征在于:所述特征提取模块包括ResNet50网络模块，用于提取图像的特征向量。 4. 根据权利要求3所述的基于计算机视觉的混凝土结构表面缺陷自动检测方法，其特 征在于:所述缺陷位置生成模块包括感兴趣区域提取模块，将图像的特征向量输入感兴趣 区域提取模块，生成疑似缺陷目标区域的目标框，提取目标框对应的目标区域特征向量，将 所有目标区域特征向量调整到统一的维度;生成目标存在置信度的概率值，并将概率值按 照大小顺序排序，选取概率值大于预设值的目标区域特征向量，作为缺陷区域位置检测模 块、缺陷区域分类模块、缺陷区域分割模块的输入。 5. 根据权利要求4所述的基于计算机视觉的混凝土结构表面缺陷自动检测方法，其特 征在于:所述缺陷区域位置检测模块，将目标区域特征向量输入到一线性回归器中计算误 差并进行反向传播，最终得到缺陷所在区域的最小外接矩形。 6. 根据权利要求4所述的基于计算机视觉的混凝土结构表面缺陷自动检测方法，其特 征在于：所述缺陷区域分类模块，将目标区域特征向量输入到一全连接层并传输给Soft Max激活层，得到疑似缺陷目标区域对应于各个类别的概率预测向量。 7. 根据权利要求4所述的基于计算机视觉的混凝土结构表面缺陷自动检测方法，其特 征在于:所述缺陷区域分割模块，将目标区域特征向量输入到一 Unet网络模块中对疑似缺 陷目标区域中的缺陷进行语义分割。 8. 根据权利要求2-7任一所述的基于计算机视觉的混凝土结构表面缺陷自动检测方 法，其特征在于:所述深度卷积神经网络模型的训练过程如下： S10、准备训练数据； S20、通过I mageNet数据集训练ResNet50网络模块； S30、训练感兴趣区域提取模块； S40、训练缺陷区域位置检测模块、缺陷区域分类模块和缺陷区域分割模块； S50、调整学习率，然后重复执行S30、S4 0两个步骤，直到设定的损失函数数值收敛到设 定的误差值以下。 9. 根据权利要求8所述的基于计算机视觉的混凝土结构表面缺陷自动检测方法，其特 征在于:所述准备训练数据包括对缺陷的训练数据进行数据增强，模拟生成出各种环境下 的混凝土表面缺陷的成像效果图像。

1. A computer vision-based automatic detection method for surface defects of concrete structures, which is characterized by: sampling the video data on the time axis to obtain images, and inputting the image into a deep convolutional neural network model to obtain the location of the defect, the category of the defect, and the segmentation effect of the defect . 2. The method for automatically detecting surface defects of concrete structures based on computer vision according to claim 1, wherein the deep convolutional neural network model comprises: a feature extraction module for extracting image features; a defect location generation module for Extract the suspected defect target area in the image according to the image characteristics; The defect area location detection module is used to obtain the precise coordinate information of the defect in the suspected defect target area in the image; The defect area classification module is used to determine the defect in the suspected defect target area The category of the defect area segmentation module, which is used for semantic segmentation of defects in the suspected defect target area. 3. The method for automatically detecting surface defects of concrete structures based on computer vision according to claim 2, wherein the feature extraction module includes a ResNet50 network module for extracting feature vectors of images. 4. The method for automatically detecting surface defects of concrete structures based on computer vision according to claim 3, wherein the defect location generation module includes a region of interest extraction module, and the feature vector of the image is input into the region of interest extraction module to generate a suspect The target frame of the defect target area, extract the target area feature vector corresponding to the target frame, adjust all the target area feature vectors to a uniform dimension; generate the probability value of the target existence confidence, and sort the probability values in order of magnitude, and select the probability value The feature vector of the target area larger than the preset value is used as the input of the defect area position detection module, the defect area classification module, and the defect area segmentation module. 5. The computer vision-based automatic detection method for surface defects of concrete structures according to claim 4, wherein the defect area position detection module inputs the feature vector of the target area into a linear regression device to calculate the error and perform back propagation , And finally get the smallest bounding rectangle of the defect area. 6. The computer vision-based automatic detection method for surface defects of concrete structures according to claim 4, wherein the defect area classification module inputs the target area feature vector to a fully connected layer and transmits it to the Soft Max activation layer to obtain The suspected defect target area corresponds to the probability prediction vector of each category. 7. The computer vision-based automatic detection method for surface defects of concrete structures according to claim 4, characterized in that: the defect area segmentation module inputs the target area feature vector into a Unet network module to detect defects in the target area of suspected defects Perform semantic segmentation. 8. The method for automatically detecting surface defects of concrete structures based on computer vision according to any one of claims 2-7, wherein the training process of the deep convolutional neural network model is as follows: S10. Adjust the learning rate, and then repeat the two steps of S30 and S40 until the set loss function value converges below the set error value. 9. The method for automatically detecting surface defects of concrete structures based on computer vision according to claim 8, characterized in that: the preparation of training data includes data enhancement of training data of defects to simulate and generate concrete surface defects in various environments. Imaging effect image.

title: IMAGE PROCESSING METHOD, DEVICE, TRAIN AND EQUIPMENT; 图像处理方法、装置、列车和设备
abstract:
本公开提供了一种图像处理方法、装置、列车和设备，可以应用于图像处理技术领域，该方法包括：响应于图像处理指令，获取受电弓在异常工作状态下的受电弓运行图像，其中，异常工作状态包括异常环境状态和/或受电弓异常状态；利用目标图像分割网络对受电弓运行图像进行分割处理，得到初始分割图像；基于像素面积判定规则对初始分割图像进行像素判定，得到判定结果；在判定结果表征初始分割图像满足像素面积判定规则的情况下，对初始分割图像进行连通连通区域分析处理，得到分析结果；在分析结果表征初始分割图像的连通区域满足预设区域数量阈值的情况下，将初始分割图像确定为用于异常检测的目标分割图像。

The present disclosure provides an image processing method, an apparatus, a train and equipment, which can be applied to the field of image processing technology. The method comprises: in response to an image processing instruction, obtaining a pantograph operation image of a pantograph in an abnormal working state, wherein the abnormal working state comprises an abnormal environmental state and/or an abnormal pantograph state; performing segmentation processing on the pantograph operation image using a target image segmentation network to obtain an initial segmented image; performing pixel determination on the initial segmented image based on a pixel area determination rule to obtain a determination result; when the determination result indicates that the initial segmented image satisfies the pixel area determination rule, performing connected region analysis processing on the initial segmented image to obtain an analysis result; when the analysis result indicates that the connected region of the initial segmented image satisfies a preset region quantity threshold, determining the initial segmented image as a target segmented image for abnormality detection.
claims:
1. 一种用于受电弓异常检测的图像处理方法，包括： 响应于图像处理指令，获取受电弓在异常工作状态下的受电弓运行图像，其中，所述异常工作状态包括异常环境状态和/或受电弓异常状态； 利用目标图像分割网络对所述受电弓运行图像进行分割处理，得到初始分割图像； 基于像素面积判定规则对所述初始分割图像进行像素判定，得到判定结果； 在所述判定结果表征所述初始分割图像满足所述像素面积判定规则的情况下，对所述初始分割图像进行连通区域分析处理，得到分析结果； 在所述分析结果表征所述初始分割图像的连通区域满足预设区域数量阈值的情况下， 将所述初始分割图像确定为用于异常检测的目标分割图像。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其中，获取受电弓在异常工作状态下的受电弓运行图像，包括： 在所述异常工作状态为所述异常环境状态的情况下，利用环境模拟装置在所述受电弓处施加异常工况环境； 采集所述受电弓工作在所述异常工况环境下的第一图像，并将所述第一图像作为所述受电弓运行图像。 3 .根据权利要求2所述的方法，其中，利用环境模拟装置在所述受电弓处施加异常工况环境，包括： 利用喷淋装置对所述受电弓处进行喷淋处理，以模拟雨天环境；和/或利用烟雾装置对所述受电弓处进行喷雾处理，以模拟雾霾环境；和/或利用流场模拟装置对所述受电弓处进行吹风处理，以模拟风暴环境；和/或利用背景模拟装置模拟所述受电弓的工作背景环境。 4 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其中，获取受电弓在异常工作状态下的受电弓运行图像，包括： 在所述异常工作状态为所述受电弓异常状态的情况下，对所述受电弓的目标组件进行调整处理，得到异常受电弓； 采集所述异常受电弓工作时的第二图像，并将所述第二图像作为所述受电弓运行图像。 5 .根据权利要求4所述的方法，其中，对所述受电弓的目标组件进行调整处理，得到异常受电弓，包括： 对第一目标组件进行替换或拆解处理，得到所述异常受电弓，其中，所述第一目标组件包括弓角和弓头中的至少一个;和/或对第二目标组件的工作状态进行调整，得到所述异常受电弓，其中，所述第二目标组件包括受电弓升降系统。 6 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其中，所述目标图像分割网络是通过如下方式训练的： 获取图像训练集，其中，所述图像训练集包括多个受电弓运行样本图像和与每个所述受电弓运行样本图像对应的标签图像，所述标签图像包括受电弓样本的像素标识； 将所述受电弓运行样本图像输入初始图像分割网络，输出融合图像，其中，所述初始图像分割网络是根据视觉自监督模型和特征金字塔网络生成的； 将所述融合图像和与所述融合图像对应的标签图像输入至目标损失函数，输出损失结 果； 根据所述损失结果迭代地调整所述初始图像分割网络的网络参数，得到经训练的所述目标图像分割网络。 7 .根据权利要求6所述的方法，其中，将所述受电弓运行样本图像输入初始图像分割网络，输出融合图像，包括： 利用所述视觉自监督模型处理所述受电弓运行样本图像，得到第一图像特征； 利用第一反卷积层处理所述第一图像特征，得到放大后的第二图像特征； 利用第二反卷积层处理所述第一图像特征，得到放大后的第三图像特征； 利用恒等映射网络处理所述第一图像特征进行，得到第四图像特征； 利用池化层处理所述第一图像特征，得到缩小后的第五图像特征，其中，所述特征金字塔网络包括所述第一反卷积层、所述第二反卷积层、所述恒等映射网络和所述池化层； 对所述第二图像特征、所述第三图像特征、所述第四图像特征和所述第五图像特征进行特征融合处理，得到所述融合图像，其中，所述融合图像中对所述受电弓样本的每个像素进行了标记。 8 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其中，基于像素面积判定规则对所述初始分割图像进行像素判定，得到判定结果，包括： 计算所述初始分割图像中所述受电弓的像素的像素数量； 在所述像素数量满足第一数量区间的情况下，生成第一判定结果，其中，所述第一判定结果表征所述初始分割图像满足所述像素面积判定规则； 在所述像素数量不满足所述第一数量区间的情况下，生成第二判定结果，其中，所述判定结果包括所述第一判定结果或所述第二判定结果。 9 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其中，所述初始分割图像包括弓头区域、支撑杆区域和底座区域，所述预设区域数量阈值包括第二数量区间、第三数量区间和第四数量区间； 其中，对所述初始分割图像进行连通区域分析处理，得到分析结果，包括： 在所述弓头区域内的部件数量满足所述第二数量区间的情况下，生成第一分析结果； 在所述支撑杆区域内的部件数量满足所述第三数量区间的情况下，生成第二分析结果； 在所述底座区域内的部件数量满足所述第四数量区间的情况下，生成第三分析结果； 根据所述第一分析结果、所述第二分析结果和所述第三分析结果，生成所述分析结果。 10 . 一种用于受电弓异常检测的图像处理装置，包括： 获取模块，用于响应于图像处理指令，获取受电弓在异常工作状态下的受电弓运行图像，其中，所述异常工作状态包括异常环境状态和/或受电弓异常状态； 分割模块，用于利用目标图像分割网络对所述受电弓运行图像进行分割处理，得到初始分割图像； 判定模块，用于基于像素面积判定规则对所述初始分割图像进行像素判定，得到判定结果； 分析模块，用于在所述判定结果表征所述初始分割图像满足所述像素面积判定规则的情况下，对所述初始分割图像进行连通区域分析处理，得到分析结果； 确定模块，用于在所述分析结果表征所述初始分割图像的连通区域满足预设区域数量 阈值的情况下，将所述初始分割图像确定为用于异常检测的目标分割图像。 11 .一种用于受电弓异常检测的列车，包括如权利要求10所述的图像处理装置。 12 . 一种电子设备，包括： 一个或多个处理器； 存储器，用于存储一个或多个程序， 其中，当所述一个或多个程序被所述一个或多个处理器执行时，使得所述一个或多个处理器实现权利要求1至9中任一项所述的方法。

1. An image processing method for pantograph abnormality detection, comprising: in response to an image processing instruction, obtaining a pantograph operation image of a pantograph in an abnormal working state, wherein the abnormal working state includes an abnormal environmental state and/or an abnormal pantograph state; performing segmentation processing on the pantograph operation image using a target image segmentation network to obtain an initial segmented image; performing pixel determination on the initial segmented image based on a pixel area determination rule to obtain a determination result; when the determination result indicates that the initial segmented image satisfies the pixel area determination rule, performing connected area analysis processing on the initial segmented image to obtain an analysis result; when the analysis result indicates that the connected area of the initial segmented image satisfies a preset area number threshold, determining the initial segmented image as a target segmented image for abnormality detection. 2 .The method according to claim 1, wherein obtaining a pantograph operation image of a pantograph in an abnormal working state comprises: when the abnormal working state is the abnormal environmental state, applying an abnormal working condition environment at the pantograph using an environmental simulation device; collecting a first image of the pantograph working under the abnormal working condition environment, and using the first image as the pantograph operation image. 3 .The method according to claim 2, wherein an abnormal working condition is applied to the pantograph by using an environmental simulation device, including: using a spray device to spray the pantograph to simulate a rainy day environment; and/or using a smoke device to spray the pantograph to simulate a haze environment; and/or using a flow field simulation device to blow air to the pantograph to simulate a storm environment; and/or using a background simulation device to simulate the working background environment of the pantograph. 4 .The method according to claim 1, wherein obtaining a pantograph operation image of a pantograph in an abnormal working state comprises: when the abnormal working state is an abnormal state of the pantograph, adjusting and processing the target components of the pantograph to obtain an abnormal pantograph; collecting a second image when the abnormal pantograph is working, and using the second image as the pantograph operation image. 5 .The method according to claim 4, wherein the target component of the pantograph is adjusted to obtain an abnormal pantograph, comprising: replacing or disassembling the first target component to obtain the abnormal pantograph, wherein the first target component includes at least one of a bow angle and a bow head; and/or adjusting the working state of the second target component to obtain the abnormal pantograph, wherein the second target component includes a pantograph lifting system. 6 .The method according to claim 1, wherein the target image segmentation network is trained in the following manner: obtaining an image training set, wherein the image training set includes a plurality of pantograph operation sample images and a label image corresponding to each of the pantograph operation sample images, and the label image includes a pixel identifier of the pantograph sample; inputting the pantograph operation sample image into an initial image segmentation network, and outputting a fused image, wherein the initial image segmentation network is generated based on a visual self-supervised model and a feature pyramid network; inputting the fused image and the label image corresponding to the fused image into a target loss function, and outputting a loss result. Iteratively adjusting the network parameters of the initial image segmentation network according to the loss result to obtain the trained target image segmentation network. 7 .The method according to claim 6, wherein the pantograph operation sample image is input into the initial image segmentation network and a fused image is output, comprising: using the visual self-supervised model to process the pantograph operation sample image to obtain a first image feature; using the first deconvolution layer to process the first image feature to obtain an enlarged second image feature; using the second deconvolution layer to process the first image feature to obtain an enlarged third image feature; using the identity mapping network to process the first image feature to obtain a fourth image feature; using the pooling layer to process the first image feature to obtain a reduced fifth image feature, wherein the feature pyramid network includes the first deconvolution layer, the second deconvolution layer, the identity mapping network and the pooling layer; performing feature fusion processing on the second image feature, the third image feature, the fourth image feature and the fifth image feature to obtain the fused image, wherein each pixel of the pantograph sample is marked in the fused image. 8 .The method according to claim 1, wherein pixel determination is performed on the initial segmented image based on a pixel area determination rule to obtain a determination result, comprising: calculating the number of pixels of the pantograph in the initial segmented image; generating a first determination result when the number of pixels satisfies a first number interval, wherein the first determination result represents that the initial segmented image satisfies the pixel area determination rule; generating a second determination result when the number of pixels does not satisfy the first number interval, wherein the determination result includes the first determination result or the second determination result. 9 .The method according to claim 1, wherein the initial segmented image includes a bow head area, a support rod area and a base area, and the preset area quantity threshold includes a second quantity interval, a third quantity interval and a fourth quantity interval; wherein the initial segmented image is subjected to connected area analysis processing to obtain an analysis result, including: generating a first analysis result when the number of components in the bow head area satisfies the second quantity interval; generating a second analysis result when the number of components in the support rod area satisfies the third quantity interval; generating a third analysis result when the number of components in the base area satisfies the fourth quantity interval; generating the analysis result based on the first analysis result, the second analysis result and the third analysis result. 10 . An image processing device for pantograph abnormality detection, comprising: an acquisition module, for responding to an image processing instruction to acquire a pantograph operation image of a pantograph in an abnormal working state, wherein the abnormal working state includes an abnormal environmental state and/or an abnormal pantograph state; a segmentation module, for performing segmentation processing on the pantograph operation image using a target image segmentation network to obtain an initial segmented image; a judgment module, for performing pixel judgment on the initial segmented image based on a pixel area judgment rule to obtain a judgment result; an analysis module, for performing connected area analysis processing on the initial segmented image to obtain an analysis result when the judgment result indicates that the initial segmented image satisfies the pixel area judgment rule; a determination module, for determining that the analysis result indicates that the connected area of the initial segmented image satisfies a preset number of areas. When the threshold is set, the initial segmented image is determined as the target segmented image for abnormality detection. 11 .A train for pantograph anomaly detection, comprising the image processing device as claimed in claim 10. 12 . An electronic device, comprising: one or more processors; a memory for storing one or more programs, wherein when the one or more programs are executed by the one or more processors, the one or more processors implement the method described in any one of claims 1 to 9.

llm_reviewer_comment_short: 製造物や設備の欠陥・異常検出を画像処理で扱っており、専門家確認候補として強い。
llm_reviewer_comment_detail: 課題文脈は欠陥・異常検出の目的を比較的よく表している。解決手段文脈は処理手順やモデル構成を読み取れる。技術手段は関連するが一般語・抽象語が中心である。製造欠陥検出・画像検査への対応が明確で、代表特許候補として説明しやすい。
human_reviewer_comment: 列車のパンタグラフ異常状態の画像分割であり、異常検出としては関連するが、製造物表面欠陥の候補としては弱い。

title: A DEFECT DETECTION METHOD FOR INDUSTRIAL RADIOGRAPHIC WELD IMAGES; 一种用于工业射线焊缝图像的缺陷检测方法
abstract:
本发明涉及工业射线焊缝技术领域，具体涉及一种用于工业射线焊缝图像的缺陷检测方法，包括如下步骤：步骤一、获取焊缝缺陷识别准确率最优的分类器并且设定分类器对于焊缝缺陷识别的概率值；步骤二、采用X射线机拍摄实验焊缝，与已知的公有技术相比，具有如下有益效果：本发明通过对实验焊缝分别进行有无铅板的对比检测，计算出X射线机对焊缝检测存在的误差均值JZ，使X射线机对实际焊缝进行检测时，能够将误差均值考虑在内从而与分类器设定的焊缝缺陷识别概率值进行对比判断，解决了现有技术中对焊缝检测判断时避免工件内壁其他部分的散射线影响实际检测结果的情况。

The present invention relates to the technical field of industrial ray welds, and in particular to a defect detection method for industrial ray weld images, comprising the following steps: step one, obtaining a classifier with the best weld defect recognition accuracy and setting a probability value for weld defect recognition by the classifier; step two, photographing an experimental weld with an X-ray machine. Compared with the known public technology, the present invention has the following beneficial effects: by performing comparative detection on the experimental welds with and without lead plates, the present invention calculates an error mean value JZ existing in weld detection by the X-ray machine, so that when the X-ray machine detects the actual weld, the error mean value can be taken into account, thereby performing a comparative judgment with the weld defect recognition probability value set by the classifier, thereby solving the problem in the prior art of avoiding the influence of scattered rays from other parts of the inner wall of the workpiece on the actual detection result when detecting and judging the weld.
