L A defect detection method for industrial ray weld images, characterized in that it includes the following steps: Step 1, obtain a classifier with the best weld defect recognition accuracy and set the probability value of the classifier for weld defect recognition; Step 2, use an X-ray machine to shoot the experimental weld, collect images and input them into the trained improved NanoDet deep network, and obtain experimental weld image data after feature enhancement, feature conversion and standardization, and mark it as SY", where i represents different experimental weld data, i = 1, 2, ..., n; Step 3, place a lead plate at the bottom of the experimental weld, use an X-ray machine to shoot the experimental weld, collect images and input them into the trained improved NanoDet deep network, and obtain standard experimental weld image data after feature enhancement, feature conversion and standardization, and mark it as BZp, where p represents different experimental weld data, p = 1, 2, ..., n; Step 4, respectively compare SY], SY?, ...SY. with BZ], BZ?, ...BZr in turn, and use the formula: WC n = (SY"BZ ) k calculates the corresponding error value: WCrWC2......WJ, (where k is the error coefficient) and averages the obtained WQ to obtain JZ, JZ _ +WQ...WQ. η ' Step 5. Use an X-ray machine to shoot the actual weld, receive the real-time picture in advance, and connect the outer contour of the picture to obtain the outer contour curve of the still picture to be processed, and then use the edge detection algorithm to detect the contour edge line, so that the X-ray machine shoots the middle position of the real-time picture, that is, the centering of the real-time picture is completed; Step 6. Collect the image and input it into the trained improved NanoDet deep network. After feature enhancement, feature conversion and standardization, the actual experimental weld image data is obtained and marked as SJy, where y represents different actual weld data, y = l, 2, , n; Step 7. Subtract the data of JZ from the data of the actual weld image data SJy to obtain the final actual data ZZy, that is, ZZy = (SJ"JZ)h, (where h is the error coefficient value), is input into the classifier, and the classifier identifies the weld defects in the image feature data. If the recognition probability value of the actual weld defect is 2 the weld defect recognition probability value set by the classifier, it is judged that the actual weld has defects, otherwise the actual weld is normal. 2 .A defect detection method for industrial X-ray weld images according to claim 1, characterized in that the specific algorithm of the probability value in step 1 is as follows; S1. Selecting a number of weld image data taken by an X-ray machine, and performing preprocessing on the weld image data including image cropping and size normalization; S2, performing Fourier transform on the preprocessed weld image data to obtain its amplitude spectrum, and taking the first quadrant part of the Fourier transform amplitude spectrum as image feature data; S3, respectively marking defective images and normal images for the image feature data and constituting all samples, randomly selecting a part of all samples to form a training set matrix, and the rest to form a test set matrix; S4. Input the training set matrix into the support vector machine algorithm and obtain the weld defect classifier through training. Use the test set matrix to verify the classifier performance. By adjusting the ratio of the training set matrix to the test set matrix in all samples, the classifier with the best weld defect recognition accuracy is obtained for subsequent automatic image classification, and the probability value of the classifier for weld defect recognition is set. 3 .A defect detection method for industrial X-ray weld images according to claim 1, characterized in that the NanoDet is a FC0S-style single-stage anchor-free target detection model, which uses ATSS for target sampling and uses the Generalized Focal Loss loss function to perform classification and box regression. 4 . According to a defect detection method for industrial X-ray weld images as described in claim 1, it is characterized in that the edge detection algorithm in step five can be one of the differential method, differential edge detection algorithm, and Roberts edge detection operator.
problem_context: 本发明涉及工业射线焊缝技术领域，具体涉及一种用于工业射线焊缝图像的缺陷检测方法，包括如下步骤：步骤一、获取焊缝缺陷识别准确率最优的分类器并且设定分类器对于焊缝缺陷识别的概率值；步骤二、采用X射线机拍摄实验焊缝，与已知的公有技术相比，具有如下有益效果：本发明通过对实验焊缝分别进行有无铅板的对比检测，计算出X射线机对焊缝检测存在的误差均值JZ，使X射线机对实际焊缝进行检测时，能够将误差均值考虑在内从而与分类器设定的焊缝缺陷识别概率值进行对比判断，解决了现有技术中对焊缝检测判断时避免工件内壁其他部分的散射线影响实际检测结果的情况。 Compared with the known public technology, the present invention has the following beneficial effects: by performing comparative detection on the experimental welds with and without lead plates, the present invention calculates an error mean value JZ existing in weld detection by the X-ray machine, so that when the X-ray machine detects the actual weld, the error mean value can be taken into account, thereby performing a comparative judgment with the weld defect recognition probability value set by the classifier, thereby solving the problem in the prior art of avoiding the influence of scattered rays from other parts of the inner wall of the workpiece on the actual detection result when detecting and judging the weld.

title: MACHINE LEARNING METHOD IMPLEMENTED IN AOI DEVICE
abstract: A machine learning method is used for improving accuracy of an automated optical inspection (AOI) device. The method includes obtaining an image of a component to be inspected, processing the image to generate digital image information, establishing a machine learning model according to the digital image information, inputting the digital image information into the machine learning model for determination, verifying accuracy of a result of determination by the machine learning model, adjusting and optimizing the machine learning model according to the result of determination of the accuracy of the machine learning model, and improving the machine learning model until the machine learning model reaches a predetermined accuracy.
claims: WE CLAIM: 1. A machine learning method for improving accuracy of an automated optical inspection (AOI) device, the method comprising: obtaining an image of a component to be inspected; processing the image to generate digital image information; establishing a machine learning model according to the digital image information; inputting the digital image information into the machine learning model for determination; verifying accuracy of a result of determination by the machine learning model; and adjusting and optimizing the machine learning model according to the result of determination of the accuracy of the machine learning model; wherein above process is repeated until the machine learning model reaches a predetermined accuracy. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of processing the image comprises: cropping the image to a predetermined size to center the component in the image; and standardizing a pixel value of each pixel of the image according to a predetermine rule to generate the digital image information. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of verifying accuracy of a result of determination by the machine learning model comprises: sending pictures determined by the machine learning model to be unqualified to a platform for visual inspection by an operator; and comparing a result of determination by the operator to the result of determination by the machine learning model. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the step of adjusting and optimizing the machine learning model according to the result of determination of the accuracy of the machine learning model comprises: adjusting and optimizing the machine learning model if the result of determination by the machine learning model is not the same as the result of determination by the operator; and verifying the accuracy of the result of determination by the machine learning model if the result of determination by the machine learning model is the same as the result of determination by the operator. 5. The method of claim 4 further comprising: saving the machine learning model to the AOI device after the machine learning model is verified. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein: the machine learning model is established by a convolutional neural network. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein: the machine learning model comprises at least four convolution layers, at least four maximum pooling layers, and at least two fully connected layers. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the machine learning model is established by: establishing a corresponding machine learning model for each kind of component. 9. The method of claim 1 further comprising: implementing the machine learning model in the AIO device; obtaining an image of a next component to be inspected; processing the image of the next component to be inspected to generate digital image information; and inputting the digital image information of the image of the next component to be inspected into the machine learning model for determination.
problem_context: A machine learning method is used for improving accuracy of an automated optical inspection (AOI) device.

title: NATURAL LEATHER DEFECT SEGMENTATION METHOD BASED ON MLP MULTILAYER NEURAL NETWORK; 基于MLP多层神经网络的天然皮革缺陷分割方法
abstract:
本发明公开了基于MLP多层神经网络的天然皮革缺陷分割方法，包括：通过图像采集模块获取皮革图像的数据集，将数据集分为训练集和测试集；根据颜色不同，生成皮革颜色特征图；以特征向量作为MLP多层神经网络的输入，经过MLP多层神经网络的特征训练完成皮革缺陷分割模型的构建；将模型文件进行存储；使用传统图像处理方法预处理待分割的皮革图像，使用模型进行皮革图像预测，得到皮革图像的缺陷分割图；使用传统图像处理方法丰富缺陷分割图的细节；提取皮革的外轮廓和缺陷轮廓，转换成DXF文件。本发明适用于皮革的缺陷分割，能够解决分割效率低和分割不够精细的技术问题，降低人工成本，提高生产效率。

The invention discloses a natural leather defect segmentation method based on an MLP multilayer neural network, which includes: acquiring a data set of leather images through an image acquisition module, dividing the data set into a training set and a test set; generating a leather color feature map according to different colors ; Take the feature vector as the input of the MLP multilayer neural network, and complete the construction of the leather defect segmentation model through the feature training of the MLP multilayer neural network; store the model file; use the traditional image processing method to preprocess the leather image to be segmented, use The model predicts the leather image and obtains the defect segmentation map of the leather image; uses traditional image processing methods to enrich the details of the defect segmentation map; extracts the outer contour and defect contour of the leather and converts it into a DXF file. The invention is suitable for the defect division of leather, can solve the technical problems of low division efficiency and insufficient division, reduces labor costs, and improves production efficiency.

title: NON-WOVEN FABRIC SURFACE SMALL DEFECT DETECTION METHOD BASED ON MULTI-CORE SUPPORT VECTOR MACHINE; 基于多核支持向量机的无纺布表面小缺陷检测方法
abstract:
本发明是一种基于多核支持向量机的无纺布表面小缺陷检测方法，属检测技术领域。该方法采集具有代表性的无纺布图像，获取高分辨率的原始图像；对原始图像进行灰度变换和滤波去噪预处理；计算求出相对应的灰度共生矩阵，使用该矩阵对预处理后的图像进行图像纹理特征的提取和分析；选择合适的基核函数进行组合，得到新的核函数，对核函数的参数进行优化；构建基于新的核函数的多核支持向量机模型，使用该向量机对待检测图像进行分类，完成缺陷识别。本发明使用多核支持向量机对图像中的小缺陷进行检测，没有庞大的计算量，具有很好的检测性能。采用本发明方法进行无纺布表面小缺陷检测，可获得较高的信噪比，且背景抑制能力较强。

The invention is a method for detecting small defects on the surface of a non-woven fabric based on a multi-core support vector machine, and belongs to the technical field of detection. This method collects representative non-woven fabric images to obtain high-resolution original images; performs gray-scale transformation and filtering and denoising preprocessing on the original images; calculates the corresponding gray-level co-occurrence matrix, and uses the matrix to predict The processed image is used to extract and analyze image texture features; select the appropriate base kernel function to combine to obtain a new kernel function, and optimize the parameters of the kernel function; construct a multi-core support vector machine model based on the new kernel function, and use The vector machine classifies the image to be inspected and completes the defect recognition. The invention uses a multi-core support vector machine to detect small defects in an image, does not have a huge amount of calculation, and has good detection performance. By adopting the method of the present invention to detect small defects on the surface of the non-woven fabric, a higher signal-to-noise ratio can be obtained, and the background suppression ability is stronger.

title: METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR DETECTING HAIRLINE DEFECTS IN STEEL PLATE ROLLING PROCESS; 一种钢板轧制过程发纹缺陷的检测方法及系统
abstract:
本发明涉及数据处理技术领域，具体涉及一种钢板轧制过程发纹缺陷的检测方法及系统，该方法包括采集钢板上、下表面灰度图像，利用灰度直方图对上、下表面灰度图进行灰度线性变化得到第一上、下表面灰度图。对第一上、下表面灰度图进行自适应阈值分割得到二值图。对二值图进行连通域分析得到连通域集合，获取连通域集合中每个连通域的最小外接矩形，根据每个最小外接矩形得到整个异常部位的角度及临近范围异常分布特征量。根据各异常部位的角度及临近异常分布特征量进行自适应形态学处理去除干扰缺陷得到增强后缺陷图像。根据增强后缺陷图像进行缺陷检测得到发纹缺陷区域，从而实现将发纹缺陷准确检测出来，提高检测精度。

The invention relates to the technical field of data processing, in particular to a method and system for detecting hairline defects in the steel plate rolling process. The grayscale of the image is linearly changed to obtain the first upper and lower surface grayscale images. A binary image is obtained by performing adaptive threshold segmentation on the first upper and lower surface grayscale images. Connected domain analysis is performed on the binary image to obtain a connected domain set, the minimum circumscribing rectangle of each connected domain in the connected domain set is obtained, and the angle of the entire abnormal part and the abnormal distribution feature quantity of the adjacent range are obtained according to each minimum circumscribing rectangle. According to the angle of each abnormal part and the characteristic value of adjacent abnormal distribution, adaptive morphological processing is performed to remove interference defects to obtain an enhanced defect image. The defect detection is performed according to the enhanced defect image to obtain the hairline defect area, so as to realize the accurate detection of the hairline defect and improve the detection accuracy.

title: AN EFFICIENCY IMPROVEMENT METHOD FOR SMALL-SCALE DEFECT DETECTION MODELS BASED ON BACKGROUND SAMPLING; 一种基于背景下采样的小规模缺陷检测模型效率提升方法
abstract:
本发明涉及一种基于背景下采样的小规模缺陷检测模型效率提升方法，所述方法包括以下步骤：步骤1：输入定义过程，步骤2：对输入图像利用特征骨干网络计算特征；步骤3：对于主干网络的输出特征，将其输入两个任务网络：中心点预测和宽高预测，进行估计目标矩形框；该技术方案采用本发明所提出的背景下采样方法能够在特征学习的池化过程中去掉更多的背景点，保留更多缺陷目标点，从而提高模型的检测精度。

This invention relates to a method for improving the efficiency of a small-scale defect detection model based on background sampling. The method includes the following steps: Step 1: Input definition process; Step 2: Calculate features of the input image using a feature backbone network; Step 3: Input the output features of the backbone network into two task networks: center point prediction and width and height prediction, to estimate the target bounding box. This technical solution uses the background sampling method proposed in this invention to remove more background points and retain more defect target points during the pooling process of feature learning, thereby improving the detection accuracy of the model.

title: A VIDEO ANOMALY DETECTION METHOD, SYSTEM, MEDIUM AND DEVICE BASED ON RECONSTRUCTION; 一种基于重构的视频异常检测方法、系统、介质及设备
abstract:
本公开提出一种基于重构的视频异常检测方法、系统、介质及设备，方法包括：将待检测视频序列进行预处理，得到原始图像；将原始图像输入训练好的重构模型，得到最终重构图，根据重构误差判断待检测视频序列是否存在异常；所述重构模型包括改进的变分自编码器和重构补充模块，改进的变分自编码器在编码器和解码器之间添加FSQ量化层和跳跃连接，用于离散化数据和捕获上下文特征，得到初步重构帧；重构补充模块用于提取异常特征构建偏移图；初步重构帧和偏移图合并为最终重构帧。通过计算原始视频帧的偏移图以补充异常检测，增强了异常特征表达；并在幂等生成网络中引入模式搜索正则化方法，缓解了生成模型中的模式崩溃问题，保证模型的稳定性。

The present disclosure proposes a method, system, medium and device for video anomaly detection based on reconstruction, the method comprising: preprocessing the video sequence to be detected to obtain the original image; inputting the original image into a trained reconstruction model to obtain the final reconstructed image, judging whether the video sequence to be detected has anomalies according to the reconstruction error; the reconstruction model comprises an improved variational autoencoder and a reconstruction supplement module, the improved variational autoencoder adds an FSQ quantization layer and a jump connection between the encoder and the decoder to discretize the data and capture context features to obtain a preliminary reconstructed frame; the reconstruction supplement module is used to extract abnormal features to construct an offset map; the preliminary reconstructed frame and the offset map are merged into the final reconstructed frame. By calculating the offset map of the original video frame to supplement the anomaly detection, the expression of abnormal features is enhanced; and the pattern search regularization method is introduced in the idempotent generation network to alleviate the pattern collapse problem in the generation model and ensure the stability of the model.
claims:
1 . 一种基于重构的视频异常检测方法，其特征在于，包括： 获取待检测视频序列，进行预处理,得到原始图像； 将原始图像输入训练好的重构模型，得到最终重构图，基于原始图像和最终重构图像计算重构误差，根据重构误差判断待检测视频序列是否存在异常； 其中，所述重构模型包括改进的变分自编码器和重构补充模块，所述改进的变分自编码器在编码器和解码器之间添加FSQ量化层和跳跃连接，用于离散化数据和捕获上下文特征，得到初步重构帧；所述重构补充模块用于提取异常特征构建偏移图;初步重构帧和偏移图合并为最终重构帧。 2 .如权利要求1所述的一种基于重构的视频异常检测方法，其特征在于，所述预处理包括:使用3D-CNN将待检测视频序列提取为视频帧，调整视频帧大小并进行强度归一化操作。 3 .如权利要求1所述的一种基于重构的视频异常检测方法，其特征在于，所述FSQ量化层对数据的离散化具体为： 根据指定级别列表创建分层结构，对数据的离散程度进行限制； 利用tanh函数对原始图像进行压缩，得到张量； 根据所述分层结构，采用Round ()操作将张量转换为相应维度的离散代码簇。 4 .如权利要求1所述的一种基于重构的视频异常检测方法，其特征在于，所述跳跃连接用于将特定编码器层的输出直接连接到相应解码器层的输入。 5 .如权利要求1所述的一种基于重构的视频异常检测方法，其特征在于，所述重构补充模块用于提取异常特征构建偏移图，具体包括： 提取原始图像的异常特征，输入到变形估计器中获取偏移量； 对偏移量进行上采样，得到标准偏移量； 将标准偏移量与原始输入的标准化坐标格网相加，形成偏移图。 6 .如权利要求1所述的一种基于重构的视频异常检测方法，其特征在于，所述初步重构帧和偏移图合并为最终重构帧，还包括:对最终重构帧进行背景去除。 7 .如权利要求1所述的一种基于重构的视频异常检测方法，其特征在于，所述计算重构误差时计算幂等损失。 8 . 一种基于重构的视频异常检测系统，其特征在于，包括： 获取模块，用于获取待检测视频序列，进行预处理，得到原始图像； 重构模块，用于将原始图像输入训练好的重构模型，得到最终重构图，基于原始图像和最终重构图像计算重构误差，根据重构误差判断待检测视频序列是否存在异常； 其中，所述重构模型包括改进的变分自编码器和重构补充模块，所述改进的变分自编码器在编码器和解码器之间添加FSQ量化层和跳跃连接，用于离散化数据和捕获上下文特征，得到初步重构帧；所述重构补充模块用于提取异常特征构建偏移图;初步重构帧和偏移图合并为最终重构帧。 9 .一种计算机可读存储介质，其上存储有计算机程序，其特征在于，所述程序被处理器执行时实现如权利要求1-7中任一项所述的一种基于重构的视频异常检测方法中的步骤。 10 . 一种计算机设备，包括存储器、处理器及存储在存储器上并可在处理器上运行的计算机程序，其特征在于，所述处理器执行所述程序时实现如权利要求1 - 7中任一项所述的一种基于重构的视频异常检测方法中的步骤。

A video anomaly detection method based on reconstruction, characterized in that it includes: obtaining a video sequence to be detected, performing preprocessing to obtain an original image; inputting the original image into a trained reconstruction model to obtain a final reconstructed image, calculating a reconstruction error based on the original image and the final reconstructed image, and judging whether there is an anomaly in the video sequence to be detected according to the reconstruction error; wherein the reconstruction model includes an improved variational autoencoder and a reconstruction supplement module, the improved variational autoencoder adds an FSQ quantization layer and a jump connection between the encoder and the decoder to discretize data and capture contextual features to obtain a preliminary reconstructed frame; the reconstruction supplement module is used to extract abnormal features to construct an offset map; the preliminary reconstructed frame and the offset map are merged into a final reconstructed frame. 2 .A reconstruction-based video anomaly detection method as described in claim 1, characterized in that the preprocessing includes: using 3D-CNN to extract the video sequence to be detected into video frames, adjusting the video frame size and performing intensity normalization operations. 3 .A reconstruction-based video anomaly detection method as described in claim 1 is characterized in that the discretization of data by the FSQ quantization layer is specifically as follows: a hierarchical structure is created according to a specified level list to limit the degree of discreteness of the data; the original image is compressed using the tanh function to obtain a tensor; according to the hierarchical structure, the tensor is converted into a discrete code cluster of the corresponding dimension using a Round() operation. 4 .A reconstruction-based video anomaly detection method as described in claim 1, characterized in that the jump connection is used to directly connect the output of a specific encoder layer to the input of a corresponding decoder layer. 5 .A reconstruction-based video anomaly detection method as described in claim 1 is characterized in that the reconstruction supplement module is used to extract abnormal features to construct an offset map, specifically including: extracting abnormal features of the original image and inputting them into the deformation estimator to obtain the offset; upsampling the offset to obtain a standard offset; adding the standard offset to the standardized coordinate grid of the original input to form an offset map. 6 .A reconstruction-based video anomaly detection method as described in claim 1 is characterized in that the preliminary reconstructed frame and the offset map are merged into a final reconstructed frame, and also includes: background removal of the final reconstructed frame. 7 .A reconstruction-based video anomaly detection method as described in claim 1, characterized in that the idempotent loss is calculated when calculating the reconstruction error. 8 . A video anomaly detection system based on reconstruction, characterized in that it includes: an acquisition module, used to acquire a video sequence to be detected, perform preprocessing, and obtain an original image; a reconstruction module, used to input the original image into a trained reconstruction model to obtain a final reconstructed image, calculate a reconstruction error based on the original image and the final reconstructed image, and judge whether there is an anomaly in the video sequence to be detected according to the reconstruction error; wherein the reconstruction model includes an improved variational autoencoder and a reconstruction supplement module, the improved variational autoencoder adds an FSQ quantization layer and a jump connection between the encoder and the decoder to discretize data and capture contextual features to obtain a preliminary reconstructed frame; the reconstruction supplement module is used to extract abnormal features to construct an offset map; the preliminary reconstructed frame and the offset map are merged into a final reconstructed frame. 9 .A computer-readable storage medium having a computer program stored thereon, characterized in that when the program is executed by a processor, the steps in a reconstruction-based video anomaly detection method as described in any one of claims 1-7 are implemented. 10 . A computer device, comprising a memory, a processor, and a computer program stored in the memory and executable on the processor, wherein when the processor executes the program, the steps of a reconstruction-based video anomaly detection method as described in any one of claims 1 to 7 are implemented.

title: A METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR ABNORMAL DETECTION OF ROADSIDE TRAFFIC SAFETY AND GREENING FACILITIES; 一种路侧交安、绿化设施异常检测方法及系统
abstract:
本发明涉及一种路侧交安、绿化设施异常检测方法及系统，其中路侧交安、绿化设施异常检测方法，包括以下步骤：云台监控相机从初始的预设位置出发，采集图像，采集的图像经过深度学习模型，生成相机运动控制指令，指导所述云台监控相机进行下一步的动作，使所述云台监控相机可以自动对路侧区域进行巡视，同时，巡视中采集的图像将经传统机器视觉或深度学习模型分析，判断是否出现异常状况，若出现异常状况将进行信息上报至数据后台。其利用机器视觉及深度学习方法，在设置基础预置位的基础上，自动控制云台相机转动、拉伸镜头远近，实现对路侧交安设施、绿化设施图像采集，同时利用深度学习算法，自动判定交安设施、绿化设施是否出现异常状况。

The present invention relates to a method and system for abnormal detection of roadside traffic safety and greening facilities, wherein the method for detecting abnormality of roadside traffic safety and greening facilities comprises the following steps: the pan-tilt monitoring camera starts from an initial preset position, collects images, collects The images of the pan-tilt monitoring camera are generated through a deep learning model to generate camera motion control instructions to guide the pan-tilt monitoring camera to perform the next action, so that the pan-tilt monitoring camera can automatically patrol the roadside area. At the same time, the images collected during the patrol will be Through the analysis of traditional machine vision or deep learning model, it is judged whether there is an abnormal situation. If there is an abnormal situation, the information will be reported to the data background. It uses machine vision and deep learning methods to automatically control the rotation of the pan-tilt camera and stretch the distance of the lens on the basis of setting the basic preset position, so as to realize the image collection of traffic safety facilities and greening facilities on the roadside. At the same time, it uses deep learning algorithms to Automatically determine whether traffic safety facilities and greening facilities are abnormal.

title: APPARATUS FOR INSPECTING DEFECT AND METHOD THEREOF; 불량 검사 장치 및 방법
abstract:
불량 검사 장치 및 방법이 제공된다. 본 발명의 일 실시예에 따른 불량 검사 장치는, 검사 대상 물체의 영상을 획득하는 영상 획득부; 상기 획득된 영상에서 상기 검사 대상 물체의 윤곽선을 추출하는 윤곽선 추출부; 상기 추출된 윤곽선에 속하는 윤곽 픽셀들의 중심 간의 거리 정보 및 각도 정보를 산출하는 정보 산출부; 및 기준 영상 중 물체의 윤곽선에 속하는 윤곽 픽셀들의 중심 간의 거리 정보 및 각도 정보, 상기 산출된 거리 정보 및 각도 정보와 비교하여 상기 검사 대상 물체의 불량 여부를 판단하는 불량 판단부를 포함한다.

A defect inspection apparatus and method are provided. A defect inspection apparatus according to an embodiment of the present invention includes: an image acquisition unit configured to acquire an image of an inspection target object; a contour extracting unit for extracting a contour of the object to be inspected from the obtained image; an information calculating unit for calculating distance information and angle information between centers of contour pixels belonging to the extracted contour line; and a defect determination unit configured to determine whether the object to be inspected is defective by comparing the distance information and angle information between centers of the contour pixels belonging to the contour line of the reference image, and the calculated distance information and angle information.
claims:
An image acquiring unit acquiring an image of an object to be inspected; An outline extracting unit for extracting an outline of the inspection object from the acquired image; An information calculation unit for calculating distance information and angle information between centers of contour pixels belonging to the extracted contour; And a defect judging unit for judging whether or not the inspection object is defective by comparing the distance information and the angle information between the center of contour pixels belonging to the outline of the object in the reference image and the calculated distance information and angle information, . 2. The image processing apparatus according to claim 1, wherein the information calculation unit extracts a first contour pixel, a second contour pixel and a third contour pixel from the extracted contour, and calculates a difference between a center of the first contour pixel and a center of the second contour pixel Distance, the distance between the center of the second contour pixel and the center of the third contour pixel, and the distance between the center of the first contour pixel and the center of the third contour pixel, Wherein the angle information is calculated through an angle formed by the center of the first contour pixel and the center of the third contour pixel with respect to the center of the pixel. The method of claim 1, wherein the failure determination unit determines that the distance between the centers of the contour pixels included in the calculated distance information and the distances between the centers of the contour pixels included in the distance information of the reference image is equal to or greater than a threshold value And judging that the inspection object is defective when the difference between the angle between the center of the contour pixels included in the calculated angle information and the angle between the center of the contour pixels included in the angle information of the reference image is equal to or greater than a threshold value, Device. Acquiring an image of an object to be inspected; Extracting a contour of the inspection object from the acquired image; Calculating distance information and angle information between centers of contour pixels belonging to the extracted contour; And judging whether or not the object to be inspected is defective by comparing distance information and angle information between the center of contour pixels belonging to the outline of the object in the reference image with the calculated distance information and angle information, . 5. The method according to claim 4, wherein the step of calculating the distance information and the angle information comprises: extracting a first contour pixel, a second contour pixel and a third contour pixel of the extracted contour, The distance between the center of the second contour pixel, the distance between the center of the second contour pixel and the center of the third contour pixel, and the distance between the center of the first contour pixel and the center of the third contour pixel, And calculating the angle information through an angle formed by the center of the first contour pixel and the center of the third contour pixel with respect to the center of the second contour pixel. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the step of determining whether the object is defective includes the step of determining whether a distance between centers of contour pixels included in the calculated distance information and a center distance of contour pixels included in the distance information of the reference image When the difference is equal to or larger than the threshold value or when the difference between the center between the centers of the contour pixels included in the calculated angle information and the center of the contour pixels included in the angle information of the reference image is equal to or larger than the threshold value, And judging that the defect is a defect.

검사 대상 물체의 영상을 획득하는 영상 획득부; 상기 획득된 영상에서 상기 검사 대상 물체의 윤곽선을 추출하는 윤곽선 추출부; 상기 추출된 윤곽선에 속하는 윤곽 픽셀들의 중심 간의 거리 정보 및 각도 정보를 산출하는 정보 산출부; 및 기준 영상 중 물체의 윤곽선에 속하는 윤곽 픽셀들의 중심 간의 거리 정보 및 각도 정보, 상기 산출된 거리 정보 및 각도 정보와 비교하여 상기 검사 대상 물체의 불량 여부를 판단하는 불량 판단부를 포함하는, 불량 검사 장치. 제 1 항에 있어서, 상기 정보 산출부는, 상기 추출된 윤곽선에서 제1 윤곽 픽셀, 제2 윤곽 픽셀 및 제3 윤곽 픽셀을 추출하고, 상기 제1 윤곽 픽셀의 중심과 상기 제2 윤곽 픽셀의 중심간의 거리, 상기 제2 윤곽 픽셀의 중심과 상기 제3 윤곽 픽셀의 중심간의 거리 및 상기 제1 윤곽 픽셀의 중심과 상기 제3 윤곽 픽셀의 중심간의 거리를 통해 상기 거리 정보를 산출하며, 상기 제2 윤곽 픽셀의 중심을 기준으로 상기 제1 윤곽 픽셀의 중심과 상기 제3 윤곽 픽셀의 중심이 이루는 각도를 통해 상기 각도 정보를 산출하는, 불량 검사 장치. 제 1 항에 있어서, 상기 불량 판단부는, 상기 산출된 거리 정보에 포함된 윤곽 픽셀들의 중심 간의 거리와 상기 기준 영상의 거리 정보에 포함된 윤곽 픽셀들의 중심 간의 거리의 차이가 임계값 이상이 경우 또는 상기 산출된 각도 정보에 포함된 윤곽 픽셀들의 중심 간의 각도와 상기 기준 영상의 각도 정보에 포함된 윤곽 픽셀들의 중심 간의 각도의 차이가 임계값 이상인 경우에 상기 검사 대상 물체를 불량으로 판단하는, 불량 검사 장치. 검사 대상 물체의 영상을 획득하는 단계; 상기 획득된 영상에서 상기 검사 대상 물체의 윤곽선을 추출하는 단계; 상기 추출된 윤곽선에 속하는 윤곽 픽셀들의 중심 간의 거리 정보 및 각도 정보를 산출하는 단계; 및 기준 영상 중 물체의 윤곽선에 속하는 윤곽 픽셀들의 중심 간의 거리 정보 및 각도 정보를, 상기 산출된 거리 정보 및 각도 정보와 비교하여 상기 검사 대상 물체의 불량 여부를 판단하는 단계를 포함하는, 불량 검사 방법. 제 4 항에 있어서, 상기 거리 정보 및 각도 정보를 산출하는 단계는, 상기 추출된 윤곽선 중 제1 윤곽 픽셀, 제2 윤곽 픽셀 및 제3 윤곽 픽셀을 추출하고, 상기 제1 윤곽 픽셀의 중심과 상기 제2 윤곽 픽셀의 중심간의 거리, 상기 제2 윤곽 픽셀의 중심과 상기 제3 윤곽 픽셀의 중심간의 거리 및 상기 제1 윤곽 픽셀의 중심과 상기 제3 윤곽 픽셀의 중심간의 거리를 통해 상기 거리 정보를 산출하며, 상기 제2 윤곽 픽셀의 중심을 기준으로 상기 제1 윤곽 픽셀의 중심과 상기 제3 윤곽 픽셀의 중심이 이루는 각도를 통해 상기 각도 정보를 산출하는 단계를 포함하는, 불량 검사 방법. 제 4 항에 있어서, 상기 검사 대상 물체의 불량 여부를 판단하는 단계는, 상기 산출된 거리 정보에 포함된 윤곽 픽셀들의 중심 간의 거리와 상기 기준 영상의 거리 정보에 포함된 윤곽 픽셀들의 중심 간의 거리의 차이가 임계값 이상이 경우 또는 상기 산출된 각도 정보에 포함된 윤곽 픽셀들의 중심 간의 각도와 상기 기준 영상의 각도 정보에 포함된 윤곽 픽셀들의 중심 간의 차이가 임계값 이상인 경우에 상기 검사 대상 물체를 불량으로 판단하는 단계를 포함하는, 불량 검사 방법.
problem_context: 불량 검사 장치 및 방법이 제공된다.
solution_context: A defect inspection apparatus and method are provided. A defect inspection apparatus according to an embodiment of the present invention includes: an image acquisition unit configured to acquire an image of an inspection target object; a contour extracting unit for extracting a contour of the object to be inspected from the obtained image; an information calculating unit for calculating distance information and angle information between centers of contour pixels belonging to the extracted contour line; and a defect determination unit configured to determine whether the object to be inspected is defective by comparing the distance information and angle information between centers of the contour pixels belonging to the contour line of the reference image, and the calculated distance information and angle information. The image processing apparatus according to claim 1, wherein the information calculation unit extracts a first contour pixel, a second contour pixel and a third contour pixel from the extracted contour, and calculates a difference between a center of the first contour pixel and a center of the second contour pixel Distance, the distance between the center of the second contour pixel and the center of the third contour pixel, and the distance between the center of the first contour pixel and the center of the third contour pixel, Wherein the angle information is calculated through an angle formed by the center of the first contour pixel and the center of the third contour pixel with respect to the center of the pixel.
technical_means: 検査装置; 画像処理システム
method_name: 検査装置; 画像処理システム
llm_reviewer_comment_short: 製造物や設備の欠陥・異常検出を画像処理で扱っており、専門家確認候補として強い。
llm_reviewer_comment_detail: 課題文脈は一部使えるが、目的や問題点の明確さに不足がある。解決手段文脈は処理手順やモデル構成を読み取れる。技術手段は関連するが一般語・抽象語が中心である。製造欠陥検出・画像検査への対応が明確で、代表特許候補として説明しやすい。
human_reviewer_comment: 検査対象物の画像から輪郭情報を用いて不良判定する装置であり、外観検査候補として強い。

title: A METHOD FOR CONFIRMING QUALITY ABNORMALITIES IN A TOBACCO PACKAGING WORKSHOP; 一种烟草卷包车间质量异常的确认方法
abstract:
提供一种烟草卷包车间质量异常的确认方法，属于质量检测技术领域。该方法基于质量异常检测系统实现，包括以下步骤：S1.生产线传感器数据收集；S2.对收集到的数据进行数据预处理；S3.进行实时数据分析并建立警报系统；S4.进行质量预测；S5.首先进行图片采集，并将采集到的图片数据建立数据集，通过质量不同的图片信息进行训练，然后进行质量异常比对、检测以及根因分析，进行智能比对诊断；S6.反馈与调整；S7.提供数据管理支持；S8.设计可视化用户交互界面。本发明实现对烟草生产过程中质量异常的智能比对诊断，减少对人力的依赖，实时采集图像数据，通过机器学习模型进行分析，及时发现和诊断生产过程中的质量异常。

A method for confirming quality anomalies in a tobacco packaging workshop is provided, which belongs to the field of quality detection technology. The method is implemented based on a quality anomaly detection system and includes the following steps: S1. Data collection of production line sensors; S2. Data preprocessing of the collected data; S3. Real-time data analysis and establishment of an alarm system; S4. Quality prediction; S5. First, image acquisition is performed, and a data set is established with the acquired image data, and training is performed through image information of different qualities, and then quality anomaly comparison, detection and root cause analysis are performed, and intelligent comparison and diagnosis are performed; S6. Feedback and adjustment; S7. Data management support is provided; S8. A visual user interaction interface is designed. The present invention realizes intelligent comparison and diagnosis of quality anomalies in the tobacco production process, reduces dependence on manpower, acquires image data in real time, and analyzes through a machine learning model to timely discover and diagnose quality anomalies in the production process.
claims:
1 . 一种烟草卷包车间质量异常的确认方法，其特征在于:该方法基于质量异常检测系统实现，包括以下步骤： S1 .生产线传感器数据收集； S2 .对收集到的数据进行数据预处理； S3 .进行实时数据分析并建立警报系统； S4 .进行质量预测； S5 .首先进行图片采集，并将采集到的图片数据建立数据集，通过质量不同的图片信息进行训练，然后进行质量异常比对、检测以及根因分析，进行智能比对诊断； S6 .反馈与调整； S7 .提供数据管理支持； S8 .设计可视化用户交互界面。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的一种烟草卷包车间质量异常的确认方法，其特征在于:所述质量异常检测系统，包括数据收集模块、数据预处理模块、实时监控模块、质量预测模块、质量异常诊断模块、反馈与调整模块、数据管理模块以及用户界面模块： 所述数据收集模块用于传感器以及人工输入数据的收集； 所述数据预处理模块用于数据清洗； 所述实时监控模块用于实时数据分析和警报生成； 所述质量预测模块用于进行机器学习预测和深度学习增强； 所述质量异常诊断模块用于烟草质量异常诊断和分析； 所述反馈与调整模块用于反馈收集和生产调整； 所述数据管理模块用于数据库管理、分析与报告； 所述用户界面模块用于建立可交互可视化用户界面。 3 .根据权利要求2所述的一种烟草卷包车间质量异常的确认方法，其特征在于:所述数据收集模块还包括传感器数据收集单元和人工输入数据单元； 所述传感器数据收集单元为部署在生产线上的传感器，包括温度、湿度、重量以及图像传感器，收集实时数据，用于后续的数据分析和质量评估； 所述人工输入数据单元提供供操作员输入非自动化检测的质量控制信息。 4 .根据权利要求3所述的一种烟草卷包车间质量异常的确认方法，其特征在于:所述数据预处理模块还包括数据清洗单元和特征工程单元； 所述数据清洗单元对收集到的数据进行清洗，包括去除噪声、处理缺失值，以确保数据质量； 所述特征工程单元从清洗后的数据中提取重要特征，为模型训练和质量异常检测提供输入。 5 .根据权利要求4所述的一种烟草卷包车间质量异常的确认方法，其特征在于:所述实时监控模块还包括实时数据分析单元和警报单元； 所述实时数据分析单元使用统计分析和机器学习算法对实时数据进行分析，快速识别质量偏差； 所述警报单元当检测到潜在的质量问题时，自动触发警报，通知操作员和管理人员进行进一·步检查。 6 .根据权利要求5所述的一种烟草卷包车间质量异常的确认方法，其特征在于:所述质量预测模块还包括机器学习预测单元和深度学习增强单元； 所述机器学习预测单元利用历史数据训练机器学习模型，预测产品质量，及早识别可能的质量下降趋势； 所述深度学习增强单元采用卷积神经网络CNN对于图像数据的分析，对复杂模式进行识别，提高预测的准确性。 7 .根据权利要求6所述的一种烟草卷包车间质量异常的确认方法，其特征在于:所述质量异常诊断模块还包括图片采集单元、数据集建立单元、质量异常比对单元、异常检测单元和根因分析单元； 所述图片采集单元负责采集生产过程中的图像数据，并将其传输至数据集建立单元； 所述数据集建立单元建立用于机器视觉分析的数据集，对图像数据进行标注和准备； 所述质量异常比对单元使用机器学习和图像处理技术，对图像数据进行分析和比对， 识别质量异常情况； 所述异常检测单元使用自编码器和孤立森林异常检测算法自动识别生产过程中的异常点； 所述根因分析单元结合机器学习与图片数据集，分析异常的原因，辅助决策制定和问题解决。 8 .根据权利要求7所述的一种烟草卷包车间质量异常的确认方法，其特征在于:所述反馈与调整模块还包括反馈收集单元和生产调整单元； 所述反馈收集单元收集来自操作员和质量检查人员的反馈信息，用于模型的持续改进和调整； 所述生产调整单元根据质量预测和诊断结果，自动或手动调整生产参数，以优化产品质量。 9 .根据权利要求8所述的一种烟草卷包车间质量异常的确认方法，其特征在于:所述数据管理模块还包括数据库管理单元和数据分析与报告单元； 所述数据库管理单元负责存储所有收集的数据、模型预测结果和反馈信息，支持数据的查询、备份和恢复； 所述数据分析与报告单元提供定期和实时的数据分析报告，帮助管理层监控生产质量并做出决策。 10 .根据权利要求9所述的一种烟草卷包车间质量异常的确认方法，其特征在于:所述用户界面模块还包括监控仪表板单元和操作员交互单元； 所述监控仪表板单元提供一个直观的用户界面，展示实时数据、警报信息、质量趋势关键信息； 所述操作员交互单元允许操作员输入数据、接收警报、查看诊断结果和建议，以及调整生产参数。

title: A SMART SCHOOL BUS SYSTEM AND DEVICE BASED ON ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE; 一种基于人工智能的智慧校车系统及装置
abstract:
本发明提供一种基于人工智能的智慧校车系统及装置，涉及校车安全领域，包括：安全路线规划模块，其配置得到规划路径；司机行为监督模块，其配置为对校车在行驶过程中的安全进行实时检测；学生安全管理模块，其配置为通过人脸识别、目标检测和语音播报进行学生安全管理，包括利用人脸识别及语音播报进行安全教育和人员点名、采用目标检测识别学生的危险行为；智能停止臂模块，其配置为在停止臂伸出时，实时检测进入校车安全区域内的车辆，对异常移动的车辆进行检测，记录车牌并上报至管理部门。本发明能够为校车工作人员的校车驾驶工作以及学生安全管理提供便捷。在降低校车安全方面人力消耗同时有效保障了学生上下学过程的安全。

The invention provides a smart school bus system and device based on artificial intelligence, which relates to the field of school bus safety and includes: a safe route planning module configured to obtain a planned path; a driver behavior monitoring module configured to monitor the safety of the school bus during driving. Real-time detection; student safety management module, which is configured to conduct student safety management through face recognition, target detection and voice broadcasting, including using face recognition and voice broadcasting to conduct safety education and personnel roll-call, and using target detection to identify students' dangerous behaviors; The intelligent stop arm module is configured to detect vehicles entering the school bus safety area in real time when the stop arm is extended, detect abnormally moving vehicles, record the license plate and report it to the management department. The invention can provide convenience for the school bus driving work of school bus staff and the safety management of students. It reduces manpower consumption in terms of school bus safety while effectively ensuring the safety of students going to and from school.

problem_context: 本发明提供一种基于人工智能的智慧校车系统及装置，涉及校车安全领域，包括：安全路线规划模块，其配置得到规划路径；司机行为监督模块，其配置为对校车在行驶过程中的安全进行实时检测；学生安全管理模块，其配置为通过人脸识别、目标检测和语音播报进行学生安全管理，包括利用人脸识别及语音播报进行安全教育和人员点名、采用目标检测识别学生的危险行为；智能停止臂模块，其配置为在停止臂伸出时，实时检测进入校车安全区域内的车辆，对异常移动的车辆进行检测，记录车牌并上报至管理部门。

title: QUALITATIVE OR QUANTITATIVE CHARACTERIZATION OF A COATING SURFACE; CARACTÉRISATION QUALITATIVE OU QUANTITATIVE D'UNE SURFACE DE REVÊTEMENT; QUALITATIVE ODER QUANTITATIVE CHARAKTERISIERUNG EINER BESCHICHTUNGSOBERFLÄCHE
claims:
A method for qualitative and/or quantitative characterization of a coating surface, the method comprising: - providing a defects-identification program (124) configured to recognize coating surface defect types (1204, 1206, 1306, 1308), the defects-identification program comprising a predictive model having been trained on training images (604, 606) acquired within a predefined distance range and/or within a predefined image acquisition angle range relative to coating surfaces depicted in the training images, - providing a data storage medium wherein each of multiple coating surface defect types is stored in association with a predefined distance range of distances of at least one camera and coating surface and/or is stored in association with one or more predefined image acquisition angle ranges, the predefined image acquisition angle ranges and/or distance ranges stored in association with a particular defect type being angles ranges and/or distances ranges allowing the acquisition of a digital image of a coating surface that enables the defect identification program to identify said defect type in a digital image of a coating surface; - determining at least one coating surface defect type to be identified; for each of the at least one determined coating surface defect types to be identified: - automatically identifying the one of the predefined distance ranges and/or the one of the one or more predefined image acquisition angle ranges which is stored in association with the determined defect type; - determining, by the defects-identification program, whether at least one camera (134) operatively coupled to the defects-identification program is positioned within the predefined distance range and/or within the predefined image acquisition angle range relative to a currently presented coating surface, whereby the defects-identification program uses the identified predefined distance range and/or the identified predefined image acquisition angle range for the determination whether the camera is positioned within the predefined distance range or image acquisition angle range; in dependence on the result of the determination: o generating a feedback signal whether adjustment of the position of the at least one camera is required such that the camera is within the predefined distance range and/or within the predefined image acquisition angle range; and/or o automatically adjusting the relative distance of the at least one camera and the coating surface such that the camera position is within the predefined distance range from the coating surface and/or automatically adjusting the angle of the at least one camera such that the camera position is within the predefined image acquisition angle range; - enabling the camera to acquire a digital image (604, 606, 1202) of the presented coating surface only in case the camera is within the predefined distance range and/or image acquisition angle range; - processing (102) the digital image (604, 606, 1202) of the coating surface (162-168) by the defect-identification program for recognizing coating surface defects; and - outputting (104) a characterization of the coating surface by the defect-identification program, the characterization being computed as a function of the recognized coating surface defects. The method of claim 1, comprising: - calculating (106), by the defect-identification program, a measure (632, 634, 1402) for the recognized defects; wherein the characterization of the coating surface is computed as a function of the qualitative and/or quantitative characterization of the measure. The method of claim 2, - the measure being a quantitative measure selected from a group comprising: the area of the defect, the number of bubbles or depressions observed in the digital image (604, 606, 1202), the maximum, minimum and/or average size of the bubbles or depressions in the digital image (604, 606, 1202); and/or - the measure being a qualitative measure, the qualitative measure being in particular the type of the defect selected from a group comprising a cratering defect, an abrasion defect, an adhesion failure defect, an alligatoring defect, a bleeding defect, a blistering defect, a bloom defect, a bridging defect, a bubbling defect, a cathodic disbanding defect, a checking defect, a cissing defect, a cobwebbing defect, a cracking defect, a crazing defect, a crowsfooting defect, a delamination defect, a fading defect, a flaking defect, a grinning defect, a heat defect, an impact defect, an intercoat contamination defect, a mud cracking defect, an orange peeling defect, a peeling defect, a pinholes defect, a rippled coating defect, a runs defect, a rust rashing defect, a rust spotting defect, a rust staining defect, a sags defect, a settlement defect, a skinning defect, a solvent lifting defect, a solvent popping defect, a stress cracking defect, an undercutting defect, a wrinkling defect. The method of any one of the previous claims, wherein the predefined distance range is the range of distances between a presented coating surface and the at least one camera which enables the camera to acquire an image having a resolution of at least a predefined minimum resolution, wherein the predefined minimum resolution is in particular a coating-defect-type-specific minimum resolution. The method of any one of the previous claims, - the automatically adjusting of the relative distance of the at least one camera (134) to the coating surface being performed by automatically modifying the position of the at least one camera and/or by automatically modifying the position of a carrier comprising a sample with the coating surface such that that the distance between the at least one camera and the coating surface lies within the predefined distance range; and/or - the automatically adjusting of the image acquisition angle angles (1504) relative to the coating surface comprising modifying the orientation of the at least one camera such that it is oriented towards the coating surface in an image acquisition angle lying within the predefined image acquisition angle range. The method of any one of the previous claims, - wherein at least one of the one or more coating surface defect types is stored in association with a predefined illumination angle range, the predefined illumination angle range stored in association with a particular defect type being a range of an illumination angle of a light source relative to a coating surface allowing the acquisition of a digital image of a coating surface that enables the defect identification program to identify said defect type in a digital image of the coating surface; for each of the at least one determined coating surface defect types to be identified: - automatically identifying the one of the predefined illumination angle ranges stored in association with the determined defect type; - using, by the defects-identification program, the identified predefined illumination angle range for determining whether one or more light sources are positioned relative to the coating surface such that the illumination angle lies within the identified predefined illumination angle range; and - if the illumination angle of the one or more light sources lies outside of the identified predefined illumination angle range, o positioning the one or more light sources (160) and the coating surface relative to each other such that the illumination angle lies within the identified predefined illumination angle range; and/or o generating a feedback signal indicating that and/or how adjustment of the positioning of the one or more light sources (160) and the coating surface relative to each other can be adapted such that the illumination angle lies within the identified predefined illumination angle range. The method of any one of the previous claims, further comprising: - after the positioning of the coating surface, the at least one camera and/or the one or more light sources relative to each other, using the at least one camera for acquiring the digital image of the coating surface. The method of any one of the previous claims, the processing of the digital image further comprising: - performing, by the defect-identification program, a classification of the digital image in respect to the type and/or amount of surface defects depicted therein and/or a semantic segmentation of the image based on one or more surface defect types depicted therein and/or an object detection of defect instances in the image and/or an instance segmentation of the image, thereby automatically assigning one or more labels to the whole digital image, to image regions and/or to individual pixels, each label being indicative of the type of defect identified in the digital image; and - outputting the one or more assigned labels. The method of any one of the previous claims, the defects-identification program being selected from a group comprising: - an application program installed on a stationary or portable data processing system (130), in particular a portable telecommunication device, e.g. a smartphone; - an application program installed on a portable or stationary device (150) specially designed for quality control of coating surfaces; - an application program installed on a high-throughput facility (244) for the automated or semi-automated manufacturing and/or testing of coatings; - a web application downloaded and/or instantiated via a network; - a program executed within a browser, e.g. a JavaScript program; - a server program instantiated on a server computer, the server program being operatively coupled via a network connection to a client program instantiated on a client data processing system, the client program in particular being configured for acquiring the digital images and providing the images via the network to the server program and/or for displaying the results provided by the server program. The method of any one of the previous claims, the predictive model (M1) having learned from training data (602) comprising the training images (604, 606) in a training step performed by a machine learning program configured to recognize patterns in digital images, the machine learning program being in particular a neural network. The method of any one of the previous claims, further comprising: - generating the predictive model by performing a training step on training data (602) comprising the training images, the training images comprising labels, the labels (616, 618) identifying the locations and/or types of defects in the coating surfaces depicted in the training images, the predictive model being trained for recognizing the defect types by means of the labeled training images using back propagation. The method of any one of the previous claims, wherein each of the training images has assigned additional data (608, 610) being processed in the training step for enabling the predictive model (M1) to correlate the additional data with the defect types, the additional data comprising: - a quantitative measure of one or more defects depicted in the training image, e.g. the size and/or severity of the defect or the number of bubbles; - optionally also parameters selected from a group comprising: o an indication of one or more components of the coating used for generating the coating surface depicting in the training image; o an indication of an absolute or relative amount of one or more of the components of the coating composition; and/or ∘ one or more manufacturing-process parameters, the manufacturing-process parameters characterizing a process of generating a coating composition, the process parameters for example comprising mixing speed and/or mixing duration of the coating composition; and/or ∘ one or more application-process parameters, the application-process parameters characterizing a process of applying a coating composition on a substrate, the application-process parameters in particular comprising the amount of coating composition applied per area of the coating surface, the type of substrate and/or or the type of application devices; and/or o system parameters of an imaging system used for acquiring the training images, the system parameters being selected from a group comprising type of light source(s) used for illuminating the coating surface, brightness of the light source(s), illumination angle, wavelength of the light source(s), type of one or more cameras used for acquiring the digital image of the coating surface, image acquisition angle(s), position(s) of the one or more camera(s). The method of claim 12, wherein the system parameters comprise at least the illumination angle, the image acquisition angle of the at least one camera and/or the relative distance of the at least one camera and the coating surface. A computer system (224) for qualitative and/or quantitative characterization of a coating surface, the computer system comprising: - a defects-identification program (124), the defect-identification program being operatively coupled to at least one camera (134) and being configured to recognize coating surface defect types (1204, 1206, 1306, 1308), the defects-identification program comprising a predictive model having been trained on training images (604, 606) acquired within a predefined distance range and/or within a predefined image acquisition angle range relative to coating surfaces depicted in the training images; - a data storage medium wherein each of multiple coating surface defect types is stored in association with a predefined distance range of distances of at least one camera and coating surface and/or is stored in association with one or more predefined image acquisition angle ranges, the predefined image acquisition angle ranges and/or distance ranges stored in association with a particular defect type being angles ranges and/or distances ranges allowing the acquisition of a digital image of a coating surface that enables the defect identification program to identify said defect type in a digital image of a coating surface; wherein the defects-identification program is configured for: - determining at least one coating surface defect type to be identified; for each of the at least one determined coating surface defect types to be identified: - automatically identifying the one of the predefined distance ranges and/or the one of the one or more predefined image acquisition angle ranges which is stored in association with the determined defect type; - determining whether the at least one camera is positioned within the predefined distance range and/or within the predefined image acquisition angle range relative to a currently presented coating surface, whereby the defects-identification program uses the identified predefined distance range and/or the identified predefined image acquisition angle range for the determination whether the camera is positioned within the predefined distance range or image acquisition angle range; - in dependence on the result of the determination: o generating a feedback signal whether adjustment of the position of the at least one camera is required such that the camera is within the predefined distance range and/or within the predefined image acquisition angle range; and/or o automatically adjusting the relative distance of the at least one camera and the coating surface such that the camera position is within the predefined distance range from the coating surface and/or automatically adjusting the angle of the at least one camera such that the camera position is within the predefined image acquisition angle range; - enabling the at least one camera to acquire a digital image of the presented coating surface only in case the camera is within the predefined distance range and/or image acquisition angle range; - processing (102) the digital image (604, 606, 1202) acquired from the enabled at least one camera in order to recognize one or more of the defect types; and - outputting (104) a characterization of the coating surface, the characterization being computed as a function of coating surface defects recognized by the defect-identification program during the processing. A system comprising - the computer system of claim 14; - the at least one camera; and/or - a facility (244) for testing compositions for paints, varnishes, printing inks, grinding resins, pigment concentrates or other coating materials, where the facility comprises: o at least one workstation configured for applying one or more coating compositions on at least one surface of multiple objects, o an automated transport system for transporting the coated objects to the image acquisition and analysis system, - wherein the system is configured to use the image acquisition and analysis system for automatically acquiring images of the coating surfaces of the multiple objects and for outputting a characterization of the coating surfaces.

Verfahren zum qualitativen und/oder quantitativen Charakterisieren einer Beschichtungsoberfläche, das Verfahren umfassend: - Bereitstellen eines Defektidentifizierungsprogramms (124), konfiguriert zum Erkennen von Beschichtungsoberflächendefekttypen (1204, 1206, 1306, 1308), das Defektidentifizierungsprogramm umfassend ein prädiktives Modell, das mit Trainingsbildern (604, 606) trainiert worden ist, erfasst innerhalb eines vordefinierten Abstandsbereichs und/oder innerhalb eines vordefinierten Bilderfassungswinkelbereichs relativ zu in den Trainingsbildern dargestellten Beschichtungsoberflächen, - Bereitstellen eines Datenspeichermediums wobei jeder von mehreren Beschichtungsoberflächendefekttypen in Verbindung mit einem vordefinierten Abstandsbereich zwischen mindestens einer Kamera und einer Beschichtungsoberfläche gespeichert wird und/oder in Verbindung mit einem oder mehreren vordefinierten Bilderfassungswinkelbereichen gespeichert wird, wobei die vordefinierten Bilderfassungswinkelbereiche und/oder Abstandsbereiche, die in Verbindung mit einem bestimmten Defekttyp gespeichert werden, Winkelbereiche und/oder Abstandsbereiche sind, die das Erfassen eines digitalen Bildes einer Beschichtungsoberfläche ermöglichen, was es dem Defektidentifizierungsprogramm ermöglicht, den Defekttyp in einem digitalen Bild einer Beschichtungsoberfläche zu identifizieren; - Bestimmen mindestens eines zu identifizierenden Beschichtungsoberflächendefekttyps; für jeden des mindestens einen bestimmten zu identifizierenden Beschichtungsoberflächendefekttyps: - automatisches Identifizieren des einen der vordefinierten Abstandsbereiche und/oder des einen des einen oder der mehreren vordefinierten Bilderfassungswinkelbereiche, die in Verbindung mit dem bestimmten Defekttyp gespeichert werden; - Bestimmen, durch das Defektidentifizierungsprogramm, ob mindestens eine mit dem Defektidentifizierungsprogramm wirkverbundene Kamera (134) innerhalb des vorbestimmten Abstandsbereichs und/oder innerhalb des vordefinierten Bilderfassungswinkelbereichs in Bezug auf eine gegenwärtig präsentierte Beschichtungsoberfläche angeordnet ist, wobei das Defektidentifizierungsprogramm den identifizierten vordefinierten Abstandsbereich und/oder den identifizierten Bilderfassungswinkelbereich einsetzt, zum Bestimmen, ob die Kamera innerhalb des vorbestimmten Abstandsbereichs oder Bilderfassungswinkelbereichs angeordnet ist; in Abhängigkeit des Ergebnisses der Bestimmung: o Erzeugen eines Feedbacksignals, ob eine Einstellung der Position der mindestens einen Kamera erforderlich ist, sodass die Kamera sich innerhalb des vordefinierten Abstandsbereichs und/oder innerhalb des vordefinierten Bilderfassungswinkelbereichs befindet; und/oder o automatisches Einstellen des relativen Abstands der mindestens einen Kamera und der Beschichtungsoberfläche derart, dass die Kameraposition innerhalb des vordefinierten Abstandsbereichs von der Beschichtungsoberfläche liegt und/oder automatisches Einstellen des Winkels der mindestens einen Kamera derart, dass sich die Kameraposition innerhalb des vordefinierten Bilderfassungswinkelbereichs befindet; - Ermöglichen, dass die Kamera ein digitales Bild (604, 606, 1202) von der vorgestellten Beschichtungsoberfläche nur dann erfasst, wenn die Kamera sich in dem vordefinierten Abstandsbereich und/oder Bilderfassungswinkelbereich befindet; - Verarbeiten (102) des digitalen Bildes (604, 606, 1202) der Beschichtungsoberfläche (162-168) durch das Defektidentifizierungsprogramm zum Erkennen von Beschichtungsoberflächendefekten; und - Ausgeben (104) einer Charakterisierung der Beschichtungsoberfläche durch das Defektidentifizierungsprogramm, wobei die Charakterisierung berechnet wird als eine Funktion der erkannten Beschichtungsoberflächendefekte. Verfahren nach Anspruch 1, umfassend: - Berechnen (106), durch das Defektidentifizierungsprogramm, eines Maßes (632, 634, 1402) für die erkannten Defekte; wobei die Charakterisierung der Beschichtungsoberfläche berechnet wird als eine Funktion der qualitativen und/oder quantitativen Charakterisierung des Maßes. Verfahren nach Anspruch 2, - wobei das Maß ein quantitatives Maß ist, ausgewählt aus einer Gruppe, umfassend: die Fläche des Defekts, die Anzahl von in dem digitalen Bild (604, 606, 1202) beobachteten Blasen oder Vertiefungen, die maximale, minimale und/oder durchschnittliche Größe der Blasen oder Vertiefungen in dem digitalen Bild (604, 606, 1202); und/oder - wobei das Maß ein qualitatives Maß ist, wobei das qualitative Maß insbesondere der Typ des Defekts ist, ausgewählt aus einer Gruppe, umfassend einen Kraterbildungsdefekt, einen Abrasionsdefekt, einen Haftversagendefekt, einen Alligatordefekt, einen Blutungsdefekt, einen Blasendefekt, einen Ausblühdefekt, einen Brückenbildungsdefekt, einen Blasenbildungsdefekt, einen kathodischen Auflösungsdefekt, einen Rissbildungsdefekt, einen Scherbenbildungsdefekt, einen Spinnwebendefekt, einen Rissdefekt, einen Haarrissbildungsdefekt, einen Krähenfußdefekt, einen Delaminationsdefekt, einen Verblassungsdefekt, einen Abblätterungsdefekt, einen Durchscheindefekt, einen Hitzedefekt, einen Aufpralldefekt, einen Defekt durch Zwischenschichtverunreinigung, einen Schlammrissdefekt, einen Orangenhautdefekt, einen Abschäldefekt, einen Nadellochdefekt, einen gewellten Beschichtungsdefekt, einen Verlaufdefekt, einen Rostausschlagdefekt, einen Rostfleckendefekt, einen Rostverfärbungsdefekt, einen Durchhangdefekt, einen Setzungsdefekt, einen Hautbildungsdefekt, einen Lösungsmittelabhebedefekt, einen Lösungsmittelaufplatzdefekt, einen Spannungsrissdefekt, einen Unterhöhlungsdefekt, einen Faltenbildungsdefekt. Verfahren nach einem der vorhergehenden Ansprüche, wobei der vordefinierte Abstandbereich der Bereich von Abständen zwischen einer vorgestellten Beschichtungsoberfläche und der mindestens einen Kamera ist, der es der Kamera ermöglicht, ein Bild mit einer Auflösung von mindestens einer vordefinierten Mindestauflösung zu erfassen, wobei die vordefinierte Mindestauflösung insbesondere eine für einen Beschichtungsdefekttyp spezifische Mindestauflösung ist. Verfahren nach einem der vorhergehenden Ansprüche, - wobei der mindestens eine des einen oder der mehreren Beschichtungsoberflächendefekttypen in Verbindung mit einem vordefinierten Beleuchtungswinkelbereich gespeichert wird, wobei der in Verbindung mit einem bestimmten Defekttyp gespeicherte vordefinierte Beleuchtungswinkelbereich ein Bereich eines Beleuchtungswinkels einer Lichtquelle in Bezug auf eine Beschichtungsoberfläche ist, der das Erfassen eines digitalen Bildes einer Beschichtungsoberfläche zulässt, was es dem Defektidentifizierungsprogramm ermöglicht, den Defekttyp in einem digitalen Bild der Beschichtungsoberfläche zu identifizieren; für jeden des mindestens einen bestimmten zu identifizierenden Beschichtungsoberflächendefekttyps: - automatisches Identifizieren des einen der in Verbindung mit dem bestimmten Defekttyp gespeicherten vordefinierten Beleuchtungswinkelbereiche; - Verwenden, durch das Defektidentifizierungsprogramm, des identifizierten vordefinierten Beleuchtungswinkelbereichs zum Bestimmen, ob eine oder mehrere Lichtquellen in Bezug auf die Beschichtungsoberfläche derart positioniert sind, dass der Beleuchtungswinkel innerhalb des identifizierten vordefinierten Beleuchtungswinkelbereichs liegt; und - wenn der Beleuchtungswinkel der einen oder der mehreren Lichtquellen außerhalb des identifizierten vordefinierten Beleuchtungswinkelbereichs liegt, o Positionieren der einen oder der mehreren Lichtquellen (160) und der Beschichtungsoberfläche in Bezug aufeinander derart, dass der Beleuchtungswinkel innerhalb des vordefinierten Beleuchtungswinkelbereichs liegt; und/oder o Erzeugen eines Feedbacksignals, das angibt, dass und/oder wie das Einstellen der Positionierung der einen oder der mehreren Lichtquellen (160) und der Beschichtungsoberfläche in Bezug aufeinander derart angepasst werden kann, dass der Beleuchtungswinkel innerhalb des identifizierten vordefinierten Beleuchtungswinkelbereichs liegt. Verfahren nach einem der vorhergehenden Ansprüche, das Verarbeiten des digitalen Bildes ferner umfassend: - Ausführen, durch das Defektidentifizierungsprogramm, einer Klassifizierung des digitalen Bildes in Bezug auf die Art und/oder Menge der darin dargestellten Oberflächendefekttypen und/oder einer semantischen Segmentierung des Bildes basierend auf einem oder mehreren darin dargestellten Oberflächendefekttypen und/oder einer Objekterkennung von Defektinstanzen im Bild und/oder eine Instanzsegmentierung des Bildes, wodurch dem gesamten digitalen Bild, Bildbereichen und/oder einzelnen Pixeln automatisch eine oder mehrere Kennzeichnungen zugewiesen werden, wobei jede Kennzeichnung den im digitalen Bild identifizierten Defekttyp angibt; und - Ausgeben der einen oder der mehreren zugeordneten Kennzeichnungen. Verfahren nach einem der vorhergehenden Ansprüche, wobei das Defektidentifizierungsprogramm ausgewählt ist aus einer Gruppe, umfassend: - ein Anwendungsprogramm, installiert auf einem stationären oder tragbaren Datenverarbeitungssystem (130), insbesondere einer tragbaren Telekommunikationsvorrichtung, z. B. auf einem Smartphone; - ein Anwendungsprogramm, installiert auf einer tragbaren oder stationären Vorrichtung (150), spezifisch gestaltet für Qualitätskontrolle von Beschichtungsoberflächen; - ein Anwendungsprogramm, installiert auf einer Einrichtung mit hohem Durchsatz (244) zur automatischen oder halbautomatischen Fertigung und/oder Prüfung von Beschichtungen; - eine Webanwendung, heruntergeladen und/oder instantiiert über ein Netzwerk; - ein Programm, ausgeführt innerhalb eines Browsers, z.
