title: EXPLAINABILITY IN MACHINE-LEARNING-BASED ANATOMICAL ABNORMALITY DETECTION ALGORITHM; EXPLICABILITÉ DANS UN ALGORITHME DE DÉTECTION D'ANOMALIE ANATOMIQUE BASÉ SUR L'APPRENTISSAGE AUTOMATIQUE; ERKLÄRBARKEIT IN EINEM ALGORITHMUS ZUR ERKENNUNG VON ANATOMISCHEN ANOMALIEN AUF DER BASIS VON MASCHINELLEM LERNEN
abstract: Various examples of the disclosure relate to explain-ability of a machine learning algorithm for detecting at least one abnormality within an anatomical region of interest. A trained machine learning algorithm is used to process one or more images associated with at least one abnormality within an anatomical region of interest to determine one or more reasons for a further trained machine learning algorithm detecting the at least one abnormality in the one or more images.
claims: A computer-implemented method (3000), comprising: - obtaining (3100) one or more images associated with at least one abnormality (2310) within an anatomical region of interest; and, - determining (3200), using a first trained machine learning algorithm (2100) and based on the one or more images (2300), one or more reasons for a second trained machine learning algorithm (2200) detecting the at least one abnormality (2310) in the one or more images (2300). The computer-implemented method (3000) of claim 1, wherein the at least one abnormality (2310) comprises one or more lung nodules and the one or more reasons comprise at least one imaging characteristic associated with the one or more lung nodules. The computer-implemented method (3000) of claim 1 or 2, wherein the one or more images comprise one or more medical images depicting the anatomical region of interest. The computer-implemented method (3000) of claim 3, wherein the one or more images further comprise one or more feature maps (2230) representing the at least one abnormality (2310), and the second trained machine learning algorithm (2200) comprises a first module (2210), the method further comprising: - generating, using the first module (2210) of the second trained machine learning algorithm (2200), the one or more feature maps (2230) based on the one or more medical images. The computer-implemented method (3000) of claim 4, wherein the one or more images (2300) further comprise a segmented image (2400) depicting the at least one abnormality (2310), and the second trained machine learning algorithm (2200) further comprises a second module (2220) connectable to the first module (2210), the method further comprising: - determining, using the second module (2220) of the second trained machine learning algorithm (2200), the segmented image (2400) based on the one or more feature maps (2230). The computer-implemented method (3000) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the one or more reasons comprises at least one of the following: - a respective size of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective location of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective margin morphology of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a count of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective growth rate of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective type of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective shape of each of the at least one abnormality (2310). A computer-implemented method (4000), comprising: - obtaining (4100) one or more training images associated with at least one abnormality (2310) within an anatomical region of interest; - determining (4200), using a first machine learning algorithm and based on the one or more training images, one or more predicted reasons for a second trained machine learning algorithm detecting the at least one abnormality (2310); - updating (4300) parameter values of the first machine learning algorithm based on a comparison between each of the one or more predicted reasons and a corresponding reference reason for the second trained machine learning algorithm detecting the at least one abnormality (2310). The computer-implemented method (4000) of claim 7, wherein the at least one abnormality (2310) comprises one or more lung nodules and the one or more predicted reasons and the corresponding reference reason respectively comprise at least one imaging characteristic associated with the one or more lung nodules. The computer-implemented method (4000) of claim 7 or 8, wherein the one or more training images comprise one or more medical images depicting the anatomical region of interest. The computer-implemented method (4000) of claim 9, wherein the one or more training images further comprise one or more feature maps representing the at least one abnormality (2310), and the second trained machine learning algorithm comprises a first module, the method further comprising: - generating, using the first module of the second trained machine learning algorithm, the one or more feature maps based on the one or more medical images. The computer-implemented method (4000) of claim 10, wherein the one or more training images further comprise a segmented image depicting the at least one abnormality (2310), and the second trained machine learning algorithm further comprises a second module connectable to the first module, the method further comprising: - determining, using the second module of the second trained machine learning algorithm, the segmented image based on the one or more feature maps. The computer-implemented method (4000) of any one of claims 7 to 11, further comprising: - obtaining a set of training images associated with the at least one abnormality (2310), wherein the set of training images comprises the one or more training images; - clustering the set of training images into at least one group of training images based on one or more characteristics associated with the at least one abnormality (2310); and, - determining, for each of the at least one group, at least one common reference reason for the second trained machine learning algorithm detecting the at least one abnormality (2310), based on the respective group of training images, wherein the corresponding reference reason comprises the at least one common reference reason. The computer-implemented method (4000) of any one of claims 7 to 12, wherein the one or more predicted reasons and the corresponding reference reason respectively comprise at least one of the following: - a respective size of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective location of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective margin morphology of each of the at least one abnormality, - a count of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective growth rate of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective type of each of the at least one abnormality (2310), - a respective shape of each of the at least one abnormality (2310). A computing device (9000) comprising a processor (9020) and a memory (9030), wherein upon loading and executing program code from the memory (9030), the processor (9020) is configured to perform the method (3000, 4000) according to any one of the preceding claims. A medical imaging device comprising the computing device (9000) of claim 14.

title: ABNORMALITY DETECTION METHOD, ABNORMALITY DETECTION PROGRAM, ABNORMALITY DETECTION DEVICE, SERVER DEVICE, AND INFORMATION PROCESSING METHOD; PROCÉDÉ DE DÉTECTION D'ANOMALIE, PROGRAMME DE DÉTECTION D'ANOMALIE, DISPOSITIF DE DÉTECTION D'ANOMALIE, DISPOSITIF SERVEUR ET PROCÉDÉ DE TRAITEMENT D'INFORMATIONS; 異常検出方法、異常検出プログラム、異常検出装置、サーバ装置及び情報処理方法
abstract:
Provided is a computer for acquiring a chest X-ray image (S50) and detecting a borderline in the acquired chest X-ray image using a model (S100). The model is constructed using machine learning before detection, sets, on the basis of the borderline, a third lung region that includes a first lung region overlapping a heart and/or a second lung region overlapping a liver in the acquired chest X-ray image (S200), extracts a blood vessel index that represents the size and/or the density of one lung blood vessel present in a region that includes the third lung region (S500), determines, on the basis of the blood vessel index and a reference index that uses an index previously extracted from a region equivalent to the region included in the third lung region of the chest X-ray image in a normal state using a method by which the blood vessel index is extracted, whether or not the region included in the third lung region is in an abnormal state (S700), and outputs information that represents determination when it is determined that the region included in the third lung region is in an abnormal state.

L'invention concerne un ordinateur servant à acquérir une image radiographique thoracique (S50) et détecter une limite dans l'image radiographique thoracique acquise à l'aide d'un modèle (S100). Le modèle est construit à l'aide d'un apprentissage automatique avant la détection, définit, sur la base de la limite, une troisième région pulmonaire qui comprend une première région pulmonaire chevauchant un cœur et/ou une seconde région pulmonaire chevauchant un foie dans l'image radiographique thoracique acquise (S200), extrait un indice de vaisseau sanguin qui représente la taille et/ou la densité d'un vaisseau sanguin pulmonaire présent dans une région qui comprend la troisième région pulmonaire (S500), détermine, sur la base de l'indice de vaisseau sanguin et d'un indice de référence qui utilise un indice précédemment extrait d'une région équivalente à la région comprise dans la troisième région pulmonaire de l'image radiographique thoracique dans un état normal à l'aide d'un procédé par lequel l'indice de vaisseau sanguin est extrait, si la région comprise dans la troisième région pulmonaire est dans un état anormal (S700), et fournit en sortie des informations qui représentent une détermination lorsqu'il est déterminé que la région comprise dans la troisième région pulmonaire est dans un état anormal.

コンピュータが、胸部X線画像を取得し(S50)、取得した胸部X線画像において、境界線を、モデルを用いて検出し(S100)、モデルは検出前に機械学習を用いて構築され、境界線を基に、取得した胸部X線画像において、心臓と重なる第1肺領域および肝臓と重なる第2肺領域の少なくとも一方を含む第3肺領域を設定し(S200)、第3肺領域に含まれる領域に存在する少なくとも1つの肺血管の太さおよび密度の少なくとも一方を表す血管指標を抽出し(S500)、血管指標と、血管指標を抽出した方法を用いて正常状態の胸部X線画像の第3肺領域に含まれる領域に相当する領域から予め抽出された指標を用いた基準指標と、に基づいて第3肺領域に含まれる領域が異常状態であるか否かを判定し(S700)、第3肺領域に含まれる領域が異常状態であると判定されると、判定を表す情報を出力する。

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR QUANTIFYING UNCERTAINTY IN REASONING ABOUT 2D AND 3D SPATIAL FEATURES WITH A COMPUTER MACHINE LEARNING ARCHITECTURE; SYSTÈME ET PROCÉDÉ PERMETTANT DE QUANTIFIER UNE INCERTITUDE DANS UN RAISONNEMENT CONCERNANT DES CARACTÉRISTIQUES SPATIALES 2D ET 3D AVEC UNE ARCHITECTURE D'APPRENTISSAGE MACHINE INFORMATIQUE
abstract:
This invention provides a system and method to propagate uncertainty information in a deep learning pipeline. It allows for the propagation of uncertainty information from one deep learning model to the next by fusing model uncertainty with the original imagery dataset. This approach results in a deep learning architecture where the output of the system contains not only the prediction, but also the model uncertainty information associated with that prediction. The embodiments herein improve upon existing deep learning-based models (CADe models) by providing the model with uncertainty/confidence information associated with (e.g. CADe) decisions. This uncertainty information can be employed in various ways, including (a) transmitting uncertainty from a first stage (or subsystem) of the machine learning system into a next (second) stage (or the next subsystem), and (b) providing uncertainty information to the end user in a manner that characterizes the uncertainty of the overall machine learning model.

La présente invention concerne un système et un procédé permettant de propager des informations d'incertitude dans un pipeline d'apprentissage profond. Elle permet la propagation d'informations d'incertitude d'un modèle d'apprentissage profond au suivant par fusion d'une incertitude de modèle avec l'ensemble de données d'imagerie d'origine. Cette approche conduit à une architecture d'apprentissage profond où la sortie du système contient non seulement la prédiction, mais également les informations d'incertitude de modèle associées à cette prédiction. Les modes de réalisation de la présente invention améliorent les modèles basés sur l'apprentissage profond (modèles CADe) existants en fournissant au modèle des informations d'incertitude/de confiance associées à des décisions (par exemple CADe). Ces informations d'incertitude peuvent être utilisées de diverses manières, comprenant (a) la transmission d'une incertitude d'un premier étage (ou sous-système) du système d'apprentissage machine dans un prochain (second) étage (ou le prochain sous-système), et (b) la fourniture d'informations d'incertitude à l'utilisateur final d'une manière qui caractérise l'incertitude du modèle global d'apprentissage machine.

title: METHOD FOR DETECTING AN ANOMALOUS IMAGE AMONG A FIRST DATASET OF IMAGES USING AN ADVERSARIAL AUTOENCODER
abstract: A method for detecting an anomalous image among a dataset of images using an Adversarial Autoencoder includes training an Adversarial Autoencoder in a first training with a training dataset of images, with the Adversarial Autoencoder being optimized such that a distribution of latent representations of images of the training dataset of images approaches a predetermined prior distribution and that a reconstruction error of reconstructed images of the training dataset of images is minimized. Subsequently, anomalies are detected in the latent representation and the Adversarial Autoencoder is trained in a second training with the training dataset of images, but taking into account the detected anomalies. The anomalous image among the first dataset of images is detected by the trained Adversarial Autoencoder dependent on at least one of the reconstruction error of the image and a probability density under the predetermined prior distribution.

in a second training, training the Adversarial Autoencoder using the training dataset of images and taking into account the detected anomalies; and detecting an anomalous image among a first dataset of images by the trained Adversarial Autoencoder dependent on one or both of the following: a reconstruction error of the anomalous image and a probability density under the predetermined prior distribution; wherein: the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by excluding detected anomalies from the training set for the second training, or the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by using a weighted loss function for tuning the Adversarial Autoencoder in at least one subsequent training, by which weighted loss function anomalies detected in the latent representation receive a reduced weight in the at least one subsequent training, or the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by modifying a reconstruction target for each of a set of the anomalies detected in the latent representation to a noise-corrupted version of itself for at least one subsequent training, or the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by modifying a reconstruction target for each anomaly of a set of the anomalies detected in the latent representation to an image, closest in terms of distance to the anomaly in the latent space, that is identified as not being anomalous in at least one subsequent training. 2. and a reconstruction error of reconstructed images of the training dataset of images is minimized; after the first training, detecting anomalies in the latent representations of images of the training dataset of images; in a second training, training the Adversarial Autoencoder using the training dataset of images and taking into account the detected anomalies; and detecting an anomalous image among a first dataset of images by the trained Adversarial Autoencoder dependent on one or both of the following: a reconstruction error of the anomalous image and a probability density under the predetermined prior distribution; wherein: the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by excluding detected anomalies from the training set for the second training, or the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by using a weighted loss function for tuning the Adversarial Autoencoder in at least one subsequent training, by which weighted loss function anomalies detected in the latent representation receive a reduced weight in the at least one subsequent training, or the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by modifying a reconstruction target for each of a set of the anomalies detected in the latent representation to a noise-corrupted version of itself for at least one subsequent training, or the anomalies detected in the latent representation are taken into account in the second training by modifying a reconstruction target for each anomaly of a set of the anomalies detected in the latent representation to an image, closest in terms of distance to the anomaly in the latent space, that is identified as not being anomalous in at least one subsequent training.

title: FAST AND LOW MEMORY USAGE CONVOLUTIONAL NEURAL NETWORK FOR TOMOSYNTHESIS DATA PROCESSING AND FEATURE DETECTION
abstract: A convolutional neural network (CNN) is employed in an automated feature and/or anomaly detection system for analyzing images provided by X-ray imaging system. The automated detection system operates in a manner that reduces the number of full-resolution CNN convolution layers required in order to speed up the network inference and learning processes for the detection system. To do so, the detection system utilizes as an input a more compact representation of the tomographic data to alleviate the CNN memory footprint and computation time issues in prior art X-ray systems.
claims: 1. A method for detecting an anomaly in one or more images obtained from an X-ray imaging procedure on an object, the method comprising the steps of: a. providing a computing system on which an automatic anomaly detection system is provided, the anomaly detection system comprising: i. a processing unit configured to receive and process projection images, the processing unit including a convolutional neural network; ii. an electronic information storage device operably connected to the processing unit and containing operational instructions for the convolutional neural network; and b. supplying a compact data representation input of the projection images of an object of interest to the automatic anomaly detection system; c. analyzing the compact data representation input with the automatic anomaly detection system in at least one layer of the convolutional neural network to obtain an anomaly detection result; and d. outputting an anomaly detection result from the automatic anomaly detection system, wherein filters for the at least one layer of the convolutional neural network employed on the compact data representation input are at least partially learned by the automatic anomaly detection system. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the at least one layer of the convolutional neural network is one of a filtering operation, a reconstruction operation, a slabbing operation, a 2D synthetic image generation and combinations thereof. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein the compact data representation input is the set of projection images. 4. The method of claim 3 , wherein the step of analyzing the compact data representation input comprises the steps of: a. performing a filtering operation on the set of projection images to form filtered projection images; b. performing a reconstruction operation on the filtered projection images to form filtered slices. 5. The method of claim 3 , wherein the step of analyzing the compact data representation input comprises the steps of: a. performing a filtering operation on the set of projection images to form filtered projection images; b. performing a reconstruction operation on the filtered projection images to form filtered slices; and c. performing a slabbing operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered slabs. 6. The method of claim 3 , wherein the step of analyzing the compact data representation input comprises the steps of: a. performing a filtering operation on the set of projection images to form filtered projection images; b. performing a reconstruction operation on the filtered projection images to form filtered slices; c. performing a slabbing operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered slabs; and d. performing a synthetic 2D image generation operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered 2D synthetic images. 7. The method of claim 6 , further comprising the steps of: a. performing one or more detection operations on the filtered slabs to obtain a first lesion type output; and b. performing one or more detection convolution operations on the filtered 2D synthetic images to obtain a second lesion type output. 8. The method of claim 3 , wherein the step of analyzing the compact data representation input comprises the steps of: a. performing a filtering operation on the set of projection images to form filtered projection images; b. performing a reconstruction operation on the filtered projection images to form filtered slices; and c. performing a synthetic 2D image generation operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered 2D synthetic images. 9. The method of claim 3 , wherein the step of analyzing the compact data representation input further comprises the steps of: a. performing a filtering operation on the set of projection images to form filtered projection images; b. performing a backprojection on the filtered projection images to form a backprojected filtered projection set; c. performing an aggregation operation on the filtered backprojected filtered projection set to form a slice image set; d. performing at least one of: i. a reconstruction operation on the slice image set to form one or more viewable slices, ii. a slabbing operation on the slice image set to form a slab image set and a convolutional operation on the slab image set to form one or more viewable slabs, or iii. combinations thereof; e. performing a detection operation on the slice image set or slab image set to form an anomaly detection result; and f. outputting the one or more viewable slices, the one or more viewable slabs, and combinations thereof in conjunction with the anomaly detection result. 10. The method of claim 1 , wherein the compact data representation input is a set of precomputed synthetic 2D image and further comprises a step of performing a filtering convolution operation on a synthetic 2D images to obtain filtered synthetic 2D images. 11. The method of claim 1 , wherein the compact data representation input is a set of precomputed slices obtained from the projection images. 12. The method of claim 11 , wherein the step of analyzing the compact data representation input comprises the steps of: a. performing a filtering operation on the set of precomputed slices to form filtered slices; and b. performing a slabbing operation on the filtered slices to form filtered slabs. 13. The method of claim 1 , wherein the compact data representation input is a set of precomputed slabs and further comprises a step of performing a filtering operation on the set of slabs to form filtered slabs. 14. An X-ray imaging system comprising: a. an X-ray source configured to emit radiation beams towards an object; b. an X-ray detector movably aligned with the X-ray source to receive the radiation beams from the X-ray source and generate image data; c. a processing unit operably connected to the X-ray source and the X-ray detector to control the movement and operation of the X-ray source and X-ray detector, the processing unit configured to receive and process image data from the X-ray detector to form projection images; d. a display operably connected to the processing unit for presenting information to a user; e. a user interface operably connected to the processing unit to enable user input to the processing unit; f. an automatic anomaly detection system operably connected to the controller; and g. an electronic information storage device operably connected to the processing unit, wherein the automatic anomaly detection system is formed of a convolutional neural network having and operational instructions contained on the storage device to be accessed by the processing unit, the automatic anomaly detection system configured to receive a compact data representation input of the projection images and to analyze the compact data representation input to obtain an anomaly detection result, and wherein filters for the at least one layer of the convolutional neural network employed by the automatic anomaly detection system on the compact data representation are at least partially learned by the automatic anomaly detection system. 15. The method of claim 14 , wherein the at least one layer of the convolutional neural network is one of a filtering operation, a reconstruction operation, a slabbing operation, a 2D synthetic image generation and combinations thereof. 16. The X-ray imaging system of claim 14 , wherein the compact data representation input is the set of projection images. 17. The X-ray imaging system of claim 16 , wherein the automatic anomaly detection system is configured to analyze the compact data representation input by performing a filtering operation on the set of projection images to form filtered projection images, and performing a reconstruction operation on the filtered projection images to form filtered slices. 18. The X-ray imaging system of claim 17 , wherein the automatic anomaly detection system is configured to analyze the compact data representation input by performing a slabbing operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered slabs. 19. The X-ray imaging system of claim 17 , wherein the automatic anomaly detection system is configured to analyze the compact data representation input by performing a synthetic 2D image generation operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered 2D synthetic images. 20. The X-ray imaging system of claim 17 , wherein the automatic anomaly detection system is configured to: a. analyze the compact data representation input by: i. performing a slabbing operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered slabs; and ii. performing a synthetic 2D image generation operation on the filtered slices to obtain filtered 2D synthetic image; and b. perform one or more detection operations on the filtered slabs to obtain a first lesion type output; and c. perform one or more detection operations on the filtered synthetic 2D to obtain a second lesion type output. 21. The X-ray imaging system of claim 16 , wherein the automatic anomaly detection system is configured to: a. perform a filtering operation on the set of projection images to form filtered projection images; b. perform a backprojection on the filtered projection images to form filtered slices; c. perform an aggregation operation on the filtered slices to form an 3D volume image set; d. perform at least one of a reconstruction operation to form a viewable slice volume from the 3D volume image set, and a slabbing operation to form a viewable slab volume from the 3D volume image set; e. perform a detection operation on the 3D volume image set to form an anomaly detection result; and f. output the viewable slices, viewable slabs, and combinations thereof in conjunction with the anomaly detection result on the display.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR TARGET IDENTIFICATION, ELECTRONIC APPARATUS, STORAGE MEDIUM AND NEUROMODULATION APPARATUS
abstract: The present disclosure provides a method and a device for target identification, an electronic apparatus, a storage medium and a neuromodulation apparatus. The method for target identification comprises: acquiring scanning data of a subject, wherein the scanning data comprise the data acquired from magnetic resonance imaging of the brain of the subject; determining at least two regions of interest of the subject based on the scanning data; determining at least one abnormal region of interest in the at least two regions of interest in accordance with a predetermined anomaly detection rule; determining a target based on the at least one abnormal region of interest.

title: METHOD FOR DETECTING ABNORMAL IMAGES IN A FIRST IMAGE DATASET USING ADVERSARIAL AUTOENCODERS; 用对抗自编码器检测第一图像数据集当中异常图像的方法
abstract:
引入了用于使用对抗自编码器（12,14,16）检测第一图像数据集当中的异常图像的方法。在第一训练中利用图像训练数据集（11）来训练对抗自编码器（12,14,16），而优化对抗自编码器（12,14,16）使得图像训练数据集（11）的图像的潜在表示（13）的分布接近预定先验分布（17），并且最小化图像训练数据集的重构图像（15）的重构误差。在对对抗自编码器（12,14,16）的第一训练之后，在潜在表示（13）中检测异常，并且在第二训练中利用图像训练数据集（11）但是把检测到的异常考虑在内来训练对抗自编码器（12,14,16）。由经训练的对抗自编码器（12,14,16）取决于图像的重构误差和预定先验分布（17）下的概率密度中的至少一个来检测第一图像数据集当中的异常图像。

A method for detecting abnormal images in a first image dataset using an adversarial autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is introduced. In a first training, the adversarial autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is trained using the image training dataset (11), and the adversarial autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is optimized so that the distribution of the latent representation (13) of the images of the image training dataset (11) is close to a predetermined prior distribution (17), and the reconstruction error of the reconstructed image (15) of the image training dataset is minimized. After the first training of the adversarial autoencoder (12, 14, 16), anomalies are detected in the latent representation (13), and in a second training, the adversarial autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is trained using the image training dataset (11) but taking the detected anomalies into account. The abnormal images in the first image dataset are detected by the trained adversarial autoencoder (12, 14, 16) depending on at least one of the reconstruction error of the image and the probability density under the predetermined prior distribution (17).
claims:
1 . 一种用于使用对抗自编码器来检测第一图像数据集当中的异常图像的方法，其特征在于： 在第一训练中利用图像训练数据集(11)训练对抗自编码器，而优化对抗自编码器使得图像训练数据集(11)的图像的潜在表示(13)的分布接近预定先验分布(17)，并且最小化图像训练数据集的重构图像(15)的重构误差， 在对对抗自编码器的第一训练之后，在图像训练数据集(11)的图像的潜在表示(13)中检测异常，并且在第二训练中利用图像训练数据集(11)但是把检测到的异常考虑在内来训练对抗自编码器， 由经训练的对抗自编码器取决于图像的重构误差和预定先验分布(17)下的概率密度中的至少一个来检测第一图像数据集当中的异常图像， 其中通过以下方式来把在潜在表示(13)中检测到的异常考虑在内： 从用于第二训练的图像训练数据集(11)中排除检测到的异常，或者在至少一次后续训练中使用用于调谐对抗自编码器的加权损失函数，而在潜在表示 (13)中检测到的异常在所述至少一次后续训练中接收减小的权重，或者对于至少一次后续训练，将在潜在表示(13)中检测到的异常集中每个异常的重构目标修改为其自身的噪声损坏版本，或者在至少一次后续训练中，将在潜在表示(13)中检测到的异常集中每个异常的重构目标修改为潜在空间中标识为没有异常的靠近或最靠近的图像。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其特征在于，潜在表示(13)中的异常的检测是取决于预期异常率来完成的，其中自适应异常检测阈值取决于预期异常率并且历史产出率提供对预期异常率的良好估计。 3 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，潜在表示(13)中的异常的检测是取决于它们的重构误差和预定先验分布(17)下它们的概率密度中的至少一个来完成的。 4 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，潜在表示(13)中的异常的检测是使用一类支持向量机或局部离群因子算法来完成的。 5 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，在一定数量的重复内相继重复地实施第一训练和第二训练，或相继重复地实施第一训练和第二训练直到达到训练目标。 6 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，第一训练包括第一预定数量的迭代。 7 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，第二训练包括第二预定数量的迭代。 8 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，预定先验分布包括至少一个专用拒绝类。 9 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，图像训练数据集(11)的图像最初是未标注的。 10 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，所述方法包括向用户的查询以将呈现的图像标识为正常或异常。 11 .根据权利要求10所述的方法，其特征在于，在第二训练中考虑在内的在潜在表示 (13)中检测到的异常包括用户标识的异常。 12 .根据权利要求10所述的方法，其特征在于，通过使用一类支持向量机或局部离群因子算法来完成潜在表示(13)中的异常的检测，并且而对于一类支持向量机或局部离群因子算法的训练，排除图像训练数据集(11)中用户标识为正常或异常的图像。 13 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，输出针对第一图像数据集中的图像的异常分数。 14 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，输出针对图像训练数据集 (11)中的图像的异常分数。 15 .根据权利要求10所述的方法，其特征在于，呈现给用户的图像是取决于它们的异常分数来选择的。 16 .根据权利要求15所述的方法，其特征在于，基于重构误差和预定先验分布(17)下的概率密度中的至少一个来计算异常分数。 17 .根据权利要求1到2中的一项所述的方法，其特征在于，它用于视觉品质检查、医学图像分析、视觉监视或自动化驾驶中。 18 . 一种计算机程序产品，包括计算机程序，其中计算机程序被处理器执行时适于施行根据权利要求1到16中的一项所述的方法。 19 . 一种计算机设备，适于施行根据权利要求1到16中的一项所述的方法。

solution_context: 引入了用于使用对抗自编码器（12,14,16）检测第一图像数据集当中的异常图像的方法。 A method for detecting abnormal images in a first image dataset using an adversarial autoencoder (12, 14, 16) is introduced. 一种用于使用对抗自编码器来检测第一图像数据集当中的异常图像的方法，其特征在于： 在第一训练中利用图像训练数据集(11)训练对抗自编码器，而优化对抗自编码器使得图像训练数据集(11)的图像的潜在表示(13)的分布接近预定先验分布(17)，并且最小化图像训练数据集的重构图像(15)的重构误差， 在对对抗自编码器的第一训练之后，在图像训练数据集(11)的图像的潜在表示(13)中检测异常，并且在第二训练中利用图像训练数据集(11)但是把检测到的异常考虑在内来训练对抗自编码器， 由经训练的对抗自编码器取决于图像的重构误差和预定先验分布(17)下的概率密度中的至少一个来检测第一图像数据集当中的异常图像， 其中通过以下方式来把在潜在表示(13)中检测到的异常考虑在内： 从用于第二训练的图像训练数据集(11)中排除检测到的异常，或者在至少一次后续训练中使用用于调谐对抗自编码器的加权损失函数，而在潜在表示 (13)中检测到的异常在所述至少一次后续训练中接收减小的权重，或者对于至少一次后续训练，将在潜在表示(13)中检测到的异常集中每个异常的重构目标修改为其自身的噪声损坏版本，或者在至少一次后续训练中，将在潜在表示(13)中检测到的异常集中每个异常的重构目标修改为潜在空间中标识为没有异常的靠近或最靠近的图像。

llm_reviewer_comment_detail: 課題文脈は欠陥・異常検出の目的を比較的よく表している。解決手段文脈は処理手順やモデル構成を読み取れる。技術手段は具体的なモデル・アルゴリズム名を含む。関連要素はあるものの、製造欠陥検出の代表例としては弱い。

title: ANOMALY DETECTION METHOD, ANOMALY DETECTION PROGRAM, ANOMALY DETECTION DEVICE, SERVER DEVICE, AND INFORMATION PROCESSING METHOD; 异常检测方法、异常检测程序、异常检测装置、服务器装置以及信息处理方法
abstract:
计算机取得胸部X线图像(S50)，在取得的胸部X线图像中，使用模型检测边界线(S100)，模型在检测前使用机器学习构建而成，基于边界线，在取得的胸部X线图像中设定包含与心脏重叠的第1肺区域和与肝脏重叠的第2肺区域中的至少一方的第3肺区域(S200)，提取表示第3肺区域所包含的区域内存在的至少一个肺血管的粗细和密度中的至少一方的血管指标(S500)，基于血管指标和基准指标，判定第3肺区域所包含的区域是否为异常状态，基准指标使用了预先使用提取血管指标的方法从正常状态的胸部X线图像的与第3肺区域所包含的区域相当的区域提取出的指标(S700)，在判定为第3肺区域所包含的区域是异常状态时，输出表示判定的信息。

The computer obtains a chest X-ray image (S50). In the obtained chest X-ray image, a model is used to detect the boundary line (S100). The model is constructed using machine learning before detection. Based on the boundary line, the obtained chest X-ray image Set a third lung area including at least one of the first lung area overlapping the heart and the second lung area overlapping the liver (S200), and extract representing at least one lung present in the area included in the third lung area The blood vessel index of at least one of the thickness and density of the blood vessel (S500). Based on the blood vessel index and the reference index, it is determined whether the area included in the third lung area is abnormal. The index (S700) extracted from the area corresponding to the area included in the third lung area of the chest X-ray image of the state, when it is determined that the area included in the third lung area is an abnormal state, outputs information indicating the determination.

llm_reviewer_comment_short: 画像中の異常検出・分割とは近いが、医療・健康用途が主題で製造欠陥検出とは対象がずれる。
llm_reviewer_comment_detail: 課題文脈は欠陥・異常検出の目的を比較的よく表している。解決手段文脈は処理手順やモデル構成を読み取れる。技術手段は具体的なモデル・アルゴリズム名を含む。関連要素はあるものの、製造欠陥検出の代表例としては弱い。
human_reviewer_comment: 胸部X線画像の肺領域・血管指標に基づく異常検出であり、医療画像診断のため対象が異なる。

title: GRAY AND WHITE MATTER VOLUME ABNORMALITY DETECTION AND CORRECTION METHOD, DEVICE, EQUIPMENT AND STORAGE MEDIUM; 灰白质体积异常检测矫正方法、装置、设备和存储介质
abstract:
本发明提出一种灰白质体积异常检测矫正方法，包括：基于行为学量表，获取测试对象的行为学评分结果，以及对应于所述行为学量表的行为学与脑结构之间的映射模板；将所述行为学评分结果与所述映射模板加权，获得加权模板；对测试对象的磁共振成像数据进行VBM分析，获得脑部灰白质体积异常区域概率模板；将所述加权模板与所述脑部灰白质体积异常区域概率模板比较，提取同时表现为显著异常的体素作为分析结果。本发明还公开了一种灰白质体积异常检测装置、一种/灰白质体积异常检测设备和一种计算机可读存储介质。采用本发明实施例，能够利用脑结构与行为学之间的关系，修正了由于VBM分析中多重比较误差所带来的假阳性。

The present invention proposes a gray and white matter volume abnormality detection and correction method, comprising: based on a behavioral scale, obtaining a behavioral scoring result of a test subject, and a mapping template between behavior and brain structure corresponding to the behavioral scale; weighting the behavioral scoring result and the mapping template to obtain a weighted template; performing VBM analysis on the magnetic resonance imaging data of the test subject to obtain a probability template of gray and white matter volume abnormality areas in the brain; comparing the weighted template with the probability template of gray and white matter volume abnormality areas in the brain, and extracting voxels that simultaneously show significant abnormalities as analysis results. The present invention also discloses a gray and white matter volume abnormality detection device, a gray and white matter volume abnormality detection device, and a computer-readable storage medium. By adopting the embodiments of the present invention, the relationship between brain structure and behavior can be utilized to correct false positives caused by multiple comparison errors in VBM analysis.
claims:
1 . 一种灰白质体积异常检测矫正方法，其特征在于，包括： 基于行为学量表，获取测试对象的行为学评分结果，以及对应于所述行为学量表的行为学与脑结构之间的映射模板； 将所述行为学评分结果与所述映射模板加权，获得加权模板； 对测试对象的磁共振成像数据进行VBM分析，获得脑部灰白质体积异常区域概率模板； 将所述加权模板与所述脑部灰白质体积异常区域概率模板比较，提取同时表现为显著异常的体素作为分析结果，从而修正VBM分析的多重比较误差所带来的假阳性； 其中，在获取所述映射模板之前，通过以下步骤构建所述映射模板，具体包括： 构建脑科学与神经病学的数据库； 利用所述数据库进行meta分析，构建对应于行为学量表的脑结构异常区域概率模板作为映射模板。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其特征在于，所述脑结构异常区域概率模板的每个体素为从行为学量表提取的特征分数与各脑区的相关系数。 3 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其特征在于，所述脑结构异常区域概率模板根据所述数据库及其分析和所述行为学量表的更新而更新。 4 .根据权利要求1所述的方法，其特征在于，所述数据库包括脑科学与神经病学的实验数据、研究结论、文献和指南中的至少一种。 5 . 一种灰白质体积异常检测装置，其特征在于，包括： 输入单元，用于获取测试对象磁共振图像数据，以及行为学评分结果； 存储单元，用于存储对应于各种行为学量表的各种行为学与脑结构之间的映射模板； 数据处理单元，用于对所述磁共振成像数据进行VBM分析，获得脑部灰白质体积异常区域概率模板，并将所述行为学评分结果与所述映射模板加权，获得加权模板，以及将所述加权模板与所述脑部灰白质体积异常区域概率模板比较，提取同时表现为显著异常的体素作为分析结果，从而修正VBM分析的多重比较误差所带来的假阳性； 其中，所述行为学与脑结构之间的映射模板通过对脑科学与神经病学的数据库进行 meta分析得出。 6 .如权利要求5所述的灰白质体积异常检测装置，其特征在于，所述灰白质体积异常检测装置还包括： 检测输出单元，用于将分析得到的脑部灰白质体积异常信号按照体积大小进行排序， 并输出检测报告；其中，所述脑部灰白质体积异常信号的体积由异常区域总体素数乘以图像分辨率计算得出。 7 . 一种灰白质体积异常检测设备，其特征在于，包括处理器、存储器以及存储在所述存储器中且被配置为由所述处理器执行的计算机程序，所述处理器执行所述计算机程序时实现如权利要求1至4中任意一项所述的灰白质体积异常检测矫正方法。 8 .一种计算机可读存储介质，其特征在于，所述计算机可读存储介质包括存储的计算机程序，其中，在所述计算机程序运行时控制所述计算机可读存储介质所在设备执行如权利要求1至4中任意一项所述的灰白质体积异常检测矫正方法。

1 . A method for detecting and correcting abnormal gray and white matter volumes, characterized in that it includes: based on a behavioral scale, obtaining the behavioral scoring results of the test subject, and a mapping template between the behavior and brain structure corresponding to the behavioral scale; weighting the behavioral scoring results and the mapping template to obtain a weighted template; performing VBM analysis on the magnetic resonance imaging data of the test subject to obtain a probability template for abnormal brain gray and white matter volume areas; comparing the weighted template with the probability template for abnormal brain gray and white matter volume areas, and extracting voxels that simultaneously show significant abnormalities as analysis results, thereby correcting false positives caused by multiple comparison errors of VBM analysis; wherein, before obtaining the mapping template, the mapping template is constructed through the following steps, specifically including: constructing a database of brain science and neurology; using the database to perform meta-analysis, and constructing a probability template for abnormal brain structure areas corresponding to the behavioral scale as a mapping template. 2 .The method according to claim 1 is characterized in that each voxel of the probability template of the abnormal brain structure area is the correlation coefficient between the feature score extracted from the behavioral scale and each brain area. 3 .The method according to claim 1 is characterized in that the probability template of the abnormal brain structure area is updated based on the database and its analysis and the update of the behavioral scale. 4 .The method according to claim 1 is characterized in that the database includes at least one of experimental data, research conclusions, literature and guidelines in brain science and neurology. 5 . A gray and white matter volume abnormality detection device, characterized in that it includes: an input unit for acquiring magnetic resonance image data of a test subject, and a behavioral scoring result; a storage unit for storing mapping templates between various behaviors and brain structures corresponding to various behavioral scales; a data processing unit for performing VBM analysis on the magnetic resonance imaging data to obtain a probability template of abnormal brain gray and white matter volume areas, and weighting the behavioral scoring result with the mapping template to obtain a weighted template, and comparing the weighted template with the probability template of abnormal brain gray and white matter volume areas to extract voxels that simultaneously show significant abnormalities as analysis results, thereby correcting false positives caused by multiple comparison errors of VBM analysis; wherein the mapping template between behaviors and brain structures is obtained by performing a meta-analysis on databases of brain science and neurology. 6 .The gray and white matter volume abnormality detection device as described in claim 5 is characterized in that the gray and white matter volume abnormality detection device also includes: a detection output unit, which is used to sort the analyzed brain gray and white matter volume abnormality signals according to volume size and output a detection report; wherein the volume of the brain gray and white matter volume abnormality signal is calculated by multiplying the total prime number of the abnormal area by the image resolution. 7 . A gray matter volume abnormality detection device, characterized in that it includes a processor, a memory, and a computer program stored in the memory and configured to be executed by the processor, and when the processor executes the computer program, the gray matter volume abnormality detection and correction method as described in any one of claims 1 to 4 is implemented. 8 .A computer-readable storage medium, characterized in that the computer-readable storage medium includes a stored computer program, wherein when the computer program is running, the device where the computer-readable storage medium is located is controlled to execute the gray-white matter volume abnormality detection and correction method as described in any one of claims 1 to 4.

llm_reviewer_comment_short: 課題との接点はあるが、技術手段が一般語寄りで具体的な候補としては慎重な確認が必要である。
llm_reviewer_comment_detail: 課題文脈は一部使えるが、目的や問題点の明確さに不足がある。解決手段文脈は処理手順やモデル構成を読み取れる。技術手段は関連するが一般語・抽象語が中心である。画像中の異常・対象領域検出という水準では参考になるが、対象や用途のずれを人手で確認する必要がある。
human_reviewer_comment: 脳の灰白質体積異常検出が主題であり、医療・脳画像解析であって製造欠陥検出とは異なる。

title: ULTRASOUND IMAGE INTERCEPTION METHOD, DEVICE, TERMINAL EQUIPMENT AND STORAGE MEDIUM; 超声影像截取方法、装置、终端设备及存储介质
abstract:
本申请提供一种超声影像截取方法、装置、终端设备及存储介质，该方法包括：获取超声扫描视频；获取超声扫描视频对应的目标截取方式，目标截取方式中包括多个截取节点，每个截取节点对应一个参考描述信息；对超声扫描视频进行影像识别，确定得到超声扫描视频中每个视频帧对应的描述信息；根据描述信息以及目标截取方式中各个截取节点对应的参考描述信息，对超声扫描视频进行截取。本申请提供的超声影像截取方法，通过按照目标截取方式，对超声扫描视频进行截取，去除了超声扫描视频中不需要的视频帧，使得医生在观看截取后的超声扫描视频时，无需再查看不需要的视频帧，减轻了医生的负担。

This application provides an ultrasonic image interception method, device, terminal equipment and storage medium. The method includes: obtaining an ultrasonic scan video; obtaining a target interception method corresponding to the ultrasonic scan video. The target interception method includes multiple interception nodes, each interception The node corresponds to a reference description information; image recognition is performed on the ultrasound scan video to determine the description information corresponding to each video frame in the ultrasound scan video; based on the description information and the reference description information corresponding to each interception node in the target interception method, the ultrasound scan is Video is intercepted. The ultrasound image interception method provided by this application intercepts the ultrasound scan video according to the target interception method, and removes unnecessary video frames in the ultrasound scan video, so that the doctor does not need to view the intercepted ultrasound scan video when watching it. required video frames, reducing the burden on doctors.
claims:
1 . 一种超声影像截取方法，其特征在于，所述方法包括： 获取超声扫描视频； 获取所述超声扫描视频对应的目标截取方式，所述目标截取方式中包括多个截取节点，每个截取节点对应一个参考描述信息； 对所述超声扫描视频进行影像识别，确定得到所述超声扫描视频中每个视频帧对应的描述信息； 根据所述描述信息以及所述目标截取方式中各个截取节点对应的参考描述信息，对所述超声扫描视频进行截取。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的超声影像截取方法，其特征在于，所述获取所述超声扫描视频对应的目标截取方式，包括： 根据预设的异常检测模型，对所述超声扫描视频进行异常检测，得到所述超声扫描视频的异常信息； 根据异常信息，从预设的数据库中得到目标截取方式，所述数据库中存储有每个异常信息对应的截取方式。 3 .根据权利要求1所述的超声影像截取方法，其特征在于，所述对所述超声扫描视频进行影像识别，确定得到所述超声扫描视频中每个视频帧对应的描述信息，包括： 对所述超声扫描视频进行物体影像识别，得到所述超声扫描视频中对应的物体类型； 根据所述物体类型，从预设的数据库中，获取与所述物体类型匹配的目标三维模型； 确定得到所述超声扫描视频中每个视频帧在所述目标三维模型中对应的三维位置信息； 确定所述三维位置信息为所述视频帧的描述信息。 4 .根据权利要求1所述的超声影像截取方法，其特征在于，所述对所述超声扫描视频进行影像识别，确定得到所述超声扫描视频中每个视频帧对应的描述信息，包括： 获取所述超声扫描视频对应的目标扫描路径； 确定所述超声扫描视频中的每个视频帧在所述目标扫描路径上的扫描路径点； 根据所述每个视频帧在所述目标扫描路径上的扫描路径点，得到所述每个视频帧的描述信息。 5 .根据权利要求1所述的超声影像截取方法，其特征在于，所述对所述超声扫描视频进行影像识别，确定得到所述超声扫描视频中每个视频帧对应的描述信息，包括： 对所述超声扫描视频进行逐帧影像识别，得到所述超声扫描视频中各个视频帧的征象信息，所述超声扫描视频中每个视频帧对应的描述信息包括所述征象信息。 6 .根据权利要求1 - 5任一项所述的超声影像截取方法，其特征在于，所述根据所述描述信息以及所述目标截取方式中各个截取节点对应的参考描述信息，对所述超声扫描视频进行截取，包括： 将所述各个截取节点对应的参考描述信息分别与所述超声扫描视频中每个视频帧对应的描述信息进行匹配，得到与所述各个截取节点对应的参考描述信息匹配的视频帧； 将所述匹配的视频帧从所述超声扫描视频中截取，得到目标视频帧。 7 .根据权利要求6所述的超声影像截取方法，其特征在于，所述将所述各个截取节点对应的参考描述信息分别与所述超声扫描视频中每个视频帧对应的描述信息进行匹配，得到 与所述各个截取节点对应的参考描述信息匹配的视频帧,包括： 将各个参考描述信息中的三维位置分别与每个视频帧对应的三维位置信息进行匹配， 得到与所述各个参考描述信息匹配的视频帧。 8 .根据权利要求6所述的超声影像截取方法，其特征在于，所述将所述各个截取节点对应的参考描述信息分别与所述超声扫描视频中每个视频帧对应的描述信息进行匹配，得到与所述各个截取节点对应的参考描述信息匹配的视频帧，包括： 将所述各个参考描述信息中的扫描路径点分别与所述每个视频帧对应的扫描路径点进行匹配，确定得到与所述各个参考描述信息匹配的视频帧。 9 .根据权利要求6所述的超声影像截取方法，其特征在于，所述将所述各个截取节点对应的参考描述信息分别与所述超声扫描视频中每个视频帧对应的描述信息进行匹配，得到与所述各个截取节点对应的参考描述信息匹配的视频帧，包括： 将各个参考描述信息中的参考征象信息分别与所述每个视频帧的征象信息进行匹配， 得到与所述各个参考描述信息匹配的视频帧。 10. 一种超声影像截取装置，其特征在于，所述装置包括： 第一获取模块，用于获取超声扫描视频； 第二获取模块，用于获取所述超声扫描视频对应的目标截取方式，所述目标截取方式中包括多个截取节点，每个截取节点对应一个参考描述信息； 确定模块，用于对所述超声扫描视频进行影像识别，确定得到所述超声扫描视频中每个视频帧对应的描述信息； 截取模块，用于根据所述描述信息以及所述目标截取方式中各个截取节点对应的参考描述信息，对所述超声扫描视频进行截取。 11. 一种终端设备，其特征在于，所述终端设备包括处理器、存储器以及存储于所述存储器中并可在所述处理器上运行的计算机程序，所述处理器执行所述计算机程序以实现权利要求1至9任一项所述的超声影像截取方法中的步骤。 12. 一种计算机可读存储介质，其特征在于，所述计算机可读存储介质上存储有计算机程序，所述计算机程序被处理器执行以实现权利要求1至9任一项所述的超声影像截取方法中的步骤。

1 . An ultrasonic image interception method, characterized in that the method includes: obtaining an ultrasonic scan video; obtaining a target interception method corresponding to the ultrasonic scan video, the target interception method including a plurality of interception nodes, each interception node Corresponding to one reference description information; Perform image recognition on the ultrasonic scanning video to determine the description information corresponding to each video frame in the ultrasonic scanning video; According to the description information and the corresponding interception nodes in the target interception method Refer to the description information to intercept the ultrasound scan video. 2 . The ultrasonic image interception method according to claim 1, wherein obtaining the target interception method corresponding to the ultrasonic scan video includes: performing anomaly detection on the ultrasonic scan video according to a preset anomaly detection model. , obtain the abnormal information of the ultrasonic scanning video; according to the abnormal information, obtain the target interception method from a preset database, and the interception method corresponding to each abnormal information is stored in the database. 3 . The ultrasonic image interception method according to claim 1, wherein the step of performing image recognition on the ultrasonic scanning video to determine the description information corresponding to each video frame in the ultrasonic scanning video includes: Perform object image recognition on the ultrasonic scanning video to obtain the corresponding object type in the ultrasonic scanning video; Obtain the target three-dimensional model matching the object type from the preset database according to the object type; Determine that the object type is obtained The three-dimensional position information corresponding to each video frame in the ultrasonic scanning video in the target three-dimensional model is determined; and the three-dimensional position information is determined to be the description information of the video frame. 4 . The ultrasonic image interception method according to claim 1, wherein the step of performing image recognition on the ultrasonic scanning video to determine the description information corresponding to each video frame in the ultrasonic scanning video includes: obtaining the The target scanning path corresponding to the ultrasonic scanning video; Determine the scanning path point of each video frame in the ultrasonic scanning video on the target scanning path; According to the scanning of each video frame on the target scanning path path point to obtain the description information of each video frame. 5 . The ultrasonic image interception method according to claim 1, wherein the step of performing image recognition on the ultrasonic scanning video to determine the description information corresponding to each video frame in the ultrasonic scanning video includes: The ultrasonic scanning video is subjected to frame-by-frame image recognition to obtain the symptom information of each video frame in the ultrasonic scanning video, and the description information corresponding to each video frame in the ultrasonic scanning video includes the symptom information. 6 . title: AN ELECTRONIC TERAHERTZ CONTINUOUS WAVE EXTENDED PHASE TOMOGRAPHY METHOD; 一种电子学太赫兹连续波扩展相位层析成像方法
abstract:
本发明涉及一种电子学太赫兹连续波扩展相位层析成像方法。该方法包括太赫兹扩展相位层析成像数据采集1和太赫兹扩展相位层析成像图像重建2，由太赫兹扩展相位数据采集装置3采集未扩展相位，并经太赫兹扩展相位提取方法4提取扩展相位，再由扩展相位数据预处理5实现投影偏移修正和高斯平滑降噪，形成基于高斯模型修正的扩展相位数据，基于该数据实现扩展相位图像重建6。该方法对太赫兹波束与样品互作用的相位变化具有高灵敏度，且对投影数据作了去噪和修正处理，可实现高清晰度成像，成像性能优于幅度成像。

The invention relates to an electronic terahertz continuous wave extended phase tomography method. The method includes terahertz extended phase tomography data acquisition 1 and terahertz extended phase tomographic image reconstruction 2, the unexpanded phase is collected by the terahertz extended phase data acquisition device 3, and the extended phase is extracted by the terahertz extended phase extraction method 4 Phase, and then the extended phase data preprocessing 5 realizes projection offset correction and Gaussian smoothing noise reduction, forming extended phase data based on Gaussian model correction, and realizing extended phase image reconstruction 6 based on this data. This method has high sensitivity to the phase change of the interaction between the terahertz beam and the sample, and denoises and corrects the projection data, which can realize high-definition imaging, and the imaging performance is better than that of amplitude imaging.

title: YOLOV8 NEURAL NETWORK IDENTIFICATION METHOD OF SOFT X-RAY IMAGES OF INTERNAL CRACKS IN CORN SEEDS; 玉米种子内部裂纹的软X光影像图YOLOV8神经网络识别方法
abstract:
本发明公开了一种玉米种子内部裂纹的软X光影像图YOLOv8神经网络识别方法，属于图像增强和深度学习领域，可实现对玉米种子X光平面影像图的图像增强，针对实时检测和目标图像中裂纹的快速检测，发明了改进的YOLOv8裂纹检测模型，该模型在速度和检测精度方面达到了很好的平衡，且具有很强的泛化性。

The invention discloses a YOLOv8 neural network recognition method of soft X-ray images of internal cracks in corn seeds, which belongs to the field of image enhancement and deep learning. It can realize image enhancement of X-ray planar images of corn seeds, aiming at real-time detection and target images. For the rapid detection of medium-sized cracks, the improved YOLOv8 crack detection model was invented, which achieves a good balance between speed and detection accuracy, and has strong generalization.
claims:
1 .玉米种子内部裂纹的软X光影像图YOLOv8神经网络识别方法，其特征在于，包括以下步骤： S1、将输入的玉米种子X光平面影像图进行图像增强； S2、对YOLOv8神经网络进行改动； S3、将增强后的玉米种子X光平面影像图进行裂纹识别。 根据权利要求1所述的玉米种子内部裂纹的软X光影像图YOLOv8神经网络识别方法，其特征在于，小波阈值去噪、标准化、双边滤波、拉普拉斯锐化；具体包括以下步骤： STEP1、玉米种子X光平面影像图的小波阈值去噪:选择Daubechies小波基函数，然后使用小波变换库(如PyWavelets)中的函数将玉米种子X光平面影像图进行小波分解，得到不同频率和不同尺度的小波系数，再根据信号的噪声水平和小波系数的统计特性，选择适当的阈值；将小波系数与所选阈值进行比较，将小于阈值的系数置为零，将大于阈值的系数保留；将阈值处理后的小波系数进行反变换，得到去噪后的图像； STEP2、标准化:计算去噪后的图像特征的均值和标准差:对于每一个特征，使用numpy 库中的mean和std函数来实现计算它们的均值和标准差。对每一个特征进行标准化:对于每一个特征，使用numpy库中的subtract和divide函数实现用该特征的值减去该特征的均值， 再除以该特征的标准差，得到该特征的标准化值。将标准化后的特征合并为数据集:通过使用numpy库中的column_stack或者hstack函数实现将标准化后的每一个特征按列合并，得到标准化后的数据集； STEP3、双边滤波:选择滤波器参数:根据标准化后的玉米种子X光平面影像图的细节和噪声水平进行调整。构建空间高斯核:使用选择的空间高斯核标准差，构建一个二维高斯核。构建灰度值高斯核:对于每一个像素，计算它和周围像素的灰度值差异，然后使用选择的灰度值高斯核标准差，构建一个一维高斯核。对每一个像素应用滤波器:对于图像中的每一个像素，将空间高斯核和灰度值高斯核分别应用在它周围的像素上，然后将结果相乘，得到该像素的新值，重复该步骤，直到处理完整个图像； STEP4、拉普拉斯锐化:对图像进行灰度化:将双边滤波后的图像转换为灰度图像。构建拉普拉斯滤波器:使用拉普拉斯算子构建一个二维拉普拉斯滤波器。对灰度图像进行滤波: 使用构建的拉普拉斯滤波器对灰度图像进行滤波，得到增强后的图像。将增强后的图像与原始图像相加:将增强后的图像与原始图像进行加权相加，得到锐化后的图像。 2 .根据权利要求2所述的玉米种子内部裂纹的软X光影像图YOLOv8神经网络识别方法， 其特征在于，在原有的Y010Y8x的基础上，引入基于Transformer构建的COT网络，同时在特征融合网络中加入跨空间学习注意力EMA，引入SIoU函数计算回归损失，使用GSConv降低模型复杂度。 3 .根据权利要求3所述的玉米种子内部裂纹的软X光影像图YOLOv8神经网络识别方法， 其特征在于，将改进的YOLOv8模型使用大量增强后的玉米种子X光平面影像图进行训练，然后得到训练好的.pth文件格式的模型，将其部署到计算机上，应用于增强后的玉米种子X光平面影像图的裂纹检测。

1 .The YOLOv8 neural network identification method for soft X-ray images of internal cracks in corn seeds is characterized by including the following steps: S1. Enhance the input X-ray planar image of corn seeds; S2. Make changes to the YOLOv8 neural network; S3. Use the enhanced X-ray planar image of corn seeds for crack identification. The YOLOv8 neural network identification method of soft X-ray images of internal cracks in corn seeds according to claim 1, characterized by wavelet threshold denoising, standardization, bilateral filtering, and Laplacian sharpening; specifically including the following steps: STEP1. Wavelet threshold denoising of corn seed X-ray planar image: Select the Daubechies wavelet basis function, and then use the functions in the wavelet transform library (such as PyWavelets) to perform wavelet decomposition on the corn seed X-ray planar image to obtain different frequencies and different Scale wavelet coefficients, and then select an appropriate threshold based on the noise level of the signal and the statistical characteristics of the wavelet coefficients; compare the wavelet coefficients with the selected threshold, set the coefficients smaller than the threshold to zero, and retain the coefficients larger than the threshold; The wavelet coefficients after threshold processing are inversely transformed to obtain the denoised image; STEP2. Standardization: Calculate the mean and standard deviation of the denoised image features: For each feature, use the mean and std functions in the numpy library to calculate their mean and standard deviation. Standardize each feature: For each feature, use the subtract and divide functions in the numpy library to subtract the mean of the feature from the value of the feature, and then divide by the standard deviation of the feature to obtain the standardized value of the feature. Merge the standardized features into a data set: Use the column_stack or hstack function in the numpy library to merge each standardized feature by column to obtain a standardized data set; STEP3. Bilateral filtering: Select filter parameters: adjust according to the details and noise level of the standardized corn seed X-ray plane image. Construct a spatial Gaussian kernel: Use the selected spatial Gaussian kernel standard deviation to construct a two-dimensional Gaussian kernel. Construct a gray value Gaussian kernel: For each pixel, calculate the gray value difference between it and surrounding pixels, and then use the selected gray value Gaussian kernel standard deviation to construct a one-dimensional Gaussian kernel. Apply the filter to each pixel: For each pixel in the image, apply the spatial Gaussian kernel and the gray value Gaussian kernel to the pixels around it respectively, and then multiply the results to get the new value of the pixel, repeat the process steps until the entire image is processed; STEP4, Laplacian sharpening: Grayscale the image: Convert the bilaterally filtered image into a grayscale image. Constructing a Laplacian filter: Construct a two-dimensional Laplacian filter using the Laplacian operator. Filter the grayscale image: Use the constructed Laplacian filter to filter the grayscale image to obtain an enhanced image. Add the enhanced image to the original image: Perform a weighted addition of the enhanced image and the original image to obtain a sharpened image. 2 . The YOLOv8 neural network identification method of soft X-ray images of internal cracks in corn seeds according to claim 2, characterized in that, on the basis of the original Y010Y8x, a COT network built based on Transformer is introduced, and at the same time, in the feature fusion network Add cross-space learning attention EMA, introduce SIoU function to calculate regression loss, and use GSConv to reduce model complexity. 3 . The YOLOv8 neural network identification method of soft X-ray images of internal cracks in corn seeds according to claim 3, characterized in that the improved YOLOv8 model is trained using a large number of enhanced X-ray planar images of corn seeds, and then obtained The trained model in .pth file format is deployed to the computer and applied to crack detection in the enhanced X-ray planar image of corn seeds.

title: AN INTELLIGENT METHOD FOR REMOVING ARTIFACTS FROM CARDIAC ULTRASOUND IMAGES; 一种心脏超声影像伪影智能去除方法
abstract:
本发明涉及图像数据处理技术领域，具体涉及一种心脏超声影像伪影智能去除方法，该方法根据心脏超声影像检测出心脏区域图像的边缘像素点后，根据边缘像素点的位置分布特征得到预识别心脏区域，根据边缘像素点的边缘灰度值分布特征得到边缘灰度值连通域，通过预识别心脏区域和边缘灰度值连通域进行对比，得到对应的边缘灰度值的近似特征值，进一步结合边缘灰度值对应边缘像素点的数量，得到每种边缘灰度值对应的边缘像素点置信度，根据边缘像素点置信度得到伪影区域识别结果并进行心脏超声影像伪影的智能去除。本发明对心脏区域的识别结果准确度更高，去除伪影的效果更好。

The present invention relates to the technical field of image data processing, and specifically relates to a method for intelligent removal of cardiac ultrasound image artifacts. After detecting edge pixels of a heart region image based on cardiac ultrasound images, the method obtains pre-identification based on the location distribution characteristics of the edge pixels. For the heart region, the edge gray value connected domain is obtained based on the edge gray value distribution characteristics of the edge pixel points. By comparing the pre-identified heart region and the edge gray value connected domain, the approximate feature value of the corresponding edge gray value is obtained. Further Combined with the number of edge pixels corresponding to the edge gray value, the edge pixel confidence corresponding to each edge gray value is obtained. Based on the edge pixel confidence, the artifact area identification result is obtained and the cardiac ultrasound image artifact is intelligently removed. The present invention has higher accuracy in identifying the heart area and better effect in removing artifacts.
claims:
1 . 一种心脏超声影像伪影智能去除方法，其特征在于，所述方法包括： 获取心脏区域图像； 在所述心脏区域图像中通过边缘检测筛选出边缘像素点，根据边缘像素点的位置分布特征得到预识别心脏区域；将边缘像素点对应的灰度值作为边缘灰度值，将每种边缘灰度值作为阈值进行二值化分割，得到每种边缘灰度值对应的边缘灰度值连通域；根据每种边缘灰度值连通域与所述预识别心脏区域之间的形态位置差异，得到每种边缘灰度值连通域对应的形态位置差异特征； 根据预识别心脏区域和边缘灰度值连通域之间边界上像素点的位置分布特征相似性， 得到预识别心脏区域和每种边缘灰度值连通域之间的距离分布特征相似度;根据每种边缘灰度值连通域对应的形态位置差异特征和距离分布特征相似度，得到每种边缘灰度值对应的近似特征值；根据每种边缘灰度值对应的边缘像素点的数量和所述近似特征值，得到每种边缘灰度值对应的边缘像素点置信度； 根据所述边缘像素点置信度和预识别心脏区域边界上像素点的位置分布特征得到伪影区域识别结果，根据所述伪影区域识别结果进行心脏超声影像伪影的智能去除。 2 .根据权利要求1所述的一种心脏超声影像伪影智能去除方法，其特征在于，所述预识别心脏区域的获取方法包括： 统计心脏区域图像中所有边缘像素点，将边缘像素点所围成的最大的区域作为预识别心脏区域。 3 .根据权利要求1所述的一种心脏超声影像伪影智能去除方法，其特征在于，所述边缘灰度值连通域的获取方法包括： 任选一种边缘灰度值作为目标边缘灰度值，在所述心脏区域图像中以所述目标边缘灰度值为阈值进行二值化分割，利用空洞填充算法将二值化分割结果中存在空洞的连通域进行填充，将填充后的区域对应的最大连通域作为边缘灰度值连通域。 4 .根据权利要求1所述的一种心脏超声影像伪影智能去除方法，其特征在于，所述形态位置差异特征的获取方法包括： 获取预识别心脏区域和每种边缘灰度值连通域的质心，根据预识别心脏区域对应质心与每种边缘灰度值连通域对应质心之间的距离，得到每种边缘灰度值连通域对应的质心距离，将所述质心距离作为对应的形态位置差异特征。 5 .根据权利要求1所述的一种心脏超声影像伪影智能去除方法，其特征在于，所述距离分布特征相似度的获取方法包括： 将预识别心脏区域边界上的像素点到对应质心的距离作为心脏边界距离，将所有心脏边界距离按预设排列规则进行排列，得到预识别心脏区域对应的心脏边界距离序列； 将边缘灰度值连通域边界上每个像素点到对应质心的距离作为连通域边界距离，将连通域边界距离按预设排列规则进行排列，得到每种边缘灰度值连通域对应的连通域边界距离序列； 通过DTW£法计算心脏边界距离序列与连通域边界距离序列之间的序列相似度，并将所述序列相似度作为预识别心脏区域和每种边缘灰度值连通域之间的距离分布特征相似度。 6 .根据权利要求1所述的一种心脏超声影像伪影智能去除方法，其特征在于，所述近似 特征值的获取方法包括： 根据所述形态位置差异特征和所述距离分布特征相似度得到对应的近似特征值,所述近似特征值与所述形态位置差异特征呈负相关，所述近似特征值与所述距离分布特征相似度呈正相关。 7 .根据权利要求1所述的一种心脏超声影像伪影智能去除方法，其特征在于，所述边缘像素点置信度的获取方法包括： 计算每种边缘灰度值对应的边缘像素点的数量与边缘像素点总数的比值，得到每种边缘灰度值对应的数量占比，将所述数量占比与所述近似特征值的乘积，作为对应的边缘像素点置信度。 8 .根据权利要求5所述的一种心脏超声影像伪影智能去除方法，其特征在于，所述伪影区域识别结果的获取方法包括： 将预识别心脏区域边界上的像素点作为边界像素点，将所述边界像素点对应的边缘像素点置信度作为边界像素点置信度，将所有边界像素点置信度按预设排列规则进行排列， 得到预识别心脏区域对应的边界像素点置信度序列；根据所述心脏边界距离序列和所述边界像素点置信度序列，计算两两相邻边界像素点的心脏边界距离之间的第一欧氏距离和边界像素点置信度之间的第二欧氏距离，将第一欧氏距离和第二欧氏距离的和值开方，得到两两相邻边界像素点之间的差异特征值，将所有差异特征值按预设排列规则进行排列得到差异序列； 对所述差异序列通过峰值点检测算法检测出至少两个峰值，将最大的两个峰值作为参考峰值，对参考峰值通过异常值检测方法进行异常检测，当两个参考峰值均为异常值时，将所述差异序列中两个参考峰值对应的差异特征值之间的所有差异特征值作为异常差异特征值，将所有异常差异特征值对应的边界像素点组成的区域作为伪影区域;当两个参考峰值不都为异常值时，认为所述心脏区域图像中不存在伪影区域。 9 .根据权利要求5或8所述的一种心脏超声影像伪影智能去除方法，其特征在于，所述预设排列规则包括： 任选一个预识别心脏区域或边缘灰度值连通域作为目标区域，将所述目标区域映射到预设二维坐标系中，在目标区域边界上的像素点中，选择与目标区域质心横坐标相同且纵坐标最大的像素点作为起始点，在顺时针方向上以起始点的前一个像素点作为终点，以顺时针方向作为顺序进行排列得到预设排列规则。

An intelligent method for removing cardiac ultrasound image artifacts, characterized in that the method includes: acquiring a heart region image; filtering out edge pixels through edge detection in the heart region image, and based on the location distribution characteristics of the edge pixels Obtain the pre-identified heart region; use the gray value corresponding to the edge pixel point as the edge gray value, use each edge gray value as a threshold to perform binary segmentation, and obtain the connected edge gray values corresponding to each edge gray value. Domain; According to the morphological position difference between each edge gray value connected domain and the pre-identified heart region, the morphological position difference characteristics corresponding to each edge gray value connected domain are obtained; According to the pre-identified heart region and edge gray The location distribution feature similarity of pixels on the boundary between value connected domains is used to obtain the distance distribution feature similarity between the pre-identified heart area and each edge gray value connected domain; according to the corresponding edge gray value connected domain Based on the morphological position difference feature and distance distribution feature similarity, the approximate feature value corresponding to each edge gray value is obtained; according to the number of edge pixels corresponding to each edge gray value and the approximate feature value, each edge gray value is obtained The edge pixel confidence corresponding to the degree value; Obtain the artifact area recognition result based on the edge pixel confidence and the position distribution characteristics of the pixels on the pre-identified heart area boundary, and perform cardiac ultrasound imaging based on the artifact area identification result Intelligent removal of artifacts. 2 . A method for intelligent removal of cardiac ultrasound image artifacts according to claim 1, characterized in that the acquisition method of pre-identifying the heart region includes: counting all edge pixels in the heart region image, and enclosing the edge pixels The largest area is used as the pre-identified heart area. 3 . An intelligent method for removing cardiac ultrasound image artifacts according to claim 1, characterized in that the method for obtaining the connected domain of edge gray values includes: selecting any edge gray value as the target edge gray value , use the target edge gray value as the threshold to perform binary segmentation in the heart region image, use the hole filling algorithm to fill the connected domains with holes in the binary segmentation result, and fill the corresponding areas of the filled areas. The maximum connected domain is used as the edge gray value connected domain. 4 . An intelligent method for removing cardiac ultrasound image artifacts according to claim 1, characterized in that the method for obtaining the morphological position difference features includes: obtaining the centroid of the pre-identified heart region and each edge gray value connected domain , based on the distance between the centroid corresponding to the pre-identified heart region and the centroid corresponding to the connected domain of each edge gray value, the centroid distance corresponding to the connected domain of each edge gray value is obtained, and the centroid distance is used as the corresponding morphological position difference feature . 5 . A method for intelligent removal of cardiac ultrasound image artifacts according to claim 1, characterized in that the method for obtaining the distance distribution feature similarity includes: calculating the distance from the pixel point on the boundary of the pre-identified heart region to the corresponding centroid As the heart boundary distance, all heart boundary distances are arranged according to the preset arrangement rules to obtain the heart boundary distance sequence corresponding to the pre-identified heart area; the distance from each pixel point on the edge gray value connected domain boundary to the corresponding centroid is used as the connected Domain boundary distance, arrange the connected domain boundary distances according to the preset arrangement rules, and obtain the connected domain boundary distance sequence corresponding to the connected domain of each edge gray value; calculate the difference between the heart boundary distance sequence and the connected domain boundary distance sequence using the DTW£ method The sequence similarity between the two regions is used as the distance distribution feature similarity between the pre-identified heart region and each type of edge gray value connected domain. 6 . A method for intelligent removal of cardiac ultrasound image artifacts according to claim 1, characterized in that the approximate The method for obtaining the feature value includes: obtaining a corresponding approximate feature value based on the similarity of the morphological position difference feature and the distance distribution feature. The approximate feature value is negatively correlated with the morphological position difference feature. The approximate feature value It is positively correlated with the distance distribution feature similarity. 7 . An intelligent method for removing cardiac ultrasound image artifacts according to claim 1, characterized in that the method for obtaining the edge pixel confidence includes: calculating the number of edge pixels corresponding to each edge gray value and The ratio of the total number of edge pixels is used to obtain the quantity proportion corresponding to each edge gray value, and the product of the quantity ratio and the approximate feature value is used as the corresponding edge pixel point confidence. 8 . An intelligent method for removing cardiac ultrasound image artifacts according to claim 5, characterized in that the method for obtaining the artifact area recognition result includes: using pixels on the boundary of the pre-identified heart area as boundary pixels, The confidence of the edge pixels corresponding to the boundary pixels is used as the confidence of the boundary pixels, and the confidences of all the boundary pixels are arranged according to the preset arrangement rules to obtain the confidence sequence of the boundary pixels corresponding to the pre-identified heart area; according to The heart boundary distance sequence and the boundary pixel point confidence sequence calculate the first Euclidean distance between the heart boundary distances of two adjacent boundary pixel points and the second Euclidean distance between the boundary pixel point confidence levels. , take the square root of the sum of the first Euclidean distance and the second Euclidean distance to obtain the difference feature values between two adjacent boundary pixels, and arrange all the difference feature values according to the preset arrangement rules to obtain a difference sequence; At least two peaks are detected for the difference sequence through the peak point detection algorithm, the two largest peaks are used as reference peaks, and the reference peaks are abnormally detected through the outlier detection method. When both reference peaks are outliers, All difference feature values between the difference feature values corresponding to the two reference peaks in the difference sequence are regarded as abnormal difference feature values, and the area composed of boundary pixels corresponding to all abnormal difference feature values is used as the artifact area; when two When all reference peaks are not abnormal values, it is considered that there is no artifact area in the heart region image. 9 . An intelligent method for removing cardiac ultrasound image artifacts according to claim 5 or 8, characterized in that the preset arrangement rules include: selecting any pre-identified heart region or edge gray value connected region as the target region , map the target area to the preset two-dimensional coordinate system. Among the pixels on the boundary of the target area, select the pixel with the same abscissa and the largest ordinate as the centroid of the target area as the starting point, in the clockwise direction. Taking the pixel before the starting point as the end point and arranging in clockwise order to obtain the preset arrangement rules.

title: AI-BASED FETAL CONGENITAL HEART DISEASE ASSESSMENT AND DETECTION METHOD, SYSTEM AND MEDIUM; 基于AI的胎儿先天性心脏病评估检测方法、系统和介质
abstract:
本申请实施例提供了基于AI的胎儿先天性心脏病评估检测方法、系统和介质。该方法包括：根据被检测胎儿的家族心脏病史和胚胎检验报告的信息数据获取对应因子，根据胎儿时段的检测阶段的超声切面检测图像识别获得图像信息，再与标准图像信息处理获得对比清晰度数据，并筛出可信检测图像和提取检测特征数据，再与同期的标准特征数据处理获得检测评估数据并聚合获得阶段和时段的心脏发育健康评估数据，再将阶段的异常分指数中超阈值对比的超标频次数结合时段发育健康异常度指数处理获得时段发育异常风险评估数据，并判断胎儿的时段发育情况；从而通过图像识别和数据处理的AI技术获得对胎儿心脏发育时段内发育健康的异常风险评估的检测技术。

The embodiment of the present application provides a fetal congenital heart disease assessment and detection method, system and medium based on AI. The method includes: obtaining corresponding factors according to the family heart disease history of the detected fetus and the information data of the embryo test report, obtaining image information according to the ultrasonic section detection image recognition of the detection stage of the fetal period, and then processing with the standard image information to obtain contrast clarity data, and screening out reliable detection images and extracting detection feature data, and then processing with the standard feature data of the same period to obtain detection evaluation data and aggregate to obtain stage and period heart development health assessment data, and then combining the number of over-threshold comparisons in the abnormal sub-index of the stage with the period development health abnormality index to obtain period development abnormality risk assessment data, and judge the period development of the fetus; thereby obtaining a detection technology for abnormal risk assessment of fetal heart development health in the period through image recognition and data processing AI technology.

1 .An AI-based fetal congenital heart disease assessment and detection method, characterized in that it includes the following steps: obtaining family heart disease history information and embryo test report information of the detected fetus, and extracting family heart disease history parameter data and embryo test parameter data respectively, and then performing data processing according to a preset family abnormal disease detection model and a preset embryo abnormality detection model respectively, to obtain a congenital heart disease induction factor and an embryo abnormality compensation factor; obtaining multiple groups of ultrasonic section detection images of the detected fetus for cardiac ultrasonic imaging detection at multiple detection stages within a preset development detection period, and performing information recognition processing on the ultrasonic section detection images according to a preset ultrasonic image information recognition model to obtain corresponding ultrasonic section detection image information, and obtaining preset ultrasonic section detection standard image information corresponding to the ultrasonic section category according to a preset heart development monitoring information database; processing the ultrasonic section detection image information and the preset ultrasonic section detection standard image information to obtain image recognition comparison clarity data of the ultrasonic section detection image, and then performing threshold comparison with the preset image recognition comparison clarity standard threshold corresponding to the ultrasonic section category, and screening the ultrasonic section detection images that meet the threshold comparison requirements and marking them as credible ultrasonic section detection images; problem_context: 本申请实施例提供了基于AI的胎儿先天性心脏病评估检测方法、系统和介质。
solution_context: 本申请实施例提供了基于AI的胎儿先天性心脏病评估检测方法、系统和介质。 该方法包括：根据被检测胎儿的家族心脏病史和胚胎检验报告的信息数据获取对应因子，根据胎儿时段的检测阶段的超声切面检测图像识别获得图像信息，再与标准图像信息处理获得对比清晰度数据，并筛出可信检测图像和提取检测特征数据，再与同期的标准特征数据处理获得检测评估数据并聚合获得阶段和时段的心脏发育健康评估数据，再将阶段的异常分指数中超阈值对比的超标频次数结合时段发育健康异常度指数处理获得时段发育异常风险评估数据，并判断胎儿的时段发育情况；从而通过图像识别和数据处理的AI技术获得对胎儿心脏发育时段内发育健康的异常风险评估的检测技术。 1 .基于AI的胎儿先天性心脏病评估检测方法，其特征在于，包括以下步骤： 获取被检测胎儿的家族心脏病史信息和胚胎检验报告信息，并分别提取家族心脏病史参数数据以及胚胎检验参数数据，再分别根据预设家族异常病检测模型以及预设胚胎异常检测模型分别进行数据处理，获得先天心脏病患诱导因子以及胚胎异常补偿因子； 获取所述被检测胎儿在预设发育检测时段内的多个检测阶段进行心脏超声影像检测的多组超声切面检测图像，并根据预设超声影像信息识别模型对超声切面检测图像进行信息识别处理，获得对应的超声切面检测图像信息，根据预设心脏发育监测信息数据库获取与超声切面类别对应的预设超声切面检测标准图像信息； 根据所述超声切面检测图像信息与所述预设超声切面检测标准图像信息进行处理，获得超声切面检测图像的图像识别对比清晰度数据，再与超声切面类别对应的预设图像识别对比清晰度标准阈值进行阈值对比，将符合阈值对比要求的超声切面检测图像进行筛选并标记为可信超声切面检测图像； 根据各所述可信超声切面检测图像的图像特征拾取信息提取对应的超声切面检测特征数据，根据所述预设心脏发育监测信息数据库获取与所述被检测胎儿在同发育时期阶段的超声切面标准特征数据，并与超声切面检测特征数据进行处理，获得超声切面检测评估数据； 对同一检测阶段的多个所述可信超声切面检测图像的对应多个超声切面检测评估数据进行处理获得阶段发育健康评估数据，并对所述预设发育检测时段内的多个所述阶段发育健康评估数据进行聚合处理获得时段发育健康评估数据； 根据各检测阶段的所述阶段发育健康评估数据进行阈值对比获得发育健康异常度分指数以及超阈值的异常度超标频次数，再结合根据时段发育健康评估数据阈值对比获得的时段发育健康异常度指数以及所述先天心脏病患诱导因子和胚胎异常补偿因子进行处理， 获得时段发育异常风险评估数据，并判断被检测胎儿在预设发育检测时段内的发育情况。

title: UNSUPERVISED NORMATIVE LEARNING METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR DIFFUSION MAGNETIC RESONANCE IMAGING QUALITY ASSESSMENT; 用于弥散磁共振影像质量评估的无监督规范学习方法及系统
abstract:
本发明设计了一种用于弥散磁共振影像质量评估的无监督规范学习方法及系统，首先通过自然对数方法对多b值的图像进行规范化统一，其中b为弥散敏感梯度参数；然后使用向量量化变分自动编码器(VQ‑VAE)在标准(高质量无伪影)数据集上进行训练得到模型；接着，将含有伪影的图像和无伪影图像输入前面训练好的模型，比较重建前后的结构相似度(SSIM)指标，最后进行数据的质量评估，判断是否属于标准数据，并给出检测样本属于伪影数据和标准数据的概率以便辅助专家的判断。本发明将无监督生成模型、异常检测方法应用到弥散磁共振伪影的识别上，实现了训练数据的无标签化，解决了训练数据缺乏大量带标注伪影的弊端。

The present invention designs an unsupervised normative learning method and system for diffusion magnetic resonance image quality assessment. First, the images with multiple b values are normalized and unified by the natural logarithm method, where b is the diffusion sensitive gradient parameter; then the vector quantized variational autoencoder (VQVAE) is used to train the model on the standard (high-quality artifact-free) data set; then, the image containing artifacts and the image without artifacts are input into the previously trained model, and the structural similarity (SSIM) index before and after reconstruction is compared. Finally, the data quality is evaluated to determine whether it belongs to standard data, and the probability that the detected sample belongs to artifact data and standard data is given to assist the expert's judgment. The present invention applies the unsupervised generative model and the anomaly detection method to the identification of diffusion magnetic resonance artifacts, realizes the unlabeling of training data, and solves the problem that the training data lacks a large number of labeled artifacts.
